title: [H7] #343: [Bug]: HAL_CRYP_Decrypt_DMA fails when transfer size is > 65535 bytes
content:
Issue #343: [Bug]: HAL_CRYP_Decrypt_DMA fails when transfer size is > 65535 bytes

Title: [Bug]: HAL_CRYP_Decrypt_DMA fails when transfer size is > 65535 bytes

Body:
### Bug Summary

HAL_CRYP_Decrypt_DMA fails when transfer size is > 65535 bytes

### Detailed Description

With CRYP set up with word-sized transfers (CRYP_DATAWIDTHUNIT_WORD), it should be able to process 65535 words, or up to 262140 bytes. Internal to HAL, the Size field is set to the number of bytes in the operation, not the number of words, which causes an overflow in the uint16_t value.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx-hal-driver/blob/a1996eed9172b59887bafaaa0ea1816ea14d48b5/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_cryp.c#L1587

    /*  Calculate Size parameter in Byte*/
    if (hcryp->Init.DataWidthUnit == CRYP_DATAWIDTHUNIT_WORD)
    {
      hcryp->Size = Size * 4U;
    }
    else
    {
      hcryp->Size = Size;
    }


The comments say that Size is the number of bytes or the number of words, depending on DataWidthUnit. However, the code sets it to the number of bytes regardless of the DataWidthUnit setting.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx-hal-driver/blob/a1996eed9172b59887bafaaa0ea1816ea14d48b5/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_cryp.h#L111

  uint16_t                          Size;           /*!< length of input data in word or in byte, according to DataWidthUnit */


It looks like many of the DMA functions are passed Size / 4.

The same issue is present within HAL_CRYP_Encrypt_DMA and possibly others.

Community post:
https://community.st.com/t5/stm32-mcus-security/unexpected-behavior-of-the-stm32-cryp-module/m-p/891638#M9633

### Expected Behavior

HAL_CRYP_Decrypt_DMA functions correctly for operations on <= 65535 words when set to CRYP_DATAWIDTHUNIT_WORD

### Actual Behavior

The transfer size is truncated due to the number of bytes being stored as a uint16_t

### Environment

_No response_

### Severity

Normal

Comments:
[MKISTM - 2026-04-14T10:51:10Z]
Hello @timkostka,

Thank you for your report, it seems that point makes sense and will be tracked internally.
I'll keep you informed about last update and our decision about this point.

BR,
Mohamed Chaker.

[MKISTM - 2026-04-14T10:51:41Z]
ST Internal Reference: CDM0061687

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx-hal-driver/blob/a1996eed9172b59887bafaaa0ea1816ea14d48b5/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_cryp.c#L1587

title: [H7] #339: [Bug]: USART with a single line
content:
Issue #339: [Bug]: USART with a single line

Title: [Bug]: USART with a single line

Body:
### Bug Summary

In CubeMx, the assignment of pins when trying to use a UART with a single data line is not OK

### Detailed Description

When setting a USART to Asynchronous and then setting Advanced Parameters->Data direction to Receive only or Transmit only, both pins stay selected and there's no way of unassigning one. For instance, if I set it to Receive only, I would expect the TX pin to be unassigned.

### Expected Behavior

If I set it to Receive only, I would expect the TX pin to be unassigned.

### Actual Behavior

both pins stay assigned and cannot be unassigned

### Environment

CubeMx 6.16.1

### Severity

None

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2026-03-26T16:17:01Z]
ST Internal Reference: CDM0061073
paragraphs: Issue #339: [Bug]: USART with a single line

If I set it to Receive only, I would expect the TX pin to be unassigned.

title: [H7] #338: [Bug]: CubeMx allows an invalid combination of parameters for ADC with DMA
content:
Issue #338: [Bug]: CubeMx allows an invalid combination of parameters for ADC with DMA

Title: [Bug]: CubeMx allows an invalid combination of parameters for ADC with DMA

Body:
### Bug Summary

CubeMx allows an invalid combination of parameters for ADC with DMA

### Detailed Description

When DMA is enabled on an ADC, DMA requests will only be issued if ADC_InitTypeDef::ConversionDataManagement is set to ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_ONESHOT or ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_CIRCULAR. That is to say, ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DR and ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DFSDM are invalid when DMA is enabled.

The issue is that if Conversion Data Management Mode is not set to ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_CIRCULAR or ~~ADC CONVERSIONDATA DR~~ ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_ONESHOT, then CubeMx will generate some unusable code.

CubeMx could detect and flag this issue for the user. Or it could disable non-DMA values when DMA is enabled. Or it could make it more obvious to the user some other way that there is some DMA-relevant (and, indeed, crucial) setting in that otherwise irrelevant-looking "Conversion Data Management Mode".

### Expected Behavior

_No response_

### Actual Behavior

_No response_

### Environment

CubeMx 6.16.1, STM32H743VITx, ADC1

### Severity

None

Comments:
[MKISTM - 2026-03-09T10:34:59Z]
Hello @giuliomoro,

Thank you for your report, I think there is a small typo in your sentence

> The issue is that if Conversion Data Management Mode is not set to ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_CIRCULAR or ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DR, then CubeMx will generate some unusable code.

You mean ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DMA_ONESHOT instead ADC_CONVERSIONDATA_DR ?

BR,
Mohamed Chaker.

[giuliomoro - 2026-03-09T13:03:36Z]
yes correct, I now amended the original post for clarity.

[MKISTM - 2026-03-19T14:00:07Z]
Hello @giuliomoro,

Thank you for your report, this point has been tracked internally.
I'll keep you informed.

BR,
Mohamed Chaker.

[MKISTM - 2026-03-19T14:00:34Z]
ST Internal Reference: CDM0060711

title: [H7] #337: IDE bug: Adding new compile time flags under all configurations
content:
Issue #337: IDE bug: Adding new compile time flags under all configurations

Title: IDE bug: Adding new compile time flags under all configurations

Body:
Hi,

I was unable to find a place to report the STM32CubeIDE related issue. As this issue also impacts projects compiled for STM32H7xx, hence I request help with reporting and with fixing of the issue.

Before I started adding few more compile time flags to the MCU GCC Compiler -> Miscellaneous -> other flags, the flag -fno-omit-frame-pointer was only available with _Debug configuration_. When I selected _All configurations_ and then added few more flags, the -fno-omit-frame-pointer flag automatically got added to _Release configuration_ as well.

To me this seems to be a bug.

Please help with resolving this bug.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2026-02-16T09:31:14Z]
Hello @rxa1031,

For issues not directly related to the software published within this repository, please report to the [ST Community](https://community.st.com/t5/stm32-mcus-software-development/ct-p/mcu-software-development-tools), as suggested in the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#3-support-requests-and-questions) guide.

Please allow me to close this thread. Thank you for your understanding.

With regards,

C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_uart.c:1147:21: warning: cast increases required alignment of target type [-Wcast-align]

Use pdata16bits = (uint16_t *)__builtin_assume_aligned(pData, 2); or similar in place of pdata16bits = (const uint16_t *) pData;

Rajeev

title: [H7] #334: STM32H743i FMC NOR Example, wrong pin definition
content:
Issue #334: STM32H743i FMC NOR Example, wrong pin definition

Title: STM32H743i FMC NOR Example, wrong pin definition

Body:
According to the schematics
Actual busy pin is PD6 and not PC6 as defined here ->
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e109a24f043ec5e6d446c7a8ef31aa57029c7168/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Examples/FMC/FMC_NOR/Inc/main.h#L43C1-L43C36
Actual code always reached timeout

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2026-02-11T13:14:36Z]
Hello @omerwax,

Thank you for the report.

Regarding the issue and based on the Datasheet [DS12110_Rev11](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h743xi.pdf), the Pin **PC6** and **PD6** both of them can be used as NOR_READY_BUSY_PIN via their corresponding alternate functions.

In addition, I have run the example located under Projects\STM32H743I-EVAL\Examples\FMC\FMC_NOR, and it is working correctly on my side.

Unless you have further questions or observations, I will proceed to close this thread.
Thank you for your understanding

With regards,

title: [H7] #333: Fix compile time warnings without removing the set compiler flags.
content:
Issue #333: Fix compile time warnings without removing the set compiler flags.

Title: Fix compile time warnings without removing the set compiler flags.

Body:
Hi,

When using GCC compiler flags like -Wsign-conversion , -Wpedantic , -Wsign-conversion , -Wswitch-enum , -Wconversion , -Wunused-parameter , -Wunused-parameter etc are thrown by STM32CubeIDE on code compilation. I have generated the code using STM32CubeMX.

Observed errors . warnings:

Search "[-W" (149 hits in 1 file of 1 searched) [Extended]
  new 118 (149 hits)
	Line  17: C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/CMSIS/Include/core_cm7.h:2132:72: warning: conversion to 'int' from 'uint32_t' {aka 'long unsigned int'} may change the sign of the result [-Wsign-conversion]
	Line  20: C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_uart_ex.c:1023:32: warning: conversion from 'int' to 'uint16_t' {aka 'short unsigned int'} may change value [-Wconversion]

paragraphs: Issue #333: Fix compile time warnings without removing the set compiler flags.
Title: Fix compile time warnings without removing the set compiler flags.

title: [H7] #332: Keil compiler AC6 throwing error: 'sys/time.h' file not found
content:
Issue #332: Keil compiler AC6 throwing error: 'sys/time.h' file not found

Title: Keil compiler AC6 throwing error: 'sys/time.h' file not found

Body:
Hi,

I am observing an issue related with inclusion of time.h when generating project for NUCLEO-H753ZI with help of STM32CubeMX.

attached ZIP file contains project and IOC file

[lwIP_Ethernet_WORKS_03.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25109447/lwIP_Ethernet_WORKS_03.zip)

I initially thought it to be an issue with LWIP related code, but later figured out that the problem reported in the file
STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1\Middlewares\Third_Party\LwIP\system\arch\cc.h is because of below conditional compilation which should work for Keil AC5 but will not work for Keil AC6


#if defined (__GNUC__) & !defined (__CC_ARM)

#define LWIP_TIMEVAL_PRIVATE 0
#include <sys/time.h>

#endif


and possibly need to be updated to:


#if defined (__GNUC__) & !( defined(__CC_ARM) /* Arm Compiler 5 */ || ( defined(__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010000) /* Arm Compiler 6 (armclang) */ ) )

#define LWIP_TIMEVAL_PRIVATE 0
#include <sys/time.h>

#endif


May you please help resolve the concern. The above fix may cause other issues to pop up, which shall also need fix within ethernetif.c

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[ASAHSTM - 2026-02-11T10:49:58Z]
Hello @rxa1031,

Thank you for your report.
This issue is already being tracked internally and will be fixed in our next STM32CubeH7 release.

With Regards,
Aya

[ASAHSTM - 2026-02-25T08:01:49Z]
Hello @rxa1031,

The fix is now available in [STM32CubeH7](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7) v1.13.0.

With Regards,

Title: Keil compiler AC6 throwing error: 'sys/time.h' file not found Body:
Hi,
I am observing an issue related with inclusion of time.h when generating project for NUCLEO-H753ZI with help of STM32CubeMX.
attached ZIP file contains project and IOC file
[lwIP_Ethernet_WORKS_03.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25109447/lwIP_Ethernet_WORKS_03.zip)

I initially thought it to be an issue with LWIP related code, but later figured out that the problem reported in the file
STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1\Middlewares\Third_Party\LwIP\system\arch\cc.h is because of below conditional compilation which should work for Keil AC5 but will not work for Keil AC6
#if defined (__GNUC__) & !defined (__CC_ARM)

#define LWIP_TIMEVAL_PRIVATE 0
#include <sys/time.h> #endif
#if defined (__GNUC__) & !( defined(__CC_ARM) /* Arm Compiler 5 */ || ( defined(__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010000) /* Arm Compiler 6 (armclang) */ ) )

#define LWIP_TIMEVAL_PRIVATE 0
#include <sys/time.h> #endif
May you please help resolve the concern. The above fix may cause other issues to pop up, which shall also need fix within ethernetif.c

Thanks,

title: [H7] #331: Incorrect format specifier use requested by compiller
content:
Issue #331: Incorrect format specifier use requested by compiller

Title: Incorrect format specifier use requested by compiller

Body:
Hi All,

I assume on 32 bit and 64 bit MCU int32_t type variable should always mean 4 bytes wide memory (preferably _int_ and not _long_).

The compiler is forcing me to use "_ld_" instead of "_d_" when I am trying to print a _int32_t_ type value.

[IMAGE ATTACHED]

Google search shows:

[IMAGE ATTACHED]

Please suggest a fix.

Thanks!
Rajeev

Comments:
[MKISTM - 2026-01-08T10:19:31Z]
Hello @rxa1031,

Thank you for your report, could you please specify which example from the repository you used? Also, kindly confirm whether the line you mentioned is part of the original example or if it was added by you.

BR,
Mohamed.

[rxa1031 - 2026-01-08T13:43:48Z]
Hello @MKISTM ,

Attaching sample project.

[Core.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24496536/Core.zip)

The project reports below error:

../Core/Src/main.c:100:21: warning: format '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument 2 has type 'uint32_t' {aka 'long unsigned int'} [-Wformat=]
  100 |   printf("Value is %d\r\n", u32Val);
      |                    ~^       ~~~~~~
      |                     |       |
      |                     int     uint32_t {aka long unsigned int}
      |                    %ld


Regards,
Rajeev

[MKISTM - 2026-01-08T14:03:36Z]
Hello @MrAbhi19,

Thank you for your report. 

I noticed that the project was created by yourself, and you have not encountered any unexpected behavior or errors related to the software provided in this repository.

as we mentioned in the [Contributing guide](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
> 3. Support requests and questions
> For support requests or any other question related to the product, the tools, the environment, you can submit a post to the ST Community on the appropriate topic [page](https://community.st.com/s/topiccatalog).

When you need support or you have any other question related to the product, the tools, the environment, you can submit a post to the ST Community.

Please let us know if you encounter any specific unexpected behavior or errors related to the software provided in this repository. and we will be happy to assist further.

I will proceed to close this issue, Thank you for your understanding.

BR,
Mohamed Chaker.

[rxa1031 - 2026-01-08T14:07:41Z]
Hello @MKISTM 

I shared the sample project so as to concentrate on the issue. The issue is that the compiler is asking me to use "%lu" and "%ld" (which are used for **long** type value) in place of "%u" and "%d" for a 32 bit value.

The actual project which I was working on is shared under different Issue number:
https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24423344/NUCLEO-H753ZI.zip

title: [H7] #330: NUCLEO-H753ZI duplicate macro defenition and incorrect COM
content:
Issue #330: NUCLEO-H753ZI duplicate macro defenition and incorrect COM

Title: NUCLEO-H753ZI duplicate macro defenition and incorrect COM

Body:
Dear Reviewer, ST Team,

I am observing duplicate macro definition. One in Header file and the other passed to compiler using the -D command.
I am also observing issue with USART3 use in Asynchronous mode.

I've created project to execute on NUCLEO-H753ZI using latest version of STM32CubeMX. Using board selector -> Commercial Part Number, I selected NUCLEO-H753ZI and only did basic configuration like updating SYSCLK to 480 MHz (then used resolve clock issues), Debug using Serial Wire, RCC -> Parameter Settings -> Power Parameters -> Power Regulator Voltage Scale -> Power Regulator Voltage Scale 0. Then generated code to compile with STM32CubeIDE.

Issues observed:

**Issue 1:**

In file included from C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/BSP/STM32H7xx_Nucleo/stm32h7xx_nucleo.h:30,
                 from ../Core/Inc/main.h:32,
                 from ../Core/Src/main.c:20:
../Core/Inc/stm32h7xx_nucleo_conf.h:50: warning: "USE_NUCLEO_144" redefined
   50 | #define USE_NUCLEO_144
      | 
<command-line>: note: this is the location of the previous definition
In file included from C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/BSP/STM32H7xx_Nucleo/stm32h7xx_nucleo.h:30,
                 from C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/BSP/STM32H7xx_Nucleo/stm32h7xx_nucleo.c:22:
../Core/Inc/stm32h7xx_nucleo_conf.h:50: warning: "USE_NUCLEO_144" redefined
   50 | #define USE_NUCLEO_144
      | 
<command-line>: note: this is the location of the previous definition
In file included from C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/BSP/STM32H7xx_Nucleo/stm32h7xx_nucleo.h:30,
                 from ../Core/Inc/main.h:32,
                 from ../Core/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_msp.c:22:
../Core/Inc/stm32h7xx_nucleo_conf.h:50: warning: "USE_NUCLEO_144" redefined
   50 | #define USE_NUCLEO_144
      | 
<command-line>: note: this is the location of the previous definition
In file included from C:/ST/Repository/STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.12.1/Drivers/BSP/STM32H7xx_Nucleo/stm32h7xx_nucleo.h:30,
                 from ../Core/Inc/main.h:32,
                 from ../Core/Src/stm32h7xx_it.c:21:
../Core/Inc/stm32h7xx_nucleo_conf.h:50: warning: "USE_NUCLEO_144" redefined
   50 | #define USE_NUCLEO_144
      | 
<command-line>: note: this is the location of the previous definition
arm-none-eabi-gcc -o "NUCLEO-H753ZI.elf" @"objects.list"   -mcpu=cortex-m7 -T"D:\IMPORTANT_CODES\STM32CubeIDE\NUCLEO-H753ZI_BSP_UART3\STM32H753ZITX_FLASH.ld" --specs=nosys.specs -Wl,-Map="NUCLEO-H753ZI.map" -Wl,--gc-sections -static --specs=nano.specs -mfpu=fpv5-d16 -mfloat-abi=hard -mthumb -Wl,--start-group -lc -lm -Wl,--end-group
Finished building target: NUCLEO-H753ZI.elf
 
arm-none-eabi-size  NUCLEO-H753ZI.elf 
arm-none-eabi-objdump -h -S NUCLEO-H753ZI.elf  > "NUCLEO-H753ZI.list"
   text	   data	    bss	    dec	    hex	filename
  19988	     44	   1740	  21772	   550c	NUCLEO-H753ZI.elf
Finished building: default.size.stdout
 
Finished building: NUCLEO-H753ZI.list
 

23:10:07 Build Finished. 0 errors, 4 warnings. (took 1s.26ms)


**Issue 2:**
Even though the IOC file informs PD8 and PD9 (related with USART3) are under BSP control, the generated code is using COM1 instead of COM3 / UART3 / USART3 as observed in main.c

  /* Initialize COM1 port (115200, 8 bits (7-bit data + 1 stop bit), no parity */
  BspCOMInit.BaudRate   = 115200;
  BspCOMInit.WordLength = COM_WORDLENGTH_8B;
  BspCOMInit.StopBits   = COM_STOPBITS_1;
  BspCOMInit.Parity     = COM_PARITY_NONE;
  BspCOMInit.HwFlowCtl  = COM_HWCONTROL_NONE;
  if (BSP_COM_Init(COM1, &BspCOMInit) != BSP_ERROR_NONE)
  {
    Error_Handler();
  }

and in stm32h7xx_nucleo.c

#if (USE_BSP_COM_FEATURE > 0)
UART_HandleTypeDef hcom_uart[COMn];
USART_TypeDef* COM_USART[COMn]   = {COM1_UART};
#endif


for issue 2, please help with correction of port / USART passed to BSP_COM_Init(). Also suggest how to update to desired baud-rate (other than 115200) or to desired number of bits per word length (like 9 bits)

Please help.

Thanks!
Rajeev

[NUCLEO-H753ZI.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24423344/NUCLEO-H753ZI.zip)

[rxa1031 - 2026-01-04T18:12:53Z]
Another observation:
User needs to use STM32CubeMX, modify IOC file and regenerate code to modify value assigned to macros present with stm32h7xx_nucleo_conf.h. Any manually made modification is lost on code regeneration. Also USE_NUCLEO_144 gets added again under defined symbol (-D) even when it was deleted or the macro was modified to disable it. The only way to disable it is to add USE_NUCLEO_144 under -U (so that -DUSE_NUCLEO_144 is nullified later by -UUSE_NUCLEO_144)

Thank you for your report.

I confirm your first reported issue; I'll log an internal tracker.

Regarding the second point, there is no issue: USART3 is mapped to COM1 as specified in the stm32h7xx_nucleo.h file:

#define COM1_RX_AF                    GPIO_AF7_USART3
#define COM1_TX_AF                    GPIO_AF7_USART3


BR,
Mohamed CHAKER.

Regards,
Rajeev

content:
Issue #328: issue init sd card on stm32 h7, using sdmmc1 + mdma + fatfs + freertos.

Title: issue init sd card on stm32 h7, using sdmmc1 + mdma + fatfs + freertos.

Body:
im having issue initializing sd card when i try to use it with the freertos, as the cubemx forcefully enables the dma templete option in fatfs. so i configured the fatfs, mdma and sdmmc1 peripherals correctly, but still the sdmmc init fails with return code 3. which is FR_NOT_READY.

regards,
mayur rajput

sorry for not updating status here, the issue has been resolved with disabling the mdma and let cubemx manage it internally.

title: [H7] #327: EEPROM emulation example - bugs
content:
Issue #327: EEPROM emulation example - bugs

Title: EEPROM emulation example - bugs

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
The example was ported to our custom board with STM32H735V and there I discovered the bugs.
IDE used is STM32CubeIDE version 1.19.0.

**Describe the bug**
1. As mentioned [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/309#issuecomment-3025018673), there are missing calls to SCB_EnableICache() before exit points in EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable().
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L617-L645
Instruction cache then may end up disabled forever.

2. EE_VerifyPageFullyErased() - if really the whole sector is to be checked, either increments have to be 2-byte or dereferenced value be uint32_t (four bytes). I prefer the former since the value is checked against ERASED which is  uint16_t.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L341-L360
My solution is that EndAddress = (uint32_t)(Address + (PAGE_SIZE - 2U)); and increment Address = Address + 2;.

3. EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() does not actually check if all the bytes are set to 0xFFFFFFFF. Since there are two 32-byte writes, actually 16 32-bit words should be checked for 0xFFFFFFFF. 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L622-L634
The code actually checks only the first word. My fix was along the lines of
if (is_flashword_erased(Address) && is_flashword_erased(Address + 32))
with

static inline int is_flashword_erased(uint32_t addr) {
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) {
        if (*((__IO uint32_t*)(addr + i*4)) != 0xFFFFFFFFU) return 0;
    }
    return 1;
}


4. This is the gravest one that I think actually caused our problems. It consists of three parts:
a. Wrong offset in EE_Init() initial check for detecting the variable. 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L137
Where does the value 6 come from? I think that the correct should be 128 - 64 for the header and 64 for the address offset. So actually
if (( *(__IO uint16_t*)(PAGE0_BASE_ADDRESS + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE + EE_ADDRESS_OFFSET)) == VirtAddVarTab[VarIdx]) where PAGE_HEADER_SIZE is 64 and EE_ADDRESS_OFFSET is 64.
b. Wrong stopping address in EE_ReadVariable() - the last check goes to the page header.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L398. I think that the correct code is
while (Address >= (PageStartAddress + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE))
or maybe just > since the first four-word actually contains the variable value.
c. EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() start address - this is the main problem
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L612
The check actually starts at the sector beginning which should contain the page header. Even though it should not be erased if it is a valid page, this ends up colliding with the header after some time. I think that the correct start address should be Address = (uint32_t)(EEPROM_START_ADDRESS + (uint32_t)(ValidPage * PAGE_SIZE) + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE);


**How To Reproduce**
With intensive writing, I always end up with both pages containing data with the page header inconsistent which in turn results in deleting of one of the pages and missing data. I think that it was caused by the point 4. which caused header invalidation and subsequent data inconsistency. With the fixes the code now seems to be running reliably.

**Aditional Context**
Again, also relevant to STM32H743I-EVAL. Not sure for NUCLEO-H7A3ZI-Q, maybe all the offsets I introduce should be halved since the writes are half the size.

Comments:
[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:00:32Z]
ST Internal Reference: 219313

[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:03:35Z]
Hello @khevessy, 

Thank you for your report. Regarding point 4 that you mentioned, I am unable to identify any issues with the current implementation. Could you please provide more details or clarify the specific problem you are experiencing?

Best regards,

[khevessy - 2025-10-13T07:41:19Z]
Page header has 2 bytes at the beginning (receive data / valid page), then there are 30 zero bytes from the 32 byte write. Since EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() increases the address by 64 when searching the erased words, there are also 32 empty bytes, that's why I say that the header is 64 bytes in total.

4a. IIUC, code tries directly checking the first variable saved in the memory. However, dereferencing PAGE0_BASE_ADDRESS + 6 goes into page header.
4b. Search for the variable to read goes into the page header in the last iteration.
4c. Search for erased address for write of the variable starts at the page beginning which is occupied by the page header. The variable can never be written there.

With only points from 4., I am now unable to reproduce the issue with invalid header, it was probably caused by some combination of the mentioned issues since I was fixing them step by step.

[MKISTM - 2026-03-31T14:39:21Z]
Hello @khevessy,

Sorry for later reply.  The valid point has been published since last release **V1.13.0**

Let's explain the analyze result of last point **(4)** step by step:

**4.a** is valid:
The current use of + 6 is wrong: it falls inside the page header and does not point to the virtual address field of an EEPROM element.
The offset must be derived dynamically, based on the element index (VarIdx) and the element size, as shown in the Gerrit link.

**4.b** is not valid:
In EE_ReadVariable(), the search start address is computed as:

/* Get the valid Page end Address */
Address = (uint32_t)((EEPROM_START_ADDRESS - 32) + (uint32_t)((1 + ValidPage) * PAGE_SIZE));

Given the layout:

- Each EEPROM element = 64 bytes

- For the last element in the page, this Address points to the last virtual address field.
- The page header uses:
  - First 32 bytes: page status
  - Second 32 bytes: left erased and never used (skipped when the first element is written).

The function scans backwards in steps of 64 bytes, always landing on virtual address locations of elements. When we eventually reach the first element’s virtual address, subtracting 64 bytes from there brings us to:

PageStartAddress + 32

which corresponds exactly to the end of the header area.

**4.c** isn't valid: 
- In EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable(), starting from the page base 
  
  Address = EEPROM_START_ADDRESS + ValidPage * PAGE_SIZE;
   
is intentional. The function scans the page in 64-byte steps, and each EEPROM element is 64 bytes. Beginning at the page base keeps all Address = Address + 64; steps aligned with element boundaries.

- The page header word is never 0xFFFFFFFF:
  - The header state is stored in the LSB 16 bits (0xFFFF for ERASED),
  - The MSB 16 bits are 0x0000, so the word is 0x0000FFFF, not 0xFFFFFFFF.  
  Therefore, the header cannot be mistaken for a free 32‑byte block, and there is no risk it will be reused or erased by this logic.

- If you shifted the start to + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE, all subsequent +64 increments could be misaligned with the real 64-byte elements, and the free space detection would no longer work correctly.

So, the current start address at the page beginning is correct and must be kept.

Kind regards,
Mohamed.

[khevessy - 2026-04-01T10:55:50Z]
Hello,


I checked the new release.

1. You just removed all occurrences to I-Cache enable / disable and left all cache maintenance to the user, understood.

2. You skipped this point altogether. EE_VerifyPageFullyErased() still increments by 4 bytes while dereferencing uint16_t, so it only checks every other 16-bit word instead of all words in the page.

3. You also ignored this point. EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() checks only (*(__IO uint32_t*)Address) == 0xFFFFFFFF (the first 4 bytes) instead of  verifying all 16 32-bit words (64 bytes total) that will be written by the two FLASH_TYPEPROGRAM_FLASHWORD operations.

4a. Fixed, thanks for this.

4b. I agree that the original code was not broken, I had confused myself regarding the > vs >= condition. My change was not incorrect, but it was addressing something that was not actually broken. Sorry about that.

4c. 
   - yes, the header shouldn't ever contain 0xFFFF at the start of a valid page 
   - due to some cache coherency issues (missing D-cache invalidation), we sometimes observed stale page header values in EE_FindValidPage() - particularly both headers appearing as 0x0000 (page valid), which led to incorrect page selection for write and consequent header corruption
   - treating the header area as a candidate write location seems not robust (even if the header is never expected to be erased)
   - about the misalignment: my proposed PAGE_HEADER_SIZE referred to the full 64-byte reserved region before the first EEPROM element, not just the first 32-byte page-status flashword. Under that definition, starting the allocation scan at page_base + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE preserves 64-byte alignment. The misalignment concern applies only if PAGE_HEADER_SIZE is interpreted as 32 bytes

Best regards,
Karel
paragraphs: Issue #327: EEPROM emulation example - bugs

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
The example was ported to our custom board with STM32H735V and there I discovered the bugs.
IDE used is STM32CubeIDE version 1.19.0.

**Describe the bug**
1. As mentioned [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/309#issuecomment-3025018673), there are missing calls to SCB_EnableICache() before exit points in EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable().
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L617-L645
Instruction cache then may end up disabled forever.

2. EE_VerifyPageFullyErased() - if really the whole sector is to be checked, either increments have to be 2-byte or dereferenced value be uint32_t (four bytes). I prefer the former since the value is checked against ERASED which is  uint16_t.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L341-L360
My solution is that EndAddress = (uint32_t)(Address + (PAGE_SIZE - 2U)); and increment Address = Address + 2;.

3. EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() does not actually check if all the bytes are set to 0xFFFFFFFF. Since there are two 32-byte writes, actually 16 32-bit words should be checked for 0xFFFFFFFF. 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L622-L634
The code actually checks only the first word. My fix was along the lines of
if (is_flashword_erased(Address) && is_flashword_erased(Address + 32))
with

4. This is the gravest one that I think actually caused our problems. It consists of three parts:
a. Wrong offset in EE_Init() initial check for detecting the variable. 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L137
Where does the value 6 come from? I think that the correct should be 128 - 64 for the header and 64 for the address offset. So actually
if (( *(__IO uint16_t*)(PAGE0_BASE_ADDRESS + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE + EE_ADDRESS_OFFSET)) == VirtAddVarTab[VarIdx]) where PAGE_HEADER_SIZE is 64 and EE_ADDRESS_OFFSET is 64.
b. Wrong stopping address in EE_ReadVariable() - the last check goes to the page header.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L398. I think that the correct code is
while (Address >= (PageStartAddress + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE))
or maybe just > since the first four-word actually contains the variable value.
c. EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() start address - this is the main problem
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/8bc11985e1ffb47ab74bac67d20e48fa26038dd3/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L612
The check actually starts at the sector beginning which should contain the page header. Even though it should not be erased if it is a valid page, this ends up colliding with the header after some time. I think that the correct start address should be Address = (uint32_t)(EEPROM_START_ADDRESS + (uint32_t)(ValidPage * PAGE_SIZE) + PAGE_HEADER_SIZE);

**How To Reproduce**
With intensive writing, I always end up with both pages containing data with the page header inconsistent which in turn results in deleting of one of the pages and missing data. I think that it was caused by the point 4. which caused header invalidation and subsequent data inconsistency. With the fixes the code now seems to be running reliably.

**Aditional Context**
Again, also relevant to STM32H743I-EVAL. Not sure for NUCLEO-H7A3ZI-Q, maybe all the offsets I introduce should be halved since the writes are half the size.

Comments:
[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:00:32Z]
ST Internal Reference: 219313

[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:03:35Z]

Thank you for your report. Regarding point 4 that you mentioned, I am unable to identify any issues with the current implementation. Could you please provide more details or clarify the specific problem you are experiencing?

Page header has 2 bytes at the beginning (receive data / valid page), then there are 30 zero bytes from the 32 byte write. Since EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() increases the address by 64 when searching the erased words, there are also 32 empty bytes, that's why I say that the header is 64 bytes in total.

4a. IIUC, code tries directly checking the first variable saved in the memory. However, dereferencing PAGE0_BASE_ADDRESS + 6 goes into page header.
4b. Search for the variable to read goes into the page header in the last iteration.

title: [H7] #325: Problems in the LwIP_TCP_Echo_Server example
content:
Issue #325: Problems in the LwIP_TCP_Echo_Server example

Title: Problems in the LwIP_TCP_Echo_Server example

Body:
[IMAGE ATTACHED]

In the LwIP_TCP_Echo_Server example, in the file
STM32CubeH7/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_TCP_Echo_Server/Src/ethernetif.c,
lines 396 and 408 initialize port G instead of port B.

Also, in the file main.c, line 290 may contain an incorrect parameter.
The example runs if the parameter is set to "MPU_ACCESS_SHAREABLE" (with the corrected port initialization).

Comments:
[ASAHSTM - 2025-09-17T13:55:15Z]
Hello @adlerm1,

Thank you for your report. Referring to the user manual, the correct pinout is:

TXD0    --------------> PG13
TXD1    --------------> PG12
TX_EN   --------------> PG11

The mistake is in the comment, and we will fix it soon. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

With Regards,
Aya

[ASAHSTM - 2025-09-24T16:33:38Z]
ST Internal Reference: 218076

Fixed in Commit: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/commit/eaf80a4c9a54695c1658e1386ba244734b359fc9

title: [H7] #324: ETH with LWIP after configuring with CUBEMX does not build correctly
content:
Issue #324: ETH with LWIP after configuring with CUBEMX does not build correctly

Title: ETH with LWIP after configuring with CUBEMX does not build correctly

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * NucleH753ZI
 * CUBEMX 6.15.0
 * FW Pack 1.12.1

**Describe the bug**
After enabling ETH in RMII and additionally enabling LWIP (both with default configuration provided by CUBEMX - no custom changes) build process crashes not being able to find ETH related definitions:

terminal
/project_path/st/generated_code/LWIP/Target/ethernetif.c:117:1: error: unknown type name 'ETH_DMADescTypeDef'
  117 | ETH_DMADescTypeDef DMARxDscrTab[ETH_RX_DESC_CNT] __attribute__((section(".RxDecripSection"))); /* Ethernet Rx DMA Descriptors */
      | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/project_path/st/generated_code/LWIP/Target/ethernetif.c:118:1: error: unknown type name 'ETH_DMADescTypeDef'
  118 | ETH_DMADescTypeDef DMATxDscrTab[ETH_TX_DESC_CNT] __attribute__((section(".TxDecripSection")));   /* Ethernet Tx DMA Descriptors */
      | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/project_path/st/generated_code/LWIP/Target/ethernetif.c:141:1: error: unknown type name 'ETH_HandleTypeDef'; did you mean 'EXTI_HandleTypeDef'?
  141 | ETH_HandleTypeDef heth;
      | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      | EXTI_HandleTypeDef
 

**How To Reproduce**
1. Prepare NUCLEOH753ZI,
2. In CUBEMX disable BSP LED pins,
3. In CUBEMX enable ETH in RMII and LWIP,
4. Generate code
5. Try to build project with your buildsystem (I am using custom CMAKE which links against stm32cubemx)

**Remarks**
If that is the issue with how I generate code, then please point me towards correct resources

Comments:
[ASAHSTM - 2025-09-15T16:25:49Z]
Hello @PeterWaIIace,

Thank you for your report. 
Have you tried generating your project with a different toolchain? If so, do you encounter the same errors?

With Regards,

[ASAHSTM - 2025-09-22T13:29:35Z]
Hello @PeterWaIIace,

Any update please?

With Regards,

[PeterWaIIace - 2025-09-22T14:10:35Z]
Hey @ASAHSTM,

I was overload with other stuff at work! I have not generated code with different toolchains.

But it seems that issue was false-positive on my side. My project has two different stm32h7xx_hal_conf.h files due to internal organization. After enabling HAL_ETH_MODULE_ENABLED everything seems to work. 

Sorry for bothering you!

> Now, as this issue is not directly related to some software component
> published within this repository (CMSIS, HAL, BSP, etc.) but rather to our
> ecosystem (namely the Cube MX tool), please allow me to close it.

> in the ST Community

> in the CubeMX related section. There you shall find people who will
> address your request and whom you will hopefully find a solution with.
>
> Thank you again for your report. We are looking forward to reading from
> you again.

title: [H7] #322: STM32H7 USB PCD driver calls CDC transmission in the interrupt, causing communication interruption
paragraphs: Issue #322: STM32H7 USB PCD driver calls CDC transmission in the interrupt, causing communication interruption
Title: STM32H7 USB PCD driver calls CDC transmission in the interrupt, causing communication interruption

Body:
**Caution**
When a high-priority timer frequently sends large data packets, interrupt preemption may occur. This leads to the scenario where after the function HAL_PCD_IRQHandler() executes hcdc->TxState = 0U, the timer interrupt calls CDC_Transmit_HS to send data. At this point, there might be an abnormal clearing of the status flag, resulting in transmission failure?

**Describe the set-up**
  STM32H723 development board with USB interface

**Describe the bug**
After running for some time, the STM32H723 development board (with USB HS interface) fails to send data to the PC via USB CDC.

**Bug fix method – please help verify correctness?**
@ stm32h7xx_hal_pcd.c line: 1216
#if (USE_HAL_PCD_REGISTER_CALLBACKS == 1U)
            hpcd->DataInStageCallback(hpcd, (uint8_t)epnum);
#else
            HAL_PCD_DataInStageCallback(hpcd, (uint8_t)epnum);
#endif /* USE_HAL_PCD_REGISTER_CALLBACKS */
            **return;**

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2025-07-31T11:40:01Z]
Hello @tangb765,

It looks there is a problem with the way you are clearing the TIM's flag. You are using the assignment operator =, while, I guess, you intend to do a **bitwise operation** using the &= operator as shown below.
Could this be the origin of the problem?

Moreover, it is advised to configure **the USB with a higher priority than the TIM**. Otherwise, and since USB operations are mainly based on interrupts, calling a USB operation from a TIM ISR is likely to result in some kind of **priority inversion** situation.
I hope this helps. Please let me know whether this solves your issue.

[tangb765 - 2025-08-02T07:28:21Z]
Dear, @ALABSTM 
Thank you for your response.
1、Yes, TIM13->SR = ~(TIM_FLAG_UPDATE) is a coding error. After fixing it, there was no improvement.
2、The interrupt priority of USB cannot be set to the highest (as other peripheral interrupts require the highest priority). After increasing it to a priority higher than TIM13, there was some improvement, but the issue still occurs occasionally.

[ALABSTM - 2025-08-05T10:43:59Z]
Dear @tangb765,
Could it be that the printf() you are calling from the IRQHandler() is messing things up?

Why not trying to call it after returning from the interrupt. You could use a variable to act as a flag, that would be set within the IRQHandler(). In your main program, whenever this flag is set, you could call the printf() and reset the flag.
Please let me know whether this helps solving the problem. With regards,
I hope my previous comment helps you resolve the issue you are facing. Since the driver does not appear to be the cause, please allow me to close this thread. Thank you for your understanding.

title: [H7] #321: STM32H74xI/G Fails to Initialize System Clock Config with Generated Code by STM32CubeMX
content:
Issue #321: STM32H74xI/G Fails to Initialize System Clock Config with Generated Code by STM32CubeMX

Title: STM32H74xI/G Fails to Initialize System Clock Config with Generated Code by STM32CubeMX

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H743XIHx
 * arm-none-eabi-gcc  (Arm GNU Toolchain 14.2.Rel1 (Build arm-14.52)) 14.2.1 20241119
 * openocd 0.12.0 (2024-09-16)

**Describe the bug**
When I used the default configuration generated by STM32CubeMX(with the highest clock frequency - 480Mhz), st-link loss its connection. While debugging with gdb, openocd complained that Error: jtag status contains invalid mode value - communication failurewhen it ran into the code as follows.

__HAL_PWR_VOLTAGESCALING_CONFIG(PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE1); // Or PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE2, but PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE3 is OK


It seems that HAL cannot set __VCORE__ bit in the reg and make the st-link fail to connect to the chip. As the result I have no way but to downgrade the clock frequency to 200MHz.

**How To Reproduce**
1. New a project with STM32CubeMX and Set the clock frequency higher than 200MHz.
2. Compile and download the generated code.
3. After download it once successfully, you cannot download it twice (st-link loses the connection).


**Additional context**
gdb stack

(gdb) c
Continuing.

paragraphs: Issue #321: STM32H74xI/G Fails to Initialize System Clock Config with Generated Code by STM32CubeMX
Title: STM32H74xI/G Fails to Initialize System Clock Config with Generated Code by STM32CubeMX

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H743XIHx
 * arm-none-eabi-gcc  (Arm GNU Toolchain 14.2.Rel1 (Build arm-14.52)) 14.2.1 20241119
 * openocd 0.12.0 (2024-09-16)

**Describe the bug**
When I used the default configuration generated by STM32CubeMX(with the highest clock frequency - 480Mhz), st-link loss its connection. While debugging with gdb, openocd complained that Error: jtag status contains invalid mode value - communication failurewhen it ran into the code as follows.
__HAL_PWR_VOLTAGESCALING_CONFIG(PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE1); // Or PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE2, but PWR_REGULATOR_VOLTAGE_SCALE3 is OK
It seems that HAL cannot set __VCORE__ bit in the reg and make the st-link fail to connect to the chip. As the result I have no way but to downgrade the clock frequency to 200MHz.

**How To Reproduce**
1. New a project with STM32CubeMX and Set the clock frequency higher than 200MHz.
2. Compile and download the generated code.
3. After download it once successfully, you cannot download it twice (st-link loses the connection).

**Additional context**

title: [H7] #319: Dual core STM32H7 - error in startup code for CM7
content:
Issue #319: Dual core STM32H7 - error in startup code for CM7

Title: Dual core STM32H7 - error in startup code for CM7

Body:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/bf1cd36e9d5cdcecddbb6745cd43ac6f1fa27bb1/Projects/NUCLEO-H745ZI-Q/Templates/BootCM4_CM7/CM7/Src/main.c#L65-L66

In case of timeout the error handler won't be called. Consider in line 66:  if ( timeout **<=** 0 )

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2025-06-30T13:31:27Z]
ST Internal Reference: 213120

[KRASTM - 2025-07-03T08:29:44Z]
Hello @pavel-a,

Could you please share with us which toolchain was used and its version? Also, please let us know if there was a particular configuration, such as optimization.

With regards,

[KoynovStas - 2025-07-03T12:27:49Z]
> Could you please share with us which toolchain was used and its version? Also, please let us know if there was a particular configuration, such as optimization.

I think it doesn't matter what optimization is installed and what compiler (toolchain) we will use to compile it. @pavel-a claims that if a timeout occurs and (timeout == 0), then the condition (timeout < 0) is always false.


Possible fixes are either:
cpp
  while((__HAL_RCC_GET_FLAG(RCC_FLAG_D2CKRDY) != RESET) && (timeout-- > 0));
  if ( timeout <= 0 ) // < replaced to <=
  {
    Error_Handler();
  }


or
cpp
  while((__HAL_RCC_GET_FLAG(RCC_FLAG_D2CKRDY) != RESET) && (timeout-- >= 0)); // > replaced to >=
  if ( timeout < 0 )
  {
    Error_Handler();
  }

[CHVSTM - 2025-07-03T13:05:33Z]
Hello,

I don't see any bug because of timeout-- => the timeout is decreased after the test.
If timeout = 1, the comparison gives (timeout > 0) = TRUE, then timeout-- gives 0.
In the next loop, the comparison gives (timeout > 0) = FALSE, then timeout-- gives -1.
Consequently, the test if (timeout <0) is TRUE => Error_Handler() is called.

Tell me if my demo is wrong.
Best regards,

[MiloAkerman - 2025-07-03T21:43:07Z]
@CHVSTM is right. If timeout is 0, in the next iteration timeout-- will evaluate to 0, which will fail the condition and end the while loop. However, since the -- is applied as a suffix, timeout will now evaluate to -1, which will pass the if condition.

[pavel-a - 2025-07-03T22:00:37Z]
My wrong. Very sorry. Please close this.

title: [H7] #316: Add CMake Support
content:
Issue #316: Add CMake Support

Title: Add CMake Support

Body:
# Overview

I’d like to contribute CMake integration for this repository, enabling projects that use CMake to easily consume it as a dependency.

# Proposal

## Find Package Support

Add support to allow this repository to be discovered via CMake’s find_package() command. This involves providing a Find<package>.cmake module file, where <package> is a name representing this repository.

For example: FindSTM32CubeH7.cmake.

## Usage

### Fetching the Repository

Consuming projects would include the following in their CMake configuration:

cmake
FetchContent_Declare(
    stm32cubeH7
    GIT_REPOSITORY git@github.com:STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7.git
    GIT_TAG v1.12.1
    GIT_SUBMODULES_RECURSE TRUE
    GIT_PROGRESS TRUE
)

FetchContent_MakeAvailable(stm32cubeH7)

list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${stm32cubeH7_SOURCE_DIR})


This fetches the repository (including submodules) into the CMake build directory.

The CMAKE_MODULE_PATH is then updated to include the repository path, so CMake can find the custom FindSTM32CubeH7.cmake file during find_package() calls.

### Finding the Package

Once fetched, the consuming project would locate and request specific components using:

cmake
find_package(
    STM32CubeH7
    COMPONENTS
        rcc
        gpio
        i2c
        usb_hid_device
    REQUIRED
)


This locates FindSTM32CubeH7.cmake and triggers additional includes for CMake files within this repository that manage locating source files for the requested components.

In the example above, the consuming project requests the rcc, gpio, i2c, and usb_hid_device drivers. The added CMake support will identify the relevant source files and configure them into a library for the consuming project.

### Linking

Finally, the consuming project would link against the created library using:

cmake
target_link_libraries(<target> PRIVATE stm32cubeh7)


Where <target> is the name of the executable or library target defined by the consuming project via add_executable() or add_library().

# Summary

This proposal improves CMake interoperability for this repository, making it easier to integrate into modern, CMake-based embedded projects via standard tooling like FetchContent and find_package().

Comments:
[arms-spageddie - 2025-05-07T23:05:08Z]
Here is a high-level overview of the CMake infrastructure being considered:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b612a362-2711-4549-a34e-ba696a7ff0aa)

This may change slightly as it's being implemented, but I don't expect it to change too much. Any suggestions or concerns are welcome!

The priority would be to get CMake support implemented for CMSIS and HAL elements as those are necessary for starting a new project with this firmware package, along with a test project to ensure functionality. Afterwards, the priority would shift to the BSP and Components elements, then finally for Middleware elements.

Let me know if anyone has any questions, comments, or concerns. Thank you.

This may change slightly as it's being implemented, but I don't expect it to change too much. Any suggestions or concerns are welcome!

The priority would be to get CMake support implemented for CMSIS and HAL elements as those are necessary for starting a new project with this firmware package, along with a test project to ensure functionality. Afterwards, the priority would shift to the BSP and Components elements, then finally for Middleware elements.
Let me know if anyone has any questions, comments, or concerns. Thank you.
issue_title: Add CMake Support

title: [H7] #315: BDMA should check for memory addresses at transfer start
content:
Issue #315: BDMA should check for memory addresses at transfer start

Title: BDMA should check for memory addresses at transfer start

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H743VI  custom board
 * STM32CubeIDE 

**Additional context**
Via CubeMx, I set up I2C4 as master with DMA for Rx only, NVIC enabled for I2C4 event, I2C4 error, BDMA channel 0 global.

In the code,  I start the transaction with HAL_I2C_Master_Receive_DMA(&hi2c4, address << 1, gI2cDmaRecv, gI2cDmaRecvSize);

Neither HAL_I2C_MasterRxCpltCallback() nor HAL_I2C_ErrorCallback() are ever called.
BDMA_Channel0_IRQHandler() is called and in turn it calls HAL_DMA_IRQHandler() and hdma->XferCallback (i.e.: I2C_DMAError()) but I see no user-defined callbacks being called. HAL_DMA_GetError(hdma) returns HAL_DMA_ERROR_TE.

As a matter of fact, the transfer error is due to the target memory(gI2cDmaRecv) not being in the RAM_D3 block. My bad, I fixed that and it works.

Still, I see are two issues with the current approach:

- [ ] Detect errors at initialisation: when calling HAL_I2C_Master_Receive_DMA(), the HAL should check that the source and target addresses are valid for the type of DMA in use on the specific platform, insofar as possible. In this case, if using BDMA it should know that the destination buffer (pointer and size) should be in SRAM domain D3. If it's not, it should fail at initialisation and return a meaningful error.
- [ ] Call error callback if errors are encountered after initialisation: if an error is encountered by the DMA at runtime, HAL_I2C_ErrorCallback() should probably be called? I am not the only one confused by this behaviour ([see](https://community.st.com/t5/stm32-mcus-products/stm32h7-s-i2c4-using-bdma-not-triggering-complete-callbacks/td-p/53004/page/2))

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2025-09-23T09:33:14Z]
Hello @giuliomoro,

Thank you for the report, and I apologize for the delayed response.

I have analyzed the issue and checked with our team, and you are indeed right about the request. Unfortunately, there are currently no plans to implement this check, but I will keep you updated if anything changes.

Please, allow me to close the issue and thank you for understanding.

With regards,

title: [H7] #314: STM32CubeH7 1.12.1 SAI4 configuration is broken
content:
Issue #314: STM32CubeH7 1.12.1 SAI4 configuration is broken

Title: STM32CubeH7 1.12.1 SAI4 configuration is broken

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus) 

**Describe the set-up**
Custom board using an STM32H725RGV6 controller with STM32CubeIDE v1.18.0.

**Describe the bug**
In the .ioc file editor, the SAI4 peripheral configuration for STM32H725RGV6 cannot be edited (editor doesn't load the configuration options). Attempting to generate code with the SAI4 peripheral enabled also shows an error about the configuration being incorrect.

**How To Reproduce**
Create a new STM32 project in STM32CubeIDE v1.18.0, targetting STM32H725RGV6, with H7_FW v1.12.1.
In the .ioc file editor, enable the SAI4 peripheral (e.g., SAI B to "Master" mode). The configuration options window doesn't load.

Bug does not occur when using an existing .ioc file, with the same STM32CubeIDE version and microcontroller target, just using H7_FW v1.11.X.
Migrating a 1.11.X .ioc file to 1.12.1 introduces the bug again.

**Screenshots**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a9b1ba0-e8e8-4ea2-9220-ddcaa999e7e6)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d32e338-4aae-4ab3-a1db-6da68a426c04)

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2025-04-11T16:22:01Z]
ST Internal Reference: 207476

[KRASTM - 2025-05-02T15:36:19Z]
Hello @BlockWorker,

Regarding the issue, the fix will be included in the future release v6.15.0 of Cube MX or later. Please allow me to close this thread.
Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #313: Wrong selections in stm32h743 hrtim's dead time settings
content:
Issue #313: Wrong selections in stm32h743 hrtim's dead time settings

Title: Wrong selections in stm32h743 hrtim's dead time settings

Body:
**Describe the bug**

STM32CubeMX version: 6.14.0
STM32CubeMX DB version: DB.6.0.140
STM32CubeH7 firmware version: STM32Cube FW_H7 V1.12.1
Chip: STM32H743IIT6

When configuring htrim's dead time, "Prescaler" has selections like "fDTG = fHRTIM * 8" and "fDTG = fHRTIM * 4", which is not present in the version of the peripheral on STM32H743, causing a compilation error.

**How To Reproduce**

1. Enable htrim on STM32H743
2. Activate channels of hrtim
3. Go to the tab of enabled channel
4. Under "Timing Unit", change "Dead Time Insertion" to "Deadtime is inserted between output 1 and output 2"
5. Now "Dead Time" group should appear. Under "Dead Time", the default selection of "Prescaler" is "fDTG = fHRTIM * 8", left it as it is
6. Generate project and build
7. The following error occurs


arm-none-eabi-gcc "../Core/Src/system_stm32h7xx.c" -mcpu=cortex-m7 -std=gnu11 -g3 -DDEBUG -DUSE_PWR_LDO_SUPPLY -DUSE_HAL_DRIVER -DSTM32H743xx -c -I../Core/Inc -I../Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc -I../Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/Legacy -I../Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include -I../Drivers/CMSIS/Include -O0 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -fstack-usage -fcyclomatic-complexity -MMD -MP -MF"Core/Src/system_stm32h7xx.d" -MT"Core/Src/system_stm32h7xx.o" --specs=nano.specs -mfpu=fpv5-d16 -mfloat-abi=hard -mthumb -o "Core/Src/system_stm32h7xx.o"
../Core/Src/main.c: In function 'MX_HRTIM_Init':
../Core/Src/main.c:271:28: error: 'HRTIM_TIMDEADTIME_PRESCALERRATIO_MUL8' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'HRTIM_TIMDEADTIME_PRESCALERRATIO_DIV8'?
  271 |   pDeadTimeCfg.Prescaler = HRTIM_TIMDEADTIME_PRESCALERRATIO_MUL8;
      |                            ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                            HRTIM_TIMDEADTIME_PRESCALERRATIO_DIV8
../Core/Src/main.c:271:28: note: each undeclared identifier is reported only once for each function it appears in
make: *** [Core/Src/subdir.mk:34: Core/Src/main.o] Error 1
make: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....


**Screenshots**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92e8ae25-0ef7-4aa6-b089-116819558b17)

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2025-03-24T10:39:59Z]
ST Internal Reference: 205967

[KRASTM - 2025-04-14T09:22:47Z]
Hello @wangyz1997,

Regarding the issue, the fix will be included in the future release v6.15.0 of Cube MX or later. Please allow me to close this thread.
Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #310: SPDIFRX_playback code throws fault hard exception
content:
Issue #310: SPDIFRX_playback code throws fault hard exception

Title: SPDIFRX_playback code throws fault hard exception

Body:
SPDIFRX_playback code throws fault hard exception. 

Target:  STM32H747I-discovery board.

Issue: it throws fault hard exception in the code function call (WM8994_ReadID(&WM8994Obj, &id). The object is not initialized. it crashed due to NULL pointer error.

The solution : Need to initialize the wm8994 code by adding the following code 

if (WM8994_RegisterBusIO(&WM8994Obj, &IOCtx) != WM8994_OK)
  {
	  Error_Handler();
  }

title: [H7] #309: NUCLEO-H743ZI EEPROM_Emulation Bug
content:
Issue #309: NUCLEO-H743ZI EEPROM_Emulation Bug

Title: NUCLEO-H743ZI EEPROM_Emulation Bug

Body:
\Projects\NUCLEO-H743ZI\Applications\EEPROM\EEPROM_Emulation\Src\eeprom.c, EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable function

SCB_EnableICache() was issued at an inappropriate location

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c18cbc19-32be-4e9d-8df0-240c61df5dfc)

Comments:
[TOUNSTM - 2025-05-08T14:01:00Z]
Hello @Gorden1970,

I tried to run the EEPROM_Emulation example on NUCLEO-H743ZI  board using **IAR version 9.40.1** and achieved  the expected behavior as described in the [readme.md](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/readme.txt) file. Could you please give me more details about the issue you're experiencing and how I might be able to reproduce it?

Best regards,

[Gorden1970 - 2025-05-20T05:30:06Z]
In my test steps, as long as I call EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable, I will find that the overall performance is reduced. Finally, I found that the SCB_EnableICache I originally set would be changed to SCB_DisableICache

[TOUNSTM - 2025-06-17T16:31:11Z]
Hello @Gorden1970,

After discussing with our experts, they explained that disabling the I-Cache before performing flash program/erase operations is essential to ensure data integrity and proper execution. This is why the SCB_EnableICache() and SCB_DisableICache() functions were added to the EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() API. However, your concern is valid—frequent enabling and disabling of the instruction cache (I-Cache) can lead to performance degradation, as it is indeed a resource-intensive operation. I hope this addresses your concern. Could you let me know if further clarification is needed?

Best regards,

[billybednar - 2025-07-01T17:59:51Z]
I believe the issue is that EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable only re-enables the cache when it returns a PAGE_FULL error and not on success or flash error. There should be a call to SCB_EnableICache before all of the return statements that come after the disabling. As-is, it appears that the cache will always end up disabled since the caller will handle the page full case by retrying on an empty page.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/bf1cd36e9d5cdcecddbb6745cd43ac6f1fa27bb1/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L617-L649

[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:03:59Z]
ST Internal Reference: 219313

[MKISTM - 2026-03-31T13:25:29Z]
Hello @Gorden1970,

This issue has been fixed in the latest release **V1.13.0**

BR,
Mohamed.

Hello @Gorden1970,

After discussing with our experts, they explained that disabling the I-Cache before performing flash program/erase operations is essential to ensure data integrity and proper execution. This is why the SCB_EnableICache() and SCB_DisableICache() functions were added to the EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable() API. However, your concern is valid—frequent enabling and disabling of the instruction cache (I-Cache) can lead to performance degradation, as it is indeed a resource-intensive operation. I hope this addresses your concern. Could you let me know if further clarification is needed?

[billybednar - 2025-07-01T17:59:51Z]
I believe the issue is that EE_VerifyPageFullWriteVariable only re-enables the cache when it returns a PAGE_FULL error and not on success or flash error. There should be a call to SCB_EnableICache before all of the return statements that come after the disabling. As-is, it appears that the cache will always end up disabled since the caller will handle the page full case by retrying on an empty page.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/bf1cd36e9d5cdcecddbb6745cd43ac6f1fa27bb1/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L617-L649

[TOUNSTM - 2025-10-10T15:03:59Z]
ST Internal Reference: 219313

[MKISTM - 2026-03-31T13:25:29Z]
Hello @Gorden1970,

This issue has been fixed in the latest release **V1.13.0** BR,
Mohamed.
issue_title: NUCLEO-H743ZI EEPROM_Emulation Bug

title: [H7] #308: STM32 USB MSC with multiple luns can cause read/write errors
content:
Issue #308: STM32 USB MSC with multiple luns can cause read/write errors

Title: STM32 USB MSC with multiple luns can cause read/write errors

Body:
I am using STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.11.2 and found a bug in

\Middlewares\ST\STM32_USB_Device_Library\Class\MSC\Src\usbd_msc_scsi.c

that can cause read/write errors when using multiple luns of different sizes.

This is what happened ...

I was unable to open a text file on the LUN 0 drive after Windows had detected the LUN 0 and 1 drives.

I used WireShark and saw the following:

...
SCSI: Test Unit Ready LUN: 0x00 
SCSI: Response LUN: 0x00 (Test Unit Ready) (Good)
SCSI: Test Unit Ready LUN: 0x01 
SCSI: Response LUN: 0x01 (Test Unit Ready) (Good)
SCSI: Read(10) LUN: 0x00 (LBA: 0x00587bf0, Len: 1)
SCSI: Response LUN: 0x00 (Read(10)) (Check Condition) with Status: Command Failed (0x01)
...

I put several breakpoints in the SCSI_Read10 function and saw that SCSI_CheckAddressRange was failing because the hmsc->scsi_blk_nbr variable contained the number of blocks for LUN 1, not LUN 0.

The SCSI_Read10, SCSI_Read12, SCSI_Write10 and SCSI_Write12 functions are all using the hmsc->scsi_blk_size and hmsc->scsi_blk_nbr variables but those values can be wrong when using multiple luns of different sizes. Those functions should first update those values via a call to GetCapacity just like the SCSI_ReadCapacity10, SCCSI_ReadCapacity16 and SCSI_ReadFormatCapacity functions do.

I added the following code to the SCSI_Read10, SCSI_Read12, SCSI_Write10 and SCSI_Write12 functions and the issue was fixed.


if (((USBD_StorageTypeDef *)pdev->pUserData[pdev->classId])->GetCapacity(lun, &hmsc->scsi_blk_nbr, &hmsc->scsi_blk_size) != 0)
{
  SCSI_SenseCode(pdev, lun, NOT_READY, MEDIUM_NOT_PRESENT);
  return -1;
}

With regards,

See also [STM32CubeL4#41](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeL4/pull/41)

[ALABSTM - 2026-03-26T16:44:55Z]

With regards,

title: [H7] #307: RBU error because of bug in stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c
content:
Issue #307: RBU error because of bug in stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c

Title: RBU error because of bug in stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c

Body:
The problem is this line of code in stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c i think.
tailidx = (descidx + 1U) % ETH_RX_DESC_CNT;

The descriptor list is organized as a ring buffer, e.g. desc[0], desc[1], desc[2], desc[3], desc[4], desc[5]

The tail pointer must set "behind" the current descriptor, not in front of it. E.g. current descriptor index = 2, tail pointer must be set to 1, and not 3!

RBU occurs when ETH_DMACRXDTPR = ETH_DMACCARXDR

You can try this fix maybe.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ea39309-bc19-4559-9801-3484c0cf9e84)

You can also check this comment. Are you sure you save the index here?

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/62c0ef8f-ca2a-4360-b148-dfc6b62cb4ac)

Comments:
[ASEHSTM - 2025-01-27T15:11:52Z]
Hello @nrg83,

Thank you for your report. This bug has been fixed in commit [ceda3ce](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/commit/ceda3ceeca2ee77a76d2de2ee6b560ad87c90a48) by correcting the calculation of the tail pointer so that it points to the last updated descriptor.

With Regards,

[ASEHSTM - 2025-02-04T11:07:28Z]
Hello @nrg83,

Please allow me to close this issue.


With Regards,

title: [H7] #306: STM32H7 ADC problem
content:
Issue #306: STM32H7 ADC problem

Title: STM32H7 ADC problem

Body:
Following the request in this [issue](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/304), I have created this one.

Describe the set-up
I am using a custom board with the following specifications:

MCU: STM32H743BIT6
STM32CubeMX version: 6.13
H7 firmware version: V1.12.0

(IOC file included) In ADC, I had set the resolution to be 12 bit for both ADC1 and ADC3, but only ADC1 is affected and ADC3 is generated without setting(and so the code presume it as 16 bit) although I have checked and it has been set to be 12 bit in IOC file.
[ADC_H7.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18405810/ADC_H7.zip)

Comments:
[RJMSTM - 2025-01-17T17:10:17Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

I am taking over your project and i try to understand why STM32CubeMX does not generate the resolution for ADC3. I repeat the process and attempt to configure ADC3 alone with different resolutions (8, 12, 16, etc.), but STM32CubeMX is unable to generate the resolution for ADC3 instance on STM32H743.

Unfortunately, we don't treat aspect related to STM32CubeMX tool in our GitHub repositories. They are rather treated at the [STM32CubeMX](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx) dedicated page of the ST Community.

Since this issue is not directly related to the STM32Cube firmware but rather to our ecosystem, please allow me then to close this thread. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[vahidajalluian - 2025-01-17T17:24:39Z]
My account has been suspended in the forum. If my account hasnt been suspended, I would have opened an issue on the forum, so if you can please inform them to solve this issue. and since you are saying that this is not releated to this repository but to the ecosystem, it is ok to close this issue.

[RJMSTM - 2025-02-05T13:45:18Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

Please don't worry. I have reported your issue to the STM32CubeMX team, and I am hopeful that it will be addressed in an upcoming release.

Regards,

[RJMSTM - 2025-02-05T13:46:11Z]
ST Internal Reference: 202321

title: [H7] #305:  STM32H7 CUBEMX SD Card FATFS NVIC problem
content:
Issue #305:  STM32H7 CUBEMX SD Card FATFS NVIC problem

Title:  STM32H7 CUBEMX SD Card FATFS NVIC problem

Body:
Following the request in this [issue](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/304), I have created this one.

Describe the set-up
I am using a custom board with the following specifications:

MCU: STM32H743BIT6
STM32CubeMX version: 6.13
H7 firmware version: V1.12.0

Describe the bug
When one creates a project from scratch with stm32cubemx this issue will happen.

Enabling SDMMC and Fatfs, I could not make it work and emulate the SDCARD. However, in the F4 series program I had earlier, I saw that it was forcibly enabled NVIC, so I enabled the NVIC and the problem was solved.

Comments:
[KRASTM - 2025-01-16T14:03:02Z]

Thank you for the report.

Could you please, share your .ioc file, and more details about the error?

I will share a checked ioc to reproduce the issue by sunday. I am not at work these two days.

Any news please?

will send an ioc file.

>

> .
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

[vahidajalluian - 2025-02-05T05:39:01Z]
@ALABSTM @KRASTM 
Here is the example code with IOC that doesn't force activation of NVIC. Sorry for being (too much) late
[sdTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18667161/sdTest.zip)

[KRASTM - 2025-02-07T09:27:37Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

Regarding your issue, I think there is a misunderstanding. The SDMMC driver supports 3 modes: polling, interrupt and DMA like almost all IP, for the NVIC and as mentioned in the brief of the driver file, it enabled when interrupt process used.

> (#) Initialize the SDMMC low level resources by implementing the HAL_SD_MspInit() API:
        (##) Enable the SDMMC interface clock using __HAL_RCC_SDMMC_CLK_ENABLE();
        (##) SDMMC pins configuration for SD card
            (+++) Enable the clock for the SDMMC GPIOs using the functions __HAL_RCC_GPIOx_CLK_ENABLE();
            (+++) Configure these SDMMC pins as alternate function pull-up using HAL_GPIO_Init()
                  and according to your pin assignment.
        **(##) NVIC configuration** if you need to use interrupt process (HAL_SD_ReadBlocks_IT()
             and HAL_SD_WriteBlocks_IT() APIs).
            (+++) Configure the SDMMC interrupt priorities using function HAL_NVIC_SetPriority();
            (+++) Enable the NVIC SDMMC IRQs using function HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ()
            (+++) SDMMC interrupts are managed using the macros __HAL_SD_ENABLE_IT()
                  and __HAL_SD_DISABLE_IT() inside the communication process.
            (+++) SDMMC interrupts pending bits are managed using the macros __HAL_SD_GET_IT()
                  and __HAL_SD_CLEAR_IT()
        (##) No general propose DMA Configuration is needed, an Internal DMA for SDMMC Peripheral are used.

I tried the same config with F4 Serie, and I didn't find that it was forcibly enabled NVIC, I think it's a choice for the user to enable or not NVIC and may depend on the application. 

If you could share your API sequence used in your project for further analysis, otherwise you can check examples and/or application with DK and Eval boards under projects for further details.

With regards,

[vahidajalluian - 2025-02-08T05:41:47Z]
@KRASTM Thank you for your follow up
My mistake about activating NVIC forcibly, but if I don't activate it, It wont work with Fatfs, and reason is unknown for me.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c873b10-f874-4d72-b8ac-8d821abdfb60)

[KRASTM - 2025-02-10T10:36:33Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

Since your application includes other IP such as USB and ADC beside SDMMC and FATFS as described in #304, also you do not activate NVIC for SDMMC (in other word you work with polling mode), maybe another interrupt is the source of your problem and to get it to work you need to enable NVIC.

I suggest you only test SDMMC and FATFS to be sure, otherwise if you could share with us your sequence and perhaps some details in debug mode.

With regards

[vahidajalluian - 2025-02-13T11:20:25Z]
Dear @KRASTM with the project I have sent here using STM32H7 CUBEMX on which SD Card and FATFS are enabled, I couldnt make it work even it doesnt emulate it. But after enabling mentioned intereupt, sd card could be emulated.

[KRASTM - 2025-02-17T08:55:18Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

Just one last request, could you please send me the same example sdTest.zip with NVIC enabled. I think I have found the cause, just to compare and be sure.

With regards,

[vahidajalluian - 2025-02-17T09:19:45Z]
Ok, I will send it tomorrow, I am not at the site today.

[KRASTM - 2025-02-20T09:22:11Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

Regarding the issue, the fix will be included in the future release v6.15.0 of Cube MX or later. Please allow me to close this thread.
Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #304: STM32H7 USB, SD Card and ADC problem
content:
Issue #304: STM32H7 USB, SD Card and ADC problem

Title: STM32H7 USB, SD Card and ADC problem

Body:
Since my forum account has been banned, I'm reporting bugs here instead.

**Describe the set-up**
I am using a custom board with the following specifications:

MCU: STM32H743BIT6
STM32CubeMX version: 6.13
H7 firmware version: V1.12.0

**Describe the bug**
 When one create project from scratch with stm32cubemx these bugs will appear.

1- USB Device Descriptor Issue with Optimizations
 I had also this problem with f4 series, when enabling optimization (o1 and above), USB device descriptor cannot be estimated, and therefore no device is presented Device manager of windows. 
Solution: Modify the Get_SerialNum function in usbd_desc.c as follows:


static void Get_SerialNum(void)
{
  uint32_t deviceserial0;
  uint32_t deviceserial1;
  uint32_t deviceserial2;

  deviceserial0 = *(uint32_t *) DEVICE_ID1;
  deviceserial1 = *(uint32_t *) DEVICE_ID2;
  deviceserial2 = *(uint32_t *) DEVICE_ID3;

  deviceserial0 += deviceserial2;

//  if (deviceserial0 != 0)
//  {
//    IntToUnicode(deviceserial0, &USBD_StringSerial[2], 8);
//    IntToUnicode(deviceserial1, &USBD_StringSerial[18], 4);
//  }

  USBD_StringSerial[2] = 'D';
  USBD_StringSerial[4] = 'E';
  USBD_StringSerial[6] = 'V';
  USBD_StringSerial[8] = 'M';
  USBD_StringSerial[10] = 'E';
}


2-USB HS Pin Configuration Issue

Enabling USB HS in device-only mode forces PB13 to take on an alternate function, but the specific function is unclear. Despite configuring PB13 as an input, the pin behavior was erratic, and how hard I have tried, I couldn't understand what is the problem, so I switched to USB FS peripheral.

3- Enabling USB does not automatically set the USB clock to 48 MHz, causing the USB to be unrecognizable by endpoint systems.

4- Enabling SDMMC and Fatfs, I could not make it work and emulate the SDCARD. However in F4 series program I had earlier, I saw that it was forcibly enabled NVIC, so I had enabled the NVIC and the problem solved.

5- (IOC file included) In ADC, I had set the resolution to be 12 bit for both ADC1 and ADC3, but only ADC1 is affected and ADC3 is generated without setting(and so the code presume it as 16 bit) although I have checked and it has been set to be 12 bit in IOC file.

[ADC_H7.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18343241/ADC_H7.zip)

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2025-01-13T11:50:02Z]
Hi @vahidajalluian,

Really sorry to hear your account has been disabled. Thank you for the above report. I have a request though. Would you mind logging a different issue for each one of problems 4 and 5, as they relate to two other topics than the USB?

Thank you,

[vahidajalluian - 2025-01-14T05:35:00Z]
@ALABSTM I have created two new issues for item 4 and 5.
And for those who don't know why my account has been disabled, It has been disabled, purely for being in Iran :( .

[vahidajalluian - 2025-02-01T09:10:22Z]
@ALABSTM Sorry, I had misleadingly said PB13, but it is PB12. Sorry for my mistake.

[vahidajalluian - 2025-04-02T13:32:42Z]
@ALABSTM any updates on this issue?
Could you reproduce Usb problems?

[ALABSTM - 2025-07-31T11:13:47Z]
Hello @vahidajalluian,

I hope you are doing well. Below the feedback from our development team regarding the USB-related points you reported.

1. We tested the original Get_SerialNum() with optimization level O1 and above using both H7 and F4 MCU and encountered **no issue**. The issue you faced is likely to be due to something else. 
 
2. Actually, the **internal PHY** of the STM32H743 can generate **only full speed** signals. To reach high speed, there is a need for an **external PHY** chip. The interface with such external PHY chip is called **ULPI**. This interface requires **12** GPIO, among which **PB12** and **PB13**. This is why both pins are configured as alternate functions **when the HS is selected**. For more details, you can refer to:
    * table 9 of [ DS12110](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h743bi.pdf) ( Rev. 10).
    * section "57.4.6 High-speed OTG PHY connected to OTG_HS" of [RM0433](https://www.st.com/resource/en/reference_manual/rm0433-stm32h742-stm32h743753-and-stm32h750-value-line-advanced-armbased-32bit-mcus-stmicroelectronics.pdf) (Rev. 8)
 
3. Depending on the peripherals enabled and your configuration, it may happen that the STM32CubeMX cannot resolve some aspects of the clock tree. In such case, you have to do it manually.

I hope this clarifies the different points. Please allow me to close this issue.

With regards,

[vahidajalluian - 2025-07-31T14:05:45Z]
Would you please also test it with o3?
I had to set it to o3 since I was using LVGL

content:
Issue #303: STM32H750XBH6 sends error data when using UART4 of the 120M clock in Latest version(STM32Cube FW H7 V1.12.1)

Title: STM32H750XBH6 sends error data when using UART4 of the 120M clock in Latest version(STM32Cube FW H7 V1.12.1)

Body:
STM32H750XBH6 sends error data when using UART4 of the 120M clock in latest version(STM32Cube FW H7 V1.12.1).But in STM32Cube FW H7 V1.11.1,the same code has no errors.Cubemx version is latest version v6.13.0.When I make UART4 clock descend to 75M，the error is ok.In STM32Cube FW H7 V1.11.1,the UART4 clock do work well in 120M clock.
