title: [H7] #201: BSP driver for STM32H743I/753I -EVAL, fix for using LEDs without the I2C I/O extender
content:
Issue #201: BSP driver for STM32H743I/753I -EVAL, fix for using LEDs without the I2C I/O extender

Title: BSP driver for STM32H743I/753I -EVAL, fix for using LEDs without the I2C I/O extender

Body:
This patch fixes LEDs directly connected to GPIOs when the I2C I/O extender is disabled.
File:   Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c#L99-L108
**Describe the set-up**
 * Board: STM32H743I/753I EVAL-2
 * IDE: any (I used CubeIDE)

**Describe the bug**
I needed to reuse the I2C for the I/O extender for other purpose, so removed #define USE_BSP_IO_CLASS 
Then two LEDs connected directly to MCU (red and green) stopped working.  
Fix proposed below.  
Maybe this applies to other similar boards.

**How To Reproduce**
see above

**Additional context**

The fragment above should be:


static const  uint32_t LED_PIN[LEDn] = {LED1_PIN,
#if (USE_BSP_IO_CLASS > 0)
                                        LED2_PIN,
#else
                                       0,
#endif
                                        LED3_PIN,
#if (USE_BSP_IO_CLASS > 0)
                                        LED4_PIN
#else
                                       0,
#endif
                                       };






Patch below:



diff --git a/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c b/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c
index eb5a8c9b..6628ba70 100644
--- a/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c
+++ b/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval.c
@@ -99,10 +99,14 @@ static GPIO_TypeDef* LED_PORT[LEDn] = {
 static const  uint32_t LED_PIN[LEDn] = {LED1_PIN,
 #if (USE_BSP_IO_CLASS > 0)
                                         LED2_PIN,
+#else
+                                        0,
 #endif
                                         LED3_PIN,
 #if (USE_BSP_IO_CLASS > 0)
                                         LED4_PIN
+#else
+                                        0,
 #endif
                                        };
 
--

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2022-04-11T07:22:42Z]
Hi @pavel-a,

Thank you for your report. An internal tracker has been logged and a fix will be implemented and made available in the frame of a future release.

Thank you again for you report.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2022-04-11T07:23:14Z]
ST Internal Reference: 126180

[ALABSTM - 2024-03-14T08:29:37Z]
Fixed in commit b9d11df3020deef82310c88f3d8590d076b43ef7

title: [H7] #199: SysTick interrupt priority confusion
content:
Issue #199: SysTick interrupt priority confusion

Title: SysTick interrupt priority confusion

Body:
As written [here](https://community.arm.com/arm-community-blogs/b/embedded-blog/posts/cutting-through-the-confusion-with-arm-cortex-m-interrupt-priorities)

> Arm Cortex-M processors offer very versatile interrupt priority management, but unfortunately, the multiple priority numbering conventions used in managing the interrupt priorities are often counter-intuitive, inconsistent, and confusing, which can lead to bugs

When using an OS, the priority for SysTick is crucial to have everything working properly.
And here comes the confusion.
Here I can see
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L255-L256

But in several documentation (as an example [here](https://forums.freertos.org/t/systick-priority-vs-all-cortex-m-priorities/9289)) I read that SysTick should run at **the lowest** possible priority.

Looking here
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L170
I see that the priority it's set to TICK_INT_PRIORITY which is 0xF
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_conf_template.h#L166

I think that the documentation for HAL_InitTick() is wrong.Can someone double check, please?

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2022-01-30T08:46:53Z]
Hi @escherstair,

Thank you for this report. We will get back to you with a feedback as soon as possible. Please excuse the delay it may take us sometimes to reply. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-12T09:43:01Z]
Hi @escherstair,

Thank you for your question. Actually, as you mentioned and in compliance with existing documentation, the SysTick should have the **lowest priority possible**.

The extract you mentioned cannot be understood correctly without the preceding key sentence which conditions such practice: *Care must be taken **if** HAL_Delay() is **called from** a peripheral ISR process*.

The complete paragraph is the one below:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/b340b13929e36a3427b8d94e8b1006022f82273f/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L254-L256

I hope this answers your question.

With regards,

[escherstair - 2022-04-12T11:51:30Z]
I think now I understood, and so I propose tho change
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/b340b13929e36a3427b8d94e8b1006022f82273f/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L254-L256
into

  *       The Systick must run at the lowest **possible** priority, and the concept of **possible** is crucial.
  *       Care must be taken if HAL_Delay() is called from a peripheral ISR process,
  *       In this case the SysTick interrupt must have higher priority (numerically lower)
  *       than the peripheral interrupt. Otherwise the caller ISR process will be blocked.
  *       And so, the lowest possible priority must be higher than the peripheral interrupt priority.


Do you agree to my proposal?

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-14T09:50:57Z]
Hi @escherstair,

Thank you for your proposal. However, we prefer stick to the current one. Indeed, as you already mentioned, the TICK_INT_PRIORITY is by default defined to be the lowest possible.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_conf_template.h#L166

I would say the capitalized 'T' in the middle of the sentence (at line 255) is probably the cause of the confusion. Actually, the word "The" itself reveals to be **redundant** with the second one.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/b340b13929e36a3427b8d94e8b1006022f82273f/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L255

A fix will be published to have this updated. Thank you again for your proposal and thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-14T09:51:07Z]
ST Internal Reference: 126437
paragraphs: Issue #199: SysTick interrupt priority confusion

As written [here](https://community.arm.com/arm-community-blogs/b/embedded-blog/posts/cutting-through-the-confusion-with-arm-cortex-m-interrupt-priorities)

Do you agree to my proposal? [ALABSTM - 2022-04-14T09:50:57Z]
Hi @escherstair,
Thank you for your proposal. However, we prefer stick to the current one. Indeed, as you already mentioned, the TICK_INT_PRIORITY is by default defined to be the lowest possible.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_conf_template.h#L166
I would say the capitalized 'T' in the middle of the sentence (at line 255) is probably the cause of the confusion. Actually, the word "The" itself reveals to be **redundant** with the second one.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/b340b13929e36a3427b8d94e8b1006022f82273f/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal.c#L255
A fix will be published to have this updated. Thank you again for your proposal and thank you for your comprehension.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2022-04-14T09:51:07Z]
ST Internal Reference: 126437
issue_title: SysTick interrupt priority confusion

title: [H7] #198: DOC and API have a bug in describing STM32H7 ETH->DMACTDRLR (ETH_DMACTXRLR) register
content:
Issue #198: DOC and API have a bug in describing STM32H7 ETH->DMACTDRLR (ETH_DMACTXRLR) register

Title: DOC and API have a bug in describing STM32H7 ETH->DMACTDRLR (ETH_DMACTXRLR) register

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus) 

**Describe the set-up**
 * The board (either ST RPN reference or your custom board)
STM32H745ZI
 * IDE or at least the compiler and its version
VISUAL STUDIO

**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

post in the forum about this issue:
https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00001JCPy0SAH/eth-transmit-ring-buffer-doesnt-wrap-around-stm32h745zi


STM32H745ZI

code comment states:
ETH_DMACTDRLR_TDRL_Msk   /* Transmit Descriptor Ring Length */

documentation states:
TDRL[9:0]: Transmit Descriptor Ring Length
This field sets the maximum number of Tx descriptors in the circular descriptor ring. The
maximum number of descriptors is limited to 1K descriptors. It is recommended to put a
minimum ring descriptor length of 4.

both are wrong. You actually need to write RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1 (minus one) in order for buffer to work properly.

I set value to 5 and buffer never wraps around when I send 6th item. Then I set it to 4 and it sends 5 items normally and then wraps around on 6th item. so buffer size is 5 but you need to write 4 into the register.


**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project

2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)

3. The use case that generates the problem

4. How we can reproduce the problem


**Additional context**
If you have a first analysis or patch correction, thank you to share your proposal.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2022-01-27T15:27:44Z]
Hi @usaguerrilla,

Thank you for your contribution. The point you reported has been already treated at [ST Community](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00001JCPy0SAH/solved-bug-in-docs-eth-transmit-ring-buffer-doesnt-wrap-around-stm32h745zi). An internal bug tracker has been logged and it will be treated by our development team.

As the issue is already treated at the **ST Community**, please allow me then to close this thread to avoid issues duplications.  Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

Since the section .pbss already includes all privileged_data, the .pdata is obsolete.

title: [H7] #191: ADC clock frequency greater than maximum allowed and LL_ADC_StartCalibration never called in STM32H723ZG example
content:
Issue #191: ADC clock frequency greater than maximum allowed and LL_ADC_StartCalibration never called in STM32H723ZG example

Title: ADC clock frequency greater than maximum allowed and LL_ADC_StartCalibration never called in STM32H723ZG example

Body:
**Issue 1**

Table 79 on page 156 of the [STM32H723ZG Datasheet](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h723zg.pdf) describes the 16-bit ADC characteristics of the STM32H723ZG. Table 79 states that the maximum ADC clock frequency is 50 MHz. In addition, if the ADC clock frequency is 50 MHz, Table 79 also states that BOOST must be set to 11 (binary).

On the other hand, the NUCLEO-H723ZG [ADC_AnalogWatchdog eample](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/tree/v1.9.0/Projects/NUCLEO-H723ZG/Examples_LL/ADC/ADC_AnalogWatchdog) sets the ADC clock frequency to 65 MHz [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/v1.9.0/Projects/NUCLEO-H723ZG/Examples_LL/ADC/ADC_AnalogWatchdog/Src/main.c#L250) (520 MHz / 4 / 2 = 65 MHz) which is above the allowed maximum of 50 MHz and never sets BOOST to 11.

**Issue 2**

If I understand the datasheet and reference manual correctly, [this code](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/v1.9.0/Projects/NUCLEO-H723ZG/Examples_LL/ADC/ADC_AnalogWatchdog/Src/main.c#L495-L498) in function Activate_ADC:

c
    /* Run ADC self calibration */
    // todo : define which calib parameter has to be used
    // LL_ADC_StartCalibration(ADC1, LL_ADC_CALIB_OFFSET, LL_ADC_SINGLE_ENDED);
    // LL_ADC_StartCalibration(ADC1, LL_ADC_CALIB_OFFSET_LINEARITY, LL_ADC_SINGLE_ENDED);


should be:

c
    /* Run ADC self calibration */
    LL_ADC_StartCalibration(ADC1, LL_ADC_CALIB_OFFSET_LINEARITY, LL_ADC_SINGLE_ENDED);


The way the code is currently structured, Activate_ADC is only called once form main which implies that LL_ADC_StartCalibration(ADC1, LL_ADC_CALIB_OFFSET_LINEARITY, LL_ADC_SINGLE_ENDED) should be called.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2022-01-30T08:38:35Z]
Hi @fivdi,

Thank you for this report. We will get back to you with a feedback as soon as possible. Please excuse the delay it may take us sometimes to reply. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2022-02-22T17:16:12Z]
ST Internal Reference: 123212

[ASELSTM - 2023-04-07T12:19:55Z]
Hi @fivdi,


Referring to the ADC Clock scheme within reference manual [RM0468](https://www.st.com/resource/en/reference_manual/rm0468-stm32h723733-stm32h725735-and-stm32h730-value-line-advanced-armbased-32bit-mcus-stmicroelectronics.pdf) Figure 161,   a clock divider factor of 2 is applied by default to the clock provided to the ADC analog block (fadc_ker_ck) .

Furthermore, it is up to the user to choose whether to perform the ADC calibration or not and it should be done only during the ADC hardware calibration. There is thus no needs to execute this code section each time.

Hope this would help you. Please allow me thus to close this thread.

With regards,

[fivdi - 2023-09-03T12:04:47Z]
@ASELSTM yes, that helps, it's clearer now, thank you.

title: [H7] #190: [STM32H750-DB] ExtMem_Application: hardfault caused and Unable to jump to Application
content:
Issue #190: [STM32H750-DB] ExtMem_Application: hardfault caused and Unable to jump to Application

Title: [STM32H750-DB] ExtMem_Application: hardfault caused and Unable to jump to Application

Body:
Hello,

I am using the STM32H750-DB and I am trying to copy a code from External Flash to External SDRAM to run it. I have followed what is described in the Readme.txt for :

- BootROM : Execution From External SDRAM , Data on Internal SRAM

I can easily run a code from external Flash, but not from external SDRAM.

I upload the external Application via CubeProgrammer and I can check it is present. also I can check that the Application code is copied to the external SDRAM.

Here is my Linkerscript Memory for extrenal Application:


 MEMORY
{
FLASH (rx)      : ORIGIN = 0xD0000000, LENGTH = 32768K
RAM (xrw)      : ORIGIN = 0x20000000, LENGTH = 128K
ITCMRAM (xrw)      : ORIGIN = 0x00000000, LENGTH = 64K
}

also I have well set the Memories in the "Memory.h" file :

#define DATA_AREA           USE_INTERNAL_SRAM
#define CODE_AREA           USE_EXTERNAL_SDRAM
#define BINARY_AREA         USE_SPI_NOR

Also the Interrupt vector :

SCB->VTOR = EXT_RAM_BK2_BASE | VECT_TAB_OFFSET; /* Vector Table Relocation in Internal FLASH */

with #define EXT_RAM_BK2_BASE          (0xD0000000UL)


Once I try to jump to the External SDRAM, HardFault exception is raised. Could you please support in this?

Thanks,
Ayoub

Comments:
[EmbeddedAyoub - 2021-11-10T15:04:03Z]
Hello,

Issue resolved! 

The SDRAM is by default set as XN (Execute Not Permitted) so you need to configure the MPU and enable the Execution for the external SDRAM.

Ayoub

[ALABSTM - 2021-11-15T08:55:50Z]
Hi @EmbeddedAyoub,

Glad to read you found the issue and overcame it. Users facing the same problem would find your post very helpful. Thank you.

With regards,

[EmbeddedAyoub - 2021-11-15T10:11:16Z]
> Hi @EmbeddedAyoub,
> 
> Glad to read you found the issue and overcame it. Users facing the same problem would find your post very helpful. Thank you.
> 
> With regards,

Welcome :)

title: [H7] #189: Ethernet: avoid descriptor leak with enabled timestamping
content:
Issue #189: Ethernet: avoid descriptor leak with enabled timestamping

Title: Ethernet: avoid descriptor leak with enabled timestamping

Body:
**Describe the set-up**

 * The board (either ST RPN reference or your custom board): NUCLEO-H745ZI-Q
 * IDE or at least the compiler and its version: arm-none-eabi-gcc (GNU Arm Embedded Toolchain 10-2020-q4-major) 10.2.1 20201103 (release)

**Describe the bug**

Ethernet HAL configures interrupts to be received upon filling every RX descriptor. When timestamping is enabled, application needs to process both normal and context descriptors, but there is a race condition in HAL_ETH_IsRxDataAvailable() that might miss context descriptor, effectively leaking it because they won't be accounted for in HAL_ETH_BuildRxDescriptors().

**How To Reproduce**

1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project

We're building a PTP master / slave implementation in Zephyr on STM32 that uses hardware timestamping.  Majority of the implementation is represented by this commit: https://github.com/maka-autonomous-robotic-systems/zephyr/commit/c25c72f91996f69dcee737e405272edcf951f560

2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)

drivers/src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c

3. The use case that generates the problem

Receive timestamping is enabled, hardware is filling RX normal and context descriptors and issues interrupts. When driver code is awoken and runs HAL_ETH_IsRxDataAvailable(), it might either see just normal RX descriptor available or both normal and context RX descriptors, even if both were supposed to be available. In former case, context RX descriptor is populated later, but since it's not accounted by HAL_ETH_IsRxDataAvailable() it is never released back and is thus leaked.

4. How we can reproduce the problem

One option is to use forked Zephyr code from this commit: https://github.com/maka-autonomous-robotic-systems/zephyr/commit/c25c72f91996f69dcee737e405272edcf951f560 and run gptp sample on STM32H7 board for a while. You'd want to revert this change (https://github.com/maka-autonomous-robotic-systems/zephyr/commit/1294620e92ef8ab89d03c7dc4978e69122c32e69) to switch back to unmodified STM32H7 HAL.

Another option is to make a custom example that enables timestamping similar to that commit, and observe descriptors leaked nondeterministically.

**Additional context**

PR with a proposed solution has been submitted to Zephyr Project's fork of STM32 HAL: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_stm32/pull/116

Please let me know if additional information would be helpful or if you have any feedback.

Comments:
[alsrgv - 2021-11-23T19:52:55Z]
Hi @ALABSTM, any update on this issue?

We'd like to get at least an acknowledgement of the issue, so the fix proposed in zephyrproject-rtos/hal_stm32#116 can be merged.

[ALABSTM - 2021-11-24T11:48:51Z]
Hi @alsrgv,

Please excuse this delayed reply. Our development teams said this issue is covered by the **new** ETH driver. This new ETH driver will be published soon. Unfortunately, we cannot share a date for the moment.

Thank you for your comprehension and thank you again for having reported this point.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-08T11:09:47Z]
Hi @alsrgv,

I hope you are fine. We just made available a fully reworked ETH HAL driver via pull-request [stm32h7xx_hal_driver#16](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/pull/16). The point you raised should have been addressed. You can use this new driver for your applications. Please feel free to provide your feedback [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/pull/16).

Please note also that this new ETH HAL driver **breaks the compatibility** with the previous one. We are looking forward to reading your feedback.

With regards,

[alsrgv - 2021-12-17T07:49:59Z]
@ALABSTM, thanks for fixing this up!  Do you know if anybody is working on porting Zephyr to this new API?

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-22T10:54:17Z]
Hi @alsrgv,

The point has already been reported at the Zephyr OS project-level. Here is the [link](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/41380).

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2023-04-10T11:03:25Z]
Hi @alsrgv,

The new HAL ETH driver has been made available in version **1.10.0** of this firmware, which has been published since a while. Please allow me to close this issue. Thank you again for having reported this point.

With regards,

title: [H7] #188: Error on include.
content:
Issue #188: Error on include.

Title: Error on include.

Body:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/BSP/STM32H743I-EVAL/stm32h743i_eval_lcd.h#L32

Shouldn't it be :
#include "stm32h743i_eval_conf_template.h"   ?

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-11-05T10:06:49Z]
Hi @georthom,

This is #include "stm32h743i_eval_conf.h" indeed. Actually, *_template.h files are generic files that are intended to be:
* **Copied** into your projects file tree.
* **Renamed** by removing the template suffix.
* **Customized** according to the needs of your application.

For instance, you can notice how this same file has been customized differently for two different applications.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/Audio/Audio_playback_and_record/Inc/stm32h743i_eval_conf.h#L31-L33

I hope this point is clearer now. Please allow me to close this issue.

With regards,

title: [H7] #187: Add support for ADC LDO output voltage ready bit
content:
Issue #187: Add support for ADC LDO output voltage ready bit

Title: Add support for ADC LDO output voltage ready bit

Body:
Revision V STM32H743 devices have an "ADC LDO output voltage ready bit" in the "ADC interrupt and status register". In the [reference manual](https://www.st.com/resource/en/reference_manual/rm0433-stm32h742-stm32h743753-and-stm32h750-value-line-advanced-armbased-32bit-mcus-stmicroelectronics.pdf) this bit is called LDORDY and it is bit 12 in the ADC_ISR register. 

STM32CubeH7 V1.9.0 doesn't provide support for the LDORDY bit. For example, there are no defines for LDORDY in [stm32h743xx.h](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/v1.9.0/Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h743xx.h) and there is no low level functionality for accessing LDORDY in [stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/v1.9.0/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h)

It would be good if support for the LDORDY bit in the ADC_ISR register was added to STM32CubeH7.

Initial tests with functions that I implemented myself for accessing the LDORDY bit in the ADC_ISR register indicate that the bit functions as expected with a [WeAct Studio STM32H743 Core Board](https://github.com/WeActTC/MiniSTM32H7xx).

The same issue may exist for other STM32H7 devices.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2021-11-15T17:56:20Z]
Hi @fivdi,

Thank you for your contribution. An internal tracker has been logged and a fix will be implemented and made available in the frame of a future release.

Thank you once again for your contribution.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2021-11-15T18:08:08Z]
ST Internal Reference: 117529.

[fivdi - 2023-04-10T12:17:01Z]
Thank you.

For anyone interested, support for the LDORDY bit in the ADC_ISR register was added to stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h with [ADC_ISR_LDORDY](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/v1.11.0/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L648) and [LL_ADC_IsActiveFlag_LDORDY(ADC_TypeDef *ADCx)](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/v1.11.0/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L7523-L7532).

[stm32h743xx.h](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/v1.11.0/Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h743xx.h) was also appropriately modified.

title: [H7] #186: STM32 HAL Timer - PWM_Config() does not reset the timer channel state.
content:
Issue #186: STM32 HAL Timer - PWM_Config() does not reset the timer channel state.

Title: STM32 HAL Timer - PWM_Config() does not reset the timer channel state.

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H7B3L evaluation board is used.
 * DRV8316R motor controller chip is used for driving a low-power BLDC motor.
 * Timer8 is configured to generate 6x PWM signals (with three of them being complimentary)

**Bug**
The function PWM_Config() does not reset the timer channel state to HAL_TIM_CHANNEL_STATE_RESET although it disables the channel.

**Describe the bug**
- STM32 HAL Timer library provides the following functions to work with PWM signals. PWM_Configure(), PWM_Start(), and PWM_Stop().
- A call to PWM_Configure() directly disables the PWM signal generation but it does not reset the timer channel state to HAL_TIM_CHANNEL_STATE_RESET. This leads to an unique usability bug.
- Let us say, we have configured a timer channel using PWM_Config() and then we have started the signal generation using PWM_Start().
- Suppose, the application wants to reconfigure the same timer channel for a different PWM settings. In this case, there are two possibilities. 
- The first possibility: Either the application will first call PWM_Stop() and then calls PWM_Config() function to change the channel settings. The channel can be started by calling PWM_Start() function.. This works perfectly fine. 
PWM_Stop() --> PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() .. Works perfectly.
- The second possibility: Application will call PWM_Config() directly without calling PWM_Stop. Then, tries to start the PWM signal by calling PWM_Start(). This function fails because, the internal state of timer channel is not HAL_TIM_CHANNEL_STATE_RESET but it is still HAL_TIM_CHANNEL_STATE_BUSY. Therefore, PWM_Start() fails.
PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() --> PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() .. This fails. 

**How To Reproduce**
1. Following call sequence will reproduce the bug
**PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() --> PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start()** .. This fails. 



**Workaround Solution**
In the application, one can explicitly check the state of each timer channel by using the following code *TIM_CHANNEL_STATE_GET(htim, Channel)*. If the timer channel state is busy, then call the PWM_Stop() function before reconfiguring the timer channel.
**PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() --> **PWM_Stop()** --> PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start()**. This works.
This workaround should be part of the HAL Timer library.

**Proper Bug Fix**
if PWM_Config() is called, then check the status of PWM output. There are two possible implementations.
- First Implementation: Return HAL_Error. But the application might not know the real cause of failure.
- Second Implementation: Internally call PWM_Stop() and then continue with the rest of the executions within the function. This might be a reasonable approach that I would prefer as an application developer and the user of the Timer library.
STM32 can make a decision about the implementation and leave a response in the reply section.

Best Regards,
Ajit B

Comments:
[ajitbasarur - 2021-10-22T10:33:42Z]
Hi @ALABSTM, 

Thanks for looking into the issue. I have noticed that you have included the issue in **stm32cube-mcu-**fw**-dashboard . Shouldn't the issue be included in stm32cube-mcu-**hal**-dashboard? 

Best Regards,
Ajit B

[ASELSTM - 2022-02-24T13:49:28Z]
Hi @ajitbasarur,

The **PWM_Stop()** is necessary to ensure a correct system behavior. Indeed, the configuration can't be updated since the channel is activated. Changes on the fly are not allowed unless the channel is stopped. The driver has been implemented in the way to respect this sequencing. Control and configuration are separately managed to avoid any system misbehavior. 
Hence, the correct sequence to deploy is :

PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() --> PWM_Stop() --> PWM_Config() --> PWM_Start() ...

Unfortunately, you request can't be accepted, so please allow me to close this thread. Thank you for you comprehension.

With regards,

[mrx23dot - 2024-01-09T23:27:34Z]
But what's the technical limitation to do this?
I could just modify the duty register directly.

I have the same issue on F401 HAL.

title: [H7] #184: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual
content:
Issue #184: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual

Title: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
Discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual, no hardware needed for verification.

**Describe the bug**
The HAL driver only allows the digital and analog I2C bus filters to be configured when the I2C driver has been initialized.

Functions HAL_I2CEx_ConfigDigitalFilter() and HAL_I2CEx_ConfigAnalogFilter() in stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c can be used to configure the filters.

Both functions check the state of the driver (line 103 & 149) and only allow configuring the filter when the driver is the state HAL_I2C_STATE_READY.
The I2C peripheral is enabled by calling __HAL_I2C_ENABLE(hi2c) right before setting the core to the mentioned state. (line 569 in stm32h7xx_hal_i2c.c)

The reference manual states that the filters should be configured before the I2C peripheral is enabled (pages 1973 and 1974), and actually warns explicitly that the filters should not be changed when the I2C peripheral is enabled. A reason for this warning is not given in the reference manual though. (quote: "Caution: Changing the filter configuration is not allowed when the I2C is enabled.")

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-10-21T16:19:35Z]
Hi @jeroendoggen,

Referring to the HAL_I2CEx_ConfigDigitalFilter() and HAL_I2CEx_ConfigAnalogFilter() implementation, the I2C is disabled just before configuring the filters which is in line with the reference manual specification. 

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c#L110-L111

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c#L156-L157

Once the filter configured, the I2C will be re-enabled . The check on the hi2c->State is performed to make sure that the I2C is ready for use and that there is no transfer operations ongoing.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-11-24T12:27:02Z]
Hi @jeroendoggen,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards,
paragraphs: Issue #184: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual
Title: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
Discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual, no hardware needed for verification.

**Describe the bug**
The HAL driver only allows the digital and analog I2C bus filters to be configured when the I2C driver has been initialized.
Functions HAL_I2CEx_ConfigDigitalFilter() and HAL_I2CEx_ConfigAnalogFilter() in stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c can be used to configure the filters.

Both functions check the state of the driver (line 103 & 149) and only allow configuring the filter when the driver is the state HAL_I2C_STATE_READY.
The I2C peripheral is enabled by calling __HAL_I2C_ENABLE(hi2c) right before setting the core to the mentioned state. (line 569 in stm32h7xx_hal_i2c.c)

The reference manual states that the filters should be configured before the I2C peripheral is enabled (pages 1973 and 1974), and actually warns explicitly that the filters should not be changed when the I2C peripheral is enabled. A reason for this warning is not given in the reference manual though. (quote: "Caution: Changing the filter configuration is not allowed when the I2C is enabled.")

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-10-21T16:19:35Z]
Hi @jeroendoggen,

Referring to the HAL_I2CEx_ConfigDigitalFilter() and HAL_I2CEx_ConfigAnalogFilter() implementation, the I2C is disabled just before configuring the filters which is in line with the reference manual specification.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c#L110-L111
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2c_ex.c#L156-L157
Once the filter configured, the I2C will be re-enabled . The check on the hi2c->State is performed to make sure that the I2C is ready for use and that there is no transfer operations ongoing.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2021-11-24T12:27:02Z]
Hi @jeroendoggen,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution.
issue_title: I2C: discrepancy between the HAL driver and the RM0433 reference manual

title: [H7] #183: Typos in MCU header files
content:
Issue #183: Typos in MCU header files

Title: Typos in MCU header files

Body:
See STM32CubeH7\Drivers\CMSIS\Device\ST\STM32H7xx\Include\*.h
The current release 1.9.0 still contains some minor typos in MCU header files that can be easily fixed with no side-effects.

-  HardFault_IRQn              = -13,    /*!< 4 Cortex-M Memory Management Interrupt                            */ -> +  HardFault_IRQn              = -13,    /*!< 3 Cortex-M Hard Fault Interrupt                                   */

Coretx -> Cortex

Rserved -> Reserved

Busrt -> Burst

title: [H7] #181: Oversensitive up/down touchscreen controls in BSP audio playback example
content:
Issue #181: Oversensitive up/down touchscreen controls in BSP audio playback example

Title: Oversensitive up/down touchscreen controls in BSP audio playback example

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
STM32H735G-DK discovery kit (may affect others with touch screens too)
STM32CubeIDE1.7.0
BSP example project. Source code [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/tree/master/Projects/STM32H735G-DK/Examples/BSP).

**Describe the bug**
When using the "audio play" screen of the example, the touch screen controls for increasing or decreasing frequency and volume are oversensitive. It is extremely difficult (luck, really) to select a specific rate or volume level.

**How To Reproduce**
Navigate to the "audio play" screen of the example. Attempt to raise or lower the playback frequency to a specific value. (Allowed values are 96000, 48000, 44100, 32000, 22050, 16000, 11025, 8000.)

**Additional context**
The problem seems to be that when an up/down control is touched, audio_play.c quickly receives multiple up/down actions, causing a single touch of the control triangles to make unpredictable changes (wild swings) to the frequency or volume. The code that interprets the touch screen presses is [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/STM32H735G-DK/Examples/BSP/Src/audio_play.c#L210).

If the code would ignore repeated values returned by AudioPlay_GetTouchPosition(), then the controls could operate predictably by tapping the touch screen. I have already written and tested a simple fix for this, and can submit a pull request if desired.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2021-10-06T17:24:21Z]
Hi @TheseGoTo11,

Thank you for your contribution. This request will be forwarded to our development teams for deeper analysis. We will be back to you as soon as they provide me with their answer.

This duplicated issue will remain closed. The pull-request #182 that you reported will be kept open to track the point.

Thank you for you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2021-10-06T17:24:56Z]
Duplicate of  #182

title: [H7] #177: ADC3 Oversampling Ratio incorrectly set
content:
Issue #177: ADC3 Oversampling Ratio incorrectly set

Title: ADC3 Oversampling Ratio incorrectly set

Body:
**The Bug**
In the function [LL_ADC_ConfigOverSamplingRatioShift](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L6260-L6263) (and corresponding [LL_ADC_GetOverSamplingRatio](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L6272-L6275)) the CFGR2->OVSR does not handle all cases correctly.

**More detail**
For example, for STM32H723's the CFGR2->OVSR field is different for ADC1+2 and ADC3. 

For ADC1 and ADC2:

> Bits 25:16 OSVR[9:0]: Oversampling ratio
This bitfield is set and cleared by software to define the oversampling ratio.
0: 1x (no oversampling)
1: 2x
2: 3x
...
1023: 1024x

For ADC3:

> Bits 4:2 OVSR[2:0]: Oversampling ratio
This bitfield is set and cleared by software to define the oversampling ratio.
000: 2x
001: 4x
010: 8x
011: 16x
100: 32x
101: 64x
110: 128x
111: 256x

Currently the LL dirver does not handle this special case.

**Proposed Solution**
Follow how the other functions handled this special case. For example:

__STATIC_INLINE void LL_ADC_ConfigOverSamplingRatioShift(ADC_TypeDef *ADCx, uint32_t Ratio, uint32_t Shift)
{
  MODIFY_REG(ADCx->CFGR2, ADC_CFGR2_OVSS, Shift);
#if defined(ADC_VER_V5_V90)
  if (ADCx == ADC3) {
      MODIFY_REG(adc->CFGR2, ADC3_CFGR2_OVSR, (Ratio - 1UL) << ADC3_CFGR2_OVSR_Pos);
  } else {
#endif
      MODIFY_REG(adc->CFGR2, ADC_CFGR2_OVSR, (Ratio - 1UL) << ADC_CFGR2_OVSR_Pos);
if defined(ADC_VER_V5_V90)
  }
#endif
}


**Edit:** It seems all H7 devices has the ratio set from 0->1023 for ADC1 and 2.

Comments:

@FRASTM definitely not, since I raised both these issues myself. They are probably related, but not duplicates. They point to different functions:

[#170](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/170): LL_ADC_SetResolution(adc, LL_ADC_RESOLUTION_12B);
#177: LL_ADC_ConfigOverSamplingRatioShift(...)

[RKOUSTM - 2021-10-19T13:26:13Z]
Hi @heinwessels,

Thank you for this contribution.  An internal tracker has been logged and a fix will be implemented and made available in the frame of a future release.

Thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards

[RKOUSTM - 2021-10-19T13:26:35Z]
ST Internal Reference: 116019

title: [H7] #176: NOR Flash driver does not support large flash chips (i.e. greater than
content:
Issue #176: NOR Flash driver does not support large flash chips (i.e. greater than 

Title: NOR Flash driver does not support large flash chips (i.e. greater than 

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
Hardware: Proprietary hardware containing STM32H743 and external NOR FLASH and external SDRAM.


**Describe the bug**
In the latest version of stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c, the HAL_NOR_Init() function gets an invalid CommandSet when using a large NOR Flash chip containing more than 1 bank.  Consequently, the driver asserts when it attempts to use the invalid CommandSet.  In this code, the driver attempts to get the CommandSet:


 /* Get the value of the command set */
NOR_WRITE(NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(deviceaddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_CMD_ADDRESS_FIRST_CFI), NOR_CMD_DATA_CFI);

hnor->CommandSet = *(__IO uint16_t *) NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(deviceaddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_ADDRESS_COMMAND_SET);


The CommandSet must be read from the base address of the chip, i.e. 0x00000000 (VMA 0x60000000).  However, if the chip contains more than one bank, when it is called for the second bank, it will read from 0x04000000 (VMA 0x64000000) which is invalid.  It would only work, if all banks were in separate FLASH chips. 

**How To Reproduce**

1. Connect a **single** 1Gb NOR Flash chip to a STM32H7

2. Call HAL_NOR_Init for Bank 1.  (It reads the CommandSet from the base address 0x0000000.  In my environment CommandSet is assigned 0x02)

3. Call HAL_NOR_Init for Bank 2.  (It reads the CommandSet from address 0x04000000.  In my environment CommandSet is assigned 0xFF)

4. HAL NOR Driver asserts because CommandSet is invalid.

**Additional Context**
I can't think of an elegant solution to this issue.  The driver doesn't know if the banks are in one chip or multiple chips.  I would recommend passing the base address of the chip (not bank address) to the function to obtain the CommandSet like here:


/* Get the value of the command set by reading the base address of the CHIP (not bank) */
baseAddress = 0x60000000;

NOR_WRITE(NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(baseAddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_CMD_ADDRESS_FIRST_CFI), NOR_CMD_DATA_CFI);

hnor->CommandSet = *(__IO uint16_t *) NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(baseAddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_ADDRESS_COMMAND_SET);



So initialising each bank you pass the base of address of the Flash the chip and not the Bank address.

In the meantime, I have added this patch to my driver:


/* Get the value of the command set */
#if READ_COMMAND_SET
  NOR_WRITE(NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(deviceaddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_CMD_ADDRESS_FIRST_CFI), NOR_CMD_DATA_CFI);
  hnor->CommandSet = *(__IO uint16_t *) NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(deviceaddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_ADDRESS_COMMAND_SET);
#else
  hnor->CommandSet = 0x02;    
#endif


I hope this issue can be resolved or at least find a workaround that doesn't need to patch the driver.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2022-04-11T08:30:07Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

Thank you for your report. This issue is related to a limitation of the stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c driver which supports a few NOR manufacturers. Actually, this point has been discussed internally, before we receive your request. A fix in the NOR FLASH  driver will be available in a future release to support different memory, very soon hopefully.  

Thank you again for you report. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2023-12-12T15:30:50Z]
ST Internal Reference: 168587

[ALABSTM - 2024-01-30T13:30:44Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

I hope you are fine. In your first comment, you said: "In the **latest** version of stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c". Do you mean the issue is not present when using older versions of the driver?

Another point please. You mentioned that the 2nd command set is set to 0xFF. When looking into the different command sets [supported](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/blob/b716379524ba3549e4db11804889776ffeb239d1/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c#L171-L181) by version **1.11.1** of the driver, none is defined to be 0xFF. Could it be that you are using a command set not supported by the driver?

c
/* Command set code assignment (defined in JEDEC JEP137B version may 2004) */
#define NOR_INTEL_SHARP_EXT_COMMAND_SET       (uint16_t)0x0001 /* Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_AMD_FUJITSU_COMMAND_SET           (uint16_t)0x0002 /* Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET        (uint16_t)0x0003 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_AMD_FUJITSU_EXT_COMMAND_SET       (uint16_t)0x0004 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_WINDBOND_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET     (uint16_t)0x0006 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_MITSUBISHI_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET   (uint16_t)0x0100 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_MITSUBISHI_EXT_COMMAND_SET        (uint16_t)0x0101 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_PAGE_WRITE_COMMAND_SET            (uint16_t)0x0102 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_PERFORMANCE_COMMAND_SET     (uint16_t)0x0200 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_DATA_COMMAND_SET            (uint16_t)0x0210 /* Not Supported in this driver */


Thank you in advance for your answers.

With regards,

[JRASTM - 2024-02-19T13:27:45Z]
Dear,

Come to you on this subject and understand your interrogation, about the fact that you use a memory size upper than a ST bank size support.

Now, up to this, for sure the ST driver is compliant with the bank size and common management of each of them.

Yours issue, seems out of FMC bank management. As you know each bank is link to a specific chip select.
And one interrogation on ST side, is how your application manage those toggle of chip select link to bank1 and chip select for bank2 ?

And so other point, how your application management the address swap between bank1 and bank2 during operational process ?

Thanks for your answer,
Regards

[ALABSTM - 2024-03-07T16:23:06Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

I hope you are doing well. Any feedback please?

Thanks,

[ALABSTM - 2024-03-11T09:32:48Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

So far, we could not reproduce the issue you reported.
* As suggested by @JRASTM [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/176#issuecomment-1952454979), "Yours issue seems out of FMC bank management".
* As suggested in [this](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/176#issuecomment-1916853702) comment, you may also be using a command set 0xFF not supported by the driver.

Anyway, as no feedback received so far. Please allow me to close the issue. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

hnor->CommandSet = *(__IO uint16_t *) NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(deviceaddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_ADDRESS_COMMAND_SET);

The CommandSet must be read from the base address of the chip, i.e. 0x00000000 (VMA 0x60000000).  However, if the chip contains more than one bank, when it is called for the second bank, it will read from 0x04000000 (VMA 0x64000000) which is invalid.  It would only work, if all banks were in separate FLASH chips.
**How To Reproduce** 1. Connect a **single** 1Gb NOR Flash chip to a STM32H7
2. Call HAL_NOR_Init for Bank 1.  (It reads the CommandSet from the base address 0x0000000.  In my environment CommandSet is assigned 0x02)
3. Call HAL_NOR_Init for Bank 2.  (It reads the CommandSet from address 0x04000000.  In my environment CommandSet is assigned 0xFF)
4. HAL NOR Driver asserts because CommandSet is invalid.

**Additional Context**
I can't think of an elegant solution to this issue.  The driver doesn't know if the banks are in one chip or multiple chips.  I would recommend passing the base address of the chip (not bank address) to the function to obtain the CommandSet like here:

/* Get the value of the command set by reading the base address of the CHIP (not bank) */
baseAddress = 0x60000000;
NOR_WRITE(NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(baseAddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_CMD_ADDRESS_FIRST_CFI), NOR_CMD_DATA_CFI);
hnor->CommandSet = *(__IO uint16_t *) NOR_ADDR_SHIFT(baseAddress, uwNORMemoryDataWidth, NOR_ADDRESS_COMMAND_SET);
So initialising each bank you pass the base of address of the Flash the chip and not the Bank address.
In the meantime, I have added this patch to my driver:
I hope this issue can be resolved or at least find a workaround that doesn't need to patch the driver.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2022-04-11T08:30:07Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

Thank you for your report. This issue is related to a limitation of the stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c driver which supports a few NOR manufacturers. Actually, this point has been discussed internally, before we receive your request. A fix in the NOR FLASH  driver will be available in a future release to support different memory, very soon hopefully.
Thank you again for you report. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2023-12-12T15:30:50Z]
ST Internal Reference: 168587

[ALABSTM - 2024-01-30T13:30:44Z]
Hi @david-oz00,
I hope you are fine. In your first comment, you said: "In the **latest** version of stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c". Do you mean the issue is not present when using older versions of the driver?

Another point please. You mentioned that the 2nd command set is set to 0xFF. When looking into the different command sets [supported](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/blob/b716379524ba3549e4db11804889776ffeb239d1/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_nor.c#L171-L181) by version **1.11.1** of the driver, none is defined to be 0xFF. Could it be that you are using a command set not supported by the driver?

c
/* Command set code assignment (defined in JEDEC JEP137B version may 2004) */
#define NOR_INTEL_SHARP_EXT_COMMAND_SET       (uint16_t)0x0001 /* Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_AMD_FUJITSU_COMMAND_SET           (uint16_t)0x0002 /* Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET        (uint16_t)0x0003 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_AMD_FUJITSU_EXT_COMMAND_SET       (uint16_t)0x0004 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_WINDBOND_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET     (uint16_t)0x0006 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_MITSUBISHI_STANDARD_COMMAND_SET   (uint16_t)0x0100 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_MITSUBISHI_EXT_COMMAND_SET        (uint16_t)0x0101 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_PAGE_WRITE_COMMAND_SET            (uint16_t)0x0102 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_PERFORMANCE_COMMAND_SET     (uint16_t)0x0200 /* Not Supported in this driver */
#define NOR_INTEL_DATA_COMMAND_SET            (uint16_t)0x0210 /* Not Supported in this driver */
Thank you in advance for your answers. With regards,
Come to you on this subject and understand your interrogation, about the fact that you use a memory size upper than a ST bank size support.
Now, up to this, for sure the ST driver is compliant with the bank size and common management of each of them.

Yours issue, seems out of FMC bank management. As you know each bank is link to a specific chip select.
And one interrogation on ST side, is how your application manage those toggle of chip select link to bank1 and chip select for bank2 ?
And so other point, how your application management the address swap between bank1 and bank2 during operational process ?

Thanks for your answer,
Regards [ALABSTM - 2024-03-07T16:23:06Z]
Hi @david-oz00,
I hope you are doing well. Any feedback please? Thanks,

[ALABSTM - 2024-03-11T09:32:48Z]
Hi @david-oz00,

So far, we could not reproduce the issue you reported.
* As suggested by @JRASTM [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/176#issuecomment-1952454979), "Yours issue seems out of FMC bank management".
* As suggested in [this](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/176#issuecomment-1916853702) comment, you may also be using a command set 0xFF not supported by the driver.
Anyway, as no feedback received so far. Please allow me to close the issue. Thank you for your comprehension.

title: [H7] #175: All Error and fault related functions with STM32H7XX_IT.C must me marked as __weak functions.
content:
Issue #175: All Error and fault related functions with STM32H7XX_IT.C must me marked as __weak functions.

Title: All Error and fault related functions with STM32H7XX_IT.C must me marked as __weak functions.

Body:
Hello,

I am using latest versions of STM32CubeMX and STM32CubeH7 to generate the driver code.

Below is a request to both STM32CubeMX and STM32CubeH7 community and developers.

All warning, error and fault related functions with STM32H7XX_IT.C must me marked as __weak functions. Example of a function that should be marked as __week:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Templates/Src/stm32h7xx_it.c#L59

I need to use my own custom function, like the one below, and I do not prefer calling it from within the Hard Fault function that is available with STM32H7XX_IT.C file.


void HardFault_Handler(void)
{
  __asm(
    ".global HardFault_Handler_C\n\t"	   
    "TST    LR, #4\n\t"
    "ITE    EQ\n\t"
    "MRSEQ  R0, MSP\n\t"
    "MRSNE  R0, PSP\n\t"
    "MOV    R1, LR\n\t"
    "B  HardFault_Handler_C\n\t"
     );
}	


Please help with updating all the error handling function types.

Thanks,
Rajeev

title: [H7] #174: EEPROM emulation example bug
content:
Issue #174: EEPROM emulation example bug Title: EEPROM emulation example bug Body: **Describe the set-up** The example was ported to our custom board with STM32H735V and there I discovered the bug. It is also mentioned [here](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W000012WR89SAG/eeprom-emulation-on-stm32h7) and [here](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X00009vKjpqSAC/eeprom-emulation-on-stm32h7). IDE used is STM32CubeIDE version 1.7.0.
**Describe the bug** I think all three examples for EEPROM emulation in this project have the same bug. On line 394 in function EE_ReadVariable() in eeprom.c, there should be two times the value from line 384 so it is not jumped to data. Here should be 64: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L394
Here should be 64: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L394
Here should be 32: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/NUCLEO-H7A3ZI-Q/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L394
**How To Reproduce** If there is some data with value of some virtual address, the read will return incorrect data. In STM32CubeF4 examples, there it is correct (see for example [eeprom.c](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeF4/blob/master/Projects/STM32446E-Nucleo/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L384)).
**Additional context** N/A
**Screenshots** N/A Comments: [ASELSTM - 2021-08-31T12:33:36Z] ST Internal Reference: 112562 [ALABSTM - 2021-12-20T19:05:10Z] Hi @Irisbus, I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub as you can see below. Thank you again for having reported. With regards, https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L393 https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L393 https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Projects/NUCLEO-H7A3ZI-Q/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L393

Here should be 64:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/EEPROM/EEPROM_Emulation/Src/eeprom.c#L394

content:
Issue #172: FDCAN examples for NUCLEO-H743ZI2

Title: FDCAN examples for NUCLEO-H743ZI2

Body:
**Set-up**
 * Board: NUCLEO-H743ZI2 (NUH743ZI$AT2)
 * IDE: STM32CubeIDE version 1.5.1
 * OS: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS

I tried using the fdcan setup from this [link](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/tree/master/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Examples/FDCAN) since I did not find any demo code for my set-up board. I would like some help regarding setting up fdcan parameters as I have tried all the parameters from the example codes and tried to understand the application note ([AN5348](https://www.st.com/resource/en/application_note/dm00625700-fdcan-peripheral-on-stm32-devices-stmicroelectronics.pdf)) and apply it, but neither transmission nor reception of fdcan or can data is working. Any help would be great.

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-10-07T15:05:15Z]
Hi @sourabhmisal,

The Nucleo board are not supporting the FDCAN unlike the EVAL boards. Actually, CAN transceivers should be added to the Nucleo boards to allow transmitting CAN messages. Please make sure you are adding the appropriate hardware to your Nucleo board otherwise you can use an EVAL board instead.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-11-24T11:21:25Z]
Hi @sourabhmisal,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you solve this issue.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards,

title: [H7] #170: STM32H723 ADC 3 cannot be set to 12-bit resolution using LL drivers
content:
Issue #170: STM32H723 ADC 3 cannot be set to 12-bit resolution using LL drivers

Title: STM32H723 ADC 3 cannot be set to 12-bit resolution using LL drivers

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
We are using a custom PCB with STM32H723ZG running Zephyr. We want to use ADC 3 at 12bit resolution.

**Describe the bug**
ADC3 cannot be set to 12bit resolution using the LL drivers, but always goes 10bit resolution. This is not an issue with ADC 1 or 2.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Initialize ADC 3
2. Set ADC3 resolution to 12 bit using LL_ADC_SetResolution(adc, LL_ADC_RESOLUTION_12B);
3. Read a high voltage (connect ADC pin to Vref+).
4. Observe the raw value returned never exceeding 1024, which shows 10bit resolution.
5. Read ADC3_CFGR_RES and see it's set to 10bit resolution.

**Additional context**

This is seemingly caused by a bug in the LL drivers's [LL_ADC_SetResolution function](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L2921). See the code snippet below:


if (ADCx == ADC3)
  {
    MODIFY_REG(ADCx->CFGR, ADC3_CFGR_RES, (((Resolution & 0x10UL) | 0x08UL | (Resolution & 0x04UL))  & 0x00000018UL));
  }


According to the Reference Manual (DM00603761) the ADC resolution is defined in the  ADC_CFGR register in RES which is at mask 0x18. To achieve 12bit resolution the RES value must be set to 00, and for 10bit to 01, etc. In the code snippet shown above the culprit seems to be the | 0x08UL, which will always set the lower bit regardless of desired Resolution, meaning it's impossible to set RES to 00.

On the same note: Resolution & 0x04UL doesn't do anything since the & 0x00000018UL will always mask it out.

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-08-13T16:46:02Z]
ST Internal Reference: 111930

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-20T18:51:31Z]
Hi @heinwessels,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub as you can see below. Thank you again for having reported.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_ll_adc.h#L2988-L2991

With regards,

title: [H7] #169: `I2C_Master_Transmit` waits for `TXIS` status instead of `TXE` status
content:
Issue #169: `I2C_Master_Transmit` waits for `TXIS` status instead of `TXE` status

Title: `I2C_Master_Transmit` waits for `TXIS` status instead of `TXE` status

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
Using H745ZI on the Nuclea H745ZI-Q w/ stmcube mx v6.3.0 w/ StmCube IDE v1.6.1

**Describe the bug**
The HAL I2C library checks the TXIS bit in the ISR register even in sequential mode. This bit is used in interrupt and DMA mode, but not necessarily in sequential mode. Therefore, in sequential mode, the HAL_I2C_Master_Transmit function blocks until timeout.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Talk to i2c slave via hal i2c driver in a (free)rtos enabled application.

2. HAL I2C

3. Blocking / Polling mode

4. use hal i2c + freertos

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-08-17T13:45:48Z]
Hi @fazerlicourice7,


We have tried to reproduce the issue you pointed out and unfortunately we couldn't. We have used , as you have mentioned above, the I2C in a FreeRTOS enabled application and there was no troubles with the I2C. Indeed, the communication was set as it should be and the data was successfully transmitted to the slave. We did not detect any problem with the HAL_I2C_Master_Transmit() function which was not blocked until the timeout.  Would you please make sure that your configuration is correct. 

Actually, the TXIS flag is set independently of the I2C transferring mode ( polling, interrupt or DMA).  Once the I2C_TXDR register becomes empty and the data to be transmitted must be written in the I2C_TXDR register an TXIS interrupt is generated. 
The TXIS will both indicates the states of the TXDR register and exactly when it should be written unlike the TXE which will indicates only the TXDR state. Thus, the check on the TXIS is more accurate. 

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-08-23T08:49:45Z]
Hi @fazerlicourice7,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you solve this issue.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards,

[kuro68k - 2025-02-24T14:18:44Z]
I believe I have the same issue on a G431. When using the blocking function, it checks for this flag which is never set. The interrupt code works.

title: [H7] #167: Incorrect Macro IS_ADC_EXTTRIGINJEC for H7 in FW V1.9
content:
Issue #167: Incorrect Macro IS_ADC_EXTTRIGINJEC for H7 in FW V1.9

Title: Incorrect Macro IS_ADC_EXTTRIGINJEC for H7 in FW V1.9

Body:
Hi dear STM32 team,

STMH7 firmware V1.9
file: stm32h7xx_hal_adc_ex.h

The macro  IS_ADC_EXTTRIGINJEC  needs the additional 5 checks:

                                         
 ((__INJTRIG__) == ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_HR1_ADCTRG2) ||  \
 ((__INJTRIG__) == ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_HR1_ADCTRG4) ||  \
 ((__INJTRIG__) == ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_LPTIM1_OUT)  ||  \
 ((__INJTRIG__) == ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_LPTIM2_OUT)  ||  \
 ((__INJTRIG__) == ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_LPTIM3_OUT)  ||  \


and throws an assert if  for example ADC_EXTERNALTRIGINJEC_HR1_ADCTRG2 is used as an injected trigger source parameter
in HAL_ADCEx_InjectedConfigChannel():

  assert_param(IS_ADC_EXTTRIGINJEC(sConfigInjected->ExternalTrigInjecConv));
 

The position in the h-file looks like as if the missing checks had been deleted accidentically. It's missing at least since V1.2.

I hope that helps,

best regards,
Michael

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-07-16T11:29:39Z]
Hi @MikeRDe,

Thank you for this report. The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you once again for your contribution.

With regards,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub. Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

[MikeRDe - 2021-12-21T17:36:23Z]
Hi ALABSTM,

I hope you are fine too. I noticed V1.9.1 and the fixed issue (and many more). Thanks for the support and have a good time!

With regards,
Michael

title: [H7] #166: Error handling in HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback
content:
Issue #166: Error handling in HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback

Title: Error handling in HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback

Body:
There are two problems with error handling in HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback.
Fixing these would also appease static analysis tools, even in code that is not directly affected.

## 1 - HAL_DMA_XFER_ALL_CB_ID
When HAL_DMA_XFER_ALL_CB_ID is passed as CallbackID to HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback, the function does not signal an error (return value of HAL_ERROR), **nor** does it do anything else (except locking and unlocking the handle).  

Expected behavior would be **either**:
   - pCallback function argument is registered as handler for ALL cases (this works, is consistent with HAL_DMA_UnRegisterCallback, but often not really helpful)
   - The function does nothing and returns HAL_ERROR

## 2 - Invalid argument
When CallbackID has an invalid value (i.e. something that triggers the default branch in the switch statement), then the function should return HAL_ERROR (consistent with HAL_DMA_UnRegisterCallback).

I can submit a pull request if you tell me how the problem 1 behavior should be. Personally, I would slightly prefer the first alternative (i.e. registering the same callback for ALL cases), because it is least surprising and consistent with HAL_DMA_UnRegisterCallback.

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-08-11T15:24:20Z]
Hi @radioflash,

Regarding the first point, actually it is not logical to assign a single and same function pointer to all callback types ( errors callback, complete callback , half complete callback , etc) . Unfortunately, your request can't be accepted.

However for the second point, you are absolutely right.  An HAL_ERROR should be returned in case of an invalid passed CallbackID.  The HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback() function will then be updated as follows : 

diff
    case  HAL_DMA_XFER_ABORT_CB_ID:
      hdma->XferAbortCallback = pCallback;
      break;

    default:
+     status =  HAL_ERROR;
      break;


This fix will be implemented and made available within the next release. Thank you for reporting this out.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-08-11T15:24:39Z]
ST Internal Reference: 111248

[ASELSTM - 2021-08-11T15:24:56Z]
ST Internal Reference: 111247

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-20T18:38:38Z]
Hi @radioflash,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub. Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

content:

Hi:

Team STM32

In the file stm32h7xx_ll_rtc.h

#if defined(TAMP_BKP0R_BKP)    /* This should be #if defined(TAMP_BKP0R) */

__STATIC_INLINE void LL_RTC_BKP_SetRegister(TAMP_TypeDef *TAMPx, uint32_t BackupRegister, uint32_t Data)


I cannot find macro definition TAMP_BKP0R_BKP,  so "LL_RTC_BKP_SetRegister" undeclared.

Sorry to trouble you, Thanks.

title: [H7] #163: STM32H723+CUBEH7  V1.9.0   Reset the USB Core, ResetBit is not cleared
content:
Issue #163: STM32H723+CUBEH7  V1.9.0   Reset the USB Core, ResetBit is not cleared 

Title: STM32H723+CUBEH7  V1.9.0   Reset the USB Core, ResetBit is not cleared 

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * custom board(only  STM32H723ZGT6+USB3300+LM1117-3.3V+Resistor、capacitor )
 *KEIL5

**Describe the bug**
In USB_CoreReset:
Reset the USB Core, ResetBit is not cleared 

before reset:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20830435/124530087-6d6b0180-de3e-11eb-868a-ac5ab9a5ad78.png)
after reset:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20830435/124530837-d43cea80-de3f-11eb-8881-a09622d06c75.png)
datasheet:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20830435/124530850-dbfc8f00-de3f-11eb-94e9-00c4c7c8b05f.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20830435/124530870-e61e8d80-de3f-11eb-8291-75d13e41fedc.png)

So I modified the code: 

  do
  {
    /*
   if (++count > 200000U)
    {
      return HAL_TIMEOUT;
    }
   */
  } while ((USBx->GRSTCTL & USB_OTG_GRSTCTL_CSRST) == USB_OTG_GRSTCTL_CSRST);

But the code stays here ........

CMOD bit is 1 after reset which does not meet expectations .

**How To Reproduce**
1. Please download the IOC file：
[STCubeGenerated.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/6767017/STCubeGenerated.zip)

Title: stm32h7xx_ll_hsem.h / LL_HSEM_1StepLock uses HSEM_R_LOCK instead of HSEM_RLR_LOCK to while checking RLR register

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus) 

**Describe the set-up**
LL API Bug

**Describe the bug**

HSEM_R_LOCK seem to be the same as HSEM_RLR_LOCK but to make sure there isn't a bug later it might be a good idea to use proper Lock indication.


__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t LL_HSEM_1StepLock(HSEM_TypeDef *HSEMx, uint32_t Semaphore)
{
  return ((HSEMx->RLR[Semaphore] != (HSEM_R_LOCK | LL_HSEM_COREID)) ? 1UL : 0UL);
}


**How To Reproduce**
LL API Bug

N/A

N/A

title: [H7] #161: Wrong paralle NOR setting
paragraphs: Issue #161: Wrong paralle NOR setting Title: Wrong paralle NOR setting

Body:
1. Reproduce the problem:
- Write data to parallel NOR.
- Walk through the NOR, reading each byte twice then compare. Check for mismatch. This is the code I use:
2. Cause. The current setting sets the read cycle exactly at 70ns. The problem is the tACC of the NOR has a maximum value of 70ns. If that happens, the FMC will not have time to sample valid data.
3. The fix: lengthen the cycle. In my code, I set "DataSetupTime" to 6 instead of 5. This is my code:

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2021-07-02T11:49:50Z]
Hi @trinhnhatnam,

Indeed, the DataSetupTime parameter defines the number of HCLK cycles to configure the duration of the data setup time. This parameter can be a value between Min_Data = 1 and Max_Data = 255,  so you can adopt it to your use case.

This issue is not directly related to some software component published within this repository (CMSIS, HAL, BSP, etc.) but rather to our ecosystem (namely the Cube MX tool), so please allow me to close it.
Thank you again for your reports. Do not hesitate in case you have other questions.
We are looking forward to reading from you again. With regards,

[trinhnhatnam - 2021-07-02T13:24:03Z]
Please don't close the bug. This bug is in the code published by ST that causes headaches for users, so please fix it. The wrong NOR settings that caused me headaches are in Drivers/BSP/STM32H747I-EVAL/stm32h747i_eval_nor.c

I already mentioned the piece of code I used to test the NOR. It's not a comprehensive test, but does the job for me. You can open an example project that uses NOR, then paste my test code in somewhere.

[RKOUSTM - 2021-07-02T13:57:01Z]
Hi @trinhnhatnam,
First of all, I would like to emphasize that you can reopen any closed issue if you have something else to add regarding your reported issue.
Indeed, in order to allow a better analysis of this problem, would you please share your project that you are used for further analysis.
Thank you once more for your contribution. With regards,

[trinhnhatnam - 2021-07-02T14:04:22Z]
I already mentioned the piece of code I used to test the NOR. It's not a comprehensive test, but does the job for me. You can open an example project already in STM32H7CUBE that uses NOR, then paste my test code in somewhere to test.

[RKOUSTM - 2021-08-17T11:40:12Z]
Hi @trinhnhatnam,

We tried once more to reproduce the issue you have described using the STM32H747I_EVAL BSP example published in this repository, with no success. You can use this BSP example to develop your application. I really hope you could find the root cause of this issue and, ideally, solve it. Anyway, if you ever become able to reproduce systematically the faulty behavior and find out that the problem is related to our drivers, do not hesitate to recontract me. We could reopen this issue again.
Thank you for your contribution. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

title: [H7] #159: Nucleo-STM32H743ZI2 board gets hung at SystemClock_Config();
paragraphs: Issue #159: Nucleo-STM32H743ZI2 board gets hung at SystemClock_Config();
Title: Nucleo-STM32H743ZI2 board gets hung at SystemClock_Config();

Body:
OS  : Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS
IDE : STM32CubeIDE version 1.5.1

I am not able to run any program on STM32H743ZI2. I genereated code from STM32CubeIDE and flashed on it. I had added HAL_GPIO_TogglePin(LD2_GPIO_Port, LD2_Pin) with delay of 1000 ms and flashed the program. When LD2 did not blink, I tried debugging and found out that pointer stops at SystemClock_Config(); In this function, the code enters into error handler of RCC Oscillator config function
In function HAL_RCC_OscConfig located at stm32h7xx_hal_rcc.c , the code sometimes stop when waiting for HSE to get ready
Other times, code stops running at PLL Clock
STM32H7 board also heats up. Is there any way to solve this issue?

Comments:
[Sumatt92 - 2021-06-11T18:39:39Z]
Sounds like maybe an issue with the clock configuration. IDK about that board but the h745 im using does not have an external oscillator so I use use the HSI RC for PLL source MUX (almost everything is downstream from that).
![clock_config](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45105885/121734147-fb5f0100-cab9-11eb-8781-cc318151e531.png)

[RKOUSTM - 2021-06-20T19:52:46Z]
Hi @sourabhmisal,

Thank you for your contribution. The problem seems to be related to migration to the CubeIDE. However, this public organization on GitHub is intended to promote co-development and issues posted here shall exclusively be related to the software published within the different repositories.
For any other issue (e.g. related to documentation, to hardware, to ecosystem, to user application, etc.), you shall address your questions to the [ST Community](https://community.st.com/s/topiccatalog).  There you shall look for the adequate topic and submit your question or remark.
You can use the IDE proposed by ST (IAR, MDK-ARM and SW4STM32) for example and application on STM32H7 board all of them are working well.
Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for you contribution.
issue_title: Nucleo-STM32H743ZI2 board gets hung at SystemClock_Config();

title: [H7] #158: TFTP Server Hanging on H7
content:
Issue #158: TFTP Server Hanging on H7

Title: TFTP Server Hanging on H7

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H745I-DISCO
 * STM32CubeIDE

**Describe the bug**
I am able to ping the device before the TFTP Server/Ethernet network hangs after TFTP utilization. My workaround is restarting the H7.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project:

Using this article the Ethernet for TCP/UDP protocols is working as expected:
https://community.st.com/s/article/How-to-create-project-for-STM32H7-with-Ethernet-and-LwIP-stack-working 
I tested tftp_init() server with MX_LWIP_Init() together in a RTOS task. Both TFTP PUT and TFTP GET complete file transfer. The device stops responding to ARP requests or pings after doing TFTP PUT/write file transfers.

2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)

IwIP and Ethernetif.c Driver

3. The use case that generates the problem:

TFTP PUT/write generates Ethernet hang problem every instance. TFTP GET does not generate this problem majority of time.

4. How we can reproduce the problem:

Enable IwIP TFTP and transfer files to/from H7 with a file system. I am currently using the LittleFS file system.

**Screenshots**

TFTP successfully transfers file to H7:
![TFTP_Success](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121595828-ed4aab00-c9fb-11eb-8282-ea0c8b32d2ad.png)

TFTP successful transfer in Wireshark:
![TFTP_Success2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596002-1ff4a380-c9fc-11eb-9a30-dcd9524ceca7.png)

H7 no longer responding to ARP requests:
![LWIP_No_Recovery](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596108-431f5300-c9fc-11eb-8a6b-4e8337048aab.png)

H7 not responding to pings:
![No_Response](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596161-56cab980-c9fc-11eb-854f-3c07f6db1855.png)

After stepping through the code l've found it is hanging on line 747 in Queue.c:
![fetch2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121919271-13ba6000-ccf4-11eb-809b-e8447b62cca6.png)

Comments:
[ghost - 2021-06-15T21:22:15Z]
Issue is covered by the stm32h745 errata. Data cache configured in write-through is generating corruption errors in the QSPI NOR flash memory. This is why the program is hanging at line 747 of Queue.c

**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H745I-DISCO
 * STM32CubeIDE

**Describe the bug**
I am able to ping the device before the TFTP Server/Ethernet network hangs after TFTP utilization. My workaround is restarting the H7.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project:

Using this article the Ethernet for TCP/UDP protocols is working as expected:
https://community.st.com/s/article/How-to-create-project-for-STM32H7-with-Ethernet-and-LwIP-stack-working 
I tested tftp_init() server with MX_LWIP_Init() together in a RTOS task. Both TFTP PUT and TFTP GET complete file transfer. The device stops responding to ARP requests or pings after doing TFTP PUT/write file transfers.
2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)
IwIP and Ethernetif.c Driver 3. The use case that generates the problem:
TFTP PUT/write generates Ethernet hang problem every instance. TFTP GET does not generate this problem majority of time.
Enable IwIP TFTP and transfer files to/from H7 with a file system. I am currently using the LittleFS file system.

TFTP successfully transfers file to H7:
![TFTP_Success](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121595828-ed4aab00-c9fb-11eb-8282-ea0c8b32d2ad.png)

TFTP successful transfer in Wireshark:
![TFTP_Success2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596002-1ff4a380-c9fc-11eb-9a30-dcd9524ceca7.png)

H7 no longer responding to ARP requests:
![LWIP_No_Recovery](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596108-431f5300-c9fc-11eb-8a6b-4e8337048aab.png)

H7 not responding to pings:
![No_Response](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84698378/121596161-56cab980-c9fc-11eb-854f-3c07f6db1855.png)

After stepping through the code l've found it is hanging on line 747 in Queue.c:

Comments:
[ghost - 2021-06-15T21:22:15Z]
Issue is covered by the stm32h745 errata. Data cache configured in write-through is generating corruption errors in the QSPI NOR flash memory. This is why the program is hanging at line 747 of Queue.c

title: [H7] #157: Enhancement: Provide a function that is capable of storing multiple CAN messages to CAN TX FIFOs before updating TXBAR
paragraphs: Issue #157: Enhancement: Provide a function that is capable of storing multiple CAN messages to CAN TX FIFOs before updating TXBAR
Title: Enhancement: Provide a function that is capable of storing multiple CAN messages to CAN TX FIFOs before updating TXBAR

I am trying to find a function that helps first find all available TX FIFOs and later fills them with (Classic) CAN Data and Extended IDs before updating a value to _hfdcan->Instance->TXBAR_. This code will be helpful to me in the long run.

There are only two functions in file _stm32h7xx_hal_fdcan.c_ that modify value of _hfdcan->Instance->TXBAR_. The two functions are namely _HAL_FDCAN_AddMessageToTxFifoQ()_ , and _HAL_FDCAN_EnableTxBufferRequest()_
_HAL_FDCAN_AddMessageToTxFifoQ()_ seems to update only one FIFO and then transmit its data.
_HAL_FDCAN_EnableTxBufferRequest()_ seems to only update _hfdcan->Instance->TXBAR_
I am unable to find a function that calls _FDCAN_CopyMessageToRAM()_ multiple times to fill the _CAN TX FIFOs_ before updating _TXBAR_

Please help implement above request. Thanks,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2021-06-09T14:34:43Z]
Hello,
Is it possible to change the _static_ typecast for _FDCAN_CopyMessageToRAM()_ and change it to just:
void FDCAN_CopyMessageToRAM(FDCAN_HandleTypeDef *hfdcan, FDCAN_TxHeaderTypeDef *pTxHeader, uint8_t *pTxData, uint32_t BufferIndex)

and extern it. Please suggest. Thanks,
@rxa1031
As the requested code piece / function was not available, I had to code a custom / my own function for this.

May I share the coded custom function here? Regards,
@rxa1031

Thank you for your contribution. Your report will be forwarded to our development team for analyzis and if confirmed the fix will be deployed on new STM32Cube series under dev.
We will get back to you as soon as we have an answer.

Thanks in advence for your patience and understanding. With Regards,
KORKAD
