title: [H7] #118: UART Receive with timeout interrupt issue
content:
Issue #118: UART Receive with timeout interrupt issue

Title: UART Receive with timeout interrupt issue

Body:
Dear @RKOUSTM 

I checked your response for #68 and #31 

Used STM32CubeMX version 6.2.0 with STM32CubeMX along with latest released STM32CubeH7 package version **1.9.0**

**I still observe an issue for a particular scenario.**


**Good Behavior / Scenario**
At least one byte must have been received via the Asynchronous UART port after a call to HAL_UART_Receive_IT() API is made.
In this scenario the _Receiver Timeout interrupt triggers_ after the specified time,

**BAD Behavior / Scenario**
If _NOT EVEN A SINGLE BYTE_ is received over Asynchronous UART after the HAL_UART_Receive_IT() API is executed, then the Receiver Timeout (RTOF) interrupt present with the UART related USART_ISR **NEVER SETS** and hence the _interrupt never triggers_ even though the related bits in USART_CR1 and USART_CR2 are set.

Please help me understand how to fix above issue.

With help of Receiver Timeout interrupt I want to ensure that:
1. The connected device started to communicate within a defined time period.
2. The response was not sent by connected device and hence data need to be retransmitted
3. Possibly loose connection or cable break

Please suggest what changes need to be made.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2021-03-09T18:02:31Z]
Sample code attached for reference:
[Gateway-H7-App-with-STM32CubeH7-ver-1.9.0-from-scratch-20210309-2311-IST.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/6110532/Gateway-H7-App-with-STM32CubeH7-ver-1.9.0-from-scratch-20210309-2311-IST.zip)

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-11T11:45:50Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

The Receiver Timeout event is triggered when line has been in idle state for a programmable time. Receiver timeout is expressed in terms of bit duration and it is applicable only once the transfer occurred. 

In fact, the receiver timeout counter starts counting from the received stop bit, in other words, as soon as a character is received.
As described in the [reference manual ](https://www.st.com/resource/en/reference_manual/dm00314099-stm32h742-stm32h743-753-and-stm32h750-value-line-advanced-arm-based-32-bit-mcus-stmicroelectronics.pdf) in the **"USART receiver timeout register (USART_RTOR)"**  section :

> In standard mode, the RTOF flag is set if, after the last received character, no new start bit is detected for more than the RTO value.

This is explained in more details within the  **"USART receiver timeout"** section :

> The receiver timeout counter starts counting:
> • from the end of the stop bit if STOP = ‘00’ or STOP = ‘11’
> • from the end of the second stop bit if STOP = ‘10’.
> • from the beginning of the stop bit if STOP = ‘01


So that the Receiver Timeout feature can't be used if no character have been received.  The interruption will never be trigged even though  USART_CR1_RTOIE and USART_CR2_RTOEN bits are set and an appropriate receiver timeout value is configured in the USART_RTOR register.

Hope this would help you. 

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-17T09:12:39Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.

Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution

With regards,

title: [H7] #116: Question: ISR Triggered Task priority
paragraphs: Issue #116: Question: ISR Triggered Task priority

Title: Question: ISR Triggered Task priority Body:
Hello,
I have a query with regards to Task that is to be triggered only when an ISR routine triggers it.
The task is meant to dequeue the data stored by ISR (say CAN receive) into the Queue and then act upon the received data.

### **Question 1: Maximum allowed task priority value**
Please help me understand what is the maximum allowed task priority for the above task. Will it be:

1. osPriorityISR 
2. osPriorityRealtime7
Also please help me understand who can report or use the priority:

1. osPriorityError
2. osPriorityReserved

### **Question 2: Task triggering mechanism**
Whether it is sufficient to have the task waiting for Task notification from ISR (with help of **ulTaskNotifyTake( pdTRUE, portMAX_DELAY);** API call) and the ISR triggering the task execution with help of **vTaskNotifyGiveFromISR(SW_IRQHandlerTaskHandle, NULL)** call?
Task initialization code in function main()
### **Query 3:  Queue implementation and use by ISR and task**
_Concern: Code crashes often_ **Dequeue mechanism below:**
Q_EventHandlerHandle = osMessageQueueNew (16, sizeof(toTaskEventHandler*), &Q_EventHandler_attributes);
Task code: #define dDummyMessagePriority					( 0 )
Please suggest which normal code and FreeRTOS API calls should I review and/or modify.

Thanks,
Rajeev Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2021-03-09T04:46:50Z]
Hello,

Used STM32CubeMX version 6.1.0
Mcu.UserName=STM32H753BITx
MxCube.Version=6.1.0
MxDb.Version=DB.6.0.10

STM32CubeH7 package version 1.8.0
ProjectManager.FirmwarePackage=STM32Cube FW_H7 V1.8.0

Regards,
Rajeev [rxa1031 - 2021-03-23T05:21:07Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,
Are there any findings / updates that you may be able to share with me.

Thanks,
Rajeev [RKOUSTM - 2021-04-16T15:20:19Z]
Hi @rxa1031,
Thank you for your contribution. Please excuse the delay we may take sometimes to reply. The CMSIS-RTOS2 offers the osPriority_t value to specify the priority for a specific thread.

Concerning the first point in the **Question 1**, the osPriorityRealtime7 priority is the highest possible priority for a normal task which is set to 55.  Therefore, the osPriorityISR priority is reserved for an Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) and should not be attributed to a normal task as defined in  [CMSIS-RTOS2 v 2.1.3](https://www.keil.com/pack/doc/CMSIS_Dev/RTOS2/html/group__CMSIS__RTOS__ThreadMgmt.html) :
> osPriorityISR: Reserved for ISR deferred thread. This highest priority might be used by the RTOS implementation but must not be used for any user thread.

About the second point, the osPriorityError is returned when the priority cannot be determined. For example, if you try to get the thread priority for an inexistent thread ID, osPriorityError is returned. According to CMSIS-RTOS2 v2.1.3, the definition of this priority a is :
> osPriorityError : priority cannot be determined or is illegal. It is also returned when the function is called from Interrupt Service Routines.

On the other hand, osPriorityReserved  is a "virtual" priority, as it is used to prevent enumerations down-size compiler optimization (i.e to keep the enumerated values encoded as a 32 bit integer). The value of this priority is set to 0x7FFFFFFF as mentioned in the [CMSIS-RTOS2 v2.1.3](https://www.keil.com/pack/doc/CMSIS_Dev/RTOS2/html/group__CMSIS__RTOS__ThreadMgmt.html).

Concerning **Question 2**,  the question is not clear, could you please give us more details? Anyway, it is fully possible to use ulTaskNotifyTake() and vTaskNotifyGiveFromISR()  to ensure that a task is notified when a hardware event takes place (rising front, fifo full, etc...). The task as defined in your question would have no issue to use this signaling mechanism.

For **Question 3**, does dvDisableInterrupts, as it name suggests, disables all interrupts? If so, using taskENTER_CRITICAL() and taskEXIT_CRITICAL() would be better.
Regardless of this, disabling the interrupts for these code sections seems a bit suspicious. There might be a possibility that getting rid of them would make the code a bit more overall unstable. It would be appreciated if you'd activate stack overflow checking as referenced in [this](https://www.freertos.org/Stacks-and-stack-overflow-checking.html) link.

I hope the links I provided you with was helpful. Unfortunately,  we don't treat these aspect related to FreeRTOS in our GitHub repositories. They are rather treated at [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos).
Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution.
issue_title: Question: ISR Triggered Task priority

title: [H7] #115: SPI and DMA is broken (no completion callback and stuck BUSY)
content:
Issue #115: SPI and DMA is broken (no completion callback and stuck BUSY)

Title: SPI and DMA is broken (no completion callback and stuck BUSY)

Body:
HAL_SPI_Transmit_DMA is broken for H7 because the callback SPI_DMATransmitCplt() in stm32h7xx_hal_spi.c doesn't really do anything unless Mode is DMA_CIRCULAR.

Specifically, it doesn't call the completion callback HAL_SPI_TxCpltCallback() and it also doesn't set the State to HAL_SPI_STATE_READY (which causes the next call to HAL_SPI_Transmit_DMA to fail).

The functions HAL_SPI_Receive_DMA() and HAL_SPI_TransmitReceive_DMA() have the same problem.

**How To Reproduce**

1. Compile test program "SPI_FullDuplex_ComDMA" for NUCLEO-H723.
2. Set a breakpoint on HAL_SPI_TxRxCpltCallback
3. Run the program
4. The breakpoint is never reached

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-02-26T15:09:56Z]
Hi @tringis,

First we would like to thank you for your contribution.

We have checked the example **SPI_FullDuplex_ComDMA** for NUCLEO-H723 and it is working correctly. The example did access the HAL_SPI_TxRxCpltCallback where LED1 and LED2 are activated to notify that transfer is completed.  

Please make sure you are correctly connecting the Master and Slave boards as mentioned in the [readme](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Projects/NUCLEO-H723ZG/Examples/SPI/SPI_FullDuplex_ComDMA/readme.txt).

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-17T09:12:16Z]
Hi @tringis,

Please allow me to close this thread. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.

Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution

With regards,

title: [H7] #114: TIM12 and 15 missing in several TIMER_x macro's.
content:
Issue #114: TIM12 and 15 missing in several TIMER_x macro's.

Title: TIM12 and 15 missing in several TIMER_x macro's.

Body:
Hello,
after debugging my timer interface I have found the following macros are missing a check for TIM12:
-  IS_TIM_MASTER_INSTANCE(), TIM12 allows master mode setting (MMS) although it only allows to drive a DMA 'channel'.
- IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_ITRX_INSTANCE(), TIM12 allows setting an internal input trigger.
- IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_TIX_INSTANCE(), TIM12 supports external clock mode 1.

Maybe there are more issues regarding TIM12 but did not check further. I can also confirm issue reported at [https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/91](url).

Potential patch to fix TIM12 checks:


--- STM32H7xxxx.orig/CMSIS_HAL/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h750xx.h        2021-02-14 12:48:21.997908200 +0800
+++ STM32H7xxxx/CMSIS_HAL/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h750xx.h     2021-02-14 13:08:38.862692100 +0800
@@ -26124,6 +26124,7 @@
                                             ((INSTANCE) == TIM6)   || \
                                             ((INSTANCE) == TIM7)   || \
                                             ((INSTANCE) == TIM8)   || \
+                                            ((INSTANCE) == TIM12)  || \
                                             ((INSTANCE) == TIM15))

 /****** TIM Instances : Salve mode available (TIMx_SMCR.TS available )*********/
@@ -26300,6 +26301,7 @@
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM4)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM5)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM8)    || \
+   ((INSTANCE) == TIM12)   || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM15)   || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM16)   || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM17))
@@ -26330,6 +26332,7 @@
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM4)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM5)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM8)    || \
+   ((INSTANCE) == TIM12)   || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM15))

 /****************** TIM Instances : supporting internal trigger inputs(ITRX) *******/
@@ -26340,6 +26343,7 @@
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM4)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM5)    || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM8)    || \
+   ((INSTANCE) == TIM12)   || \
    ((INSTANCE) == TIM15))

 /****************** TIM Instances : supporting OCxREF clear *******************/


Hope this helps.

Best Regards,
Bas

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-02-15T11:17:08Z]
Hi @basvloon,

First we would like to thank you for pointing this out.

Regarding the first macro IS_TIM_MASTER_INSTANCE(),  the issue has already been dealt and fixed internally. The fix will be part of the next release.

For the remaining macros, IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_ITRX_INSTANCE() and IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_TIX_INSTANCE(), you're absolutely right, TIM12 is missing and should be added.  This fix will be made available in the frame of a next release as well as the fix of the issue #91 which has been also confirmed . 

Thank you once again for your contribution and hope to hear from you again.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-02-15T11:17:28Z]
ST Internal Reference: 101417

[ASELSTM - 2021-02-15T11:18:35Z]
ST Internal Reference: 98873

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-16T11:14:10Z]
Related to #91.

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-16T11:17:36Z]
Hi @basvloon,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub. Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

Regarding the first macro IS_TIM_MASTER_INSTANCE(),  the issue has already been dealt and fixed internally. The fix will be part of the next release.

For the remaining macros, IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_ITRX_INSTANCE() and IS_TIM_CLOCKSOURCE_TIX_INSTANCE(), you're absolutely right, TIM12 is missing and should be added.  This fix will be made available in the frame of a next release as well as the fix of the issue #91 which has been also confirmed .
Thank you once again for your contribution and hope to hear from you again.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2021-02-15T11:17:28Z]
ST Internal Reference: 101417

[ASELSTM - 2021-02-15T11:18:35Z]
ST Internal Reference: 98873

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-16T11:14:10Z]
Related to #91.

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-16T11:17:36Z]
Hi @basvloon,
I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub. Thank you again for having reported.
issue_title: TIM12 and 15 missing in several TIMER_x macro's.

title: [H7] #113: STM32CubeIDE Default Code for Nucleo H743ZI Includes Ethernet Bug
content:
Issue #113: STM32CubeIDE Default Code for Nucleo H743ZI Includes Ethernet Bug

Title: STM32CubeIDE Default Code for Nucleo H743ZI Includes Ethernet Bug

Body:
CubeMX provides a default Ethernet configuration for Nucleo boards incorporating an unassigned NULL pointer to which the MAC address is written.  When written, this corrupts the zero wait state, deterministic tightly coupled instruction memory (ITCM) located at 0x00000000 such that anyone locating code there will face a particularly nasty bug, just when they're making the already challenging leap, attempting to load code to and run code from ITCM, which in principle doesn't need to be so hard. 

The STM32H7 series hardware is AMAZING.  The STM32CubeIDE and the low cost Nucleo boards are invaluable.  But it's a long road to getting H7 devices to deliver the performance they're capable of, like 550 Mflops from a sub $3 H730VB device, for example!  Rather than wasting nearly all that immense computational power faster than the hardware guys can serve it up, software guys should count clock cycles, look at the often ENORMOUS gaps between what they're getting and what the hardware was brilliantly engineered to achieve, and close the gaps... completely.  It takes much more than clock configuration.  To get there, you need:

- Both ICache and Dcache, understanding their impact on zero wait state performance and single cycle instruction execution as well as the overhead, risks and complications involved in keeping the regular RAM in sync with the cache. 
- Both ITCM and DTCM (tightly coupled memory), understanding their more deterministic impact on zero wait state performance and single cycle execution
- The ability to use linker scripts and startup assembly code to configure memory in a way that locates code, initialized data and uninitialized data in TCM.  (AFIK, Cube doesn't help with this.) 
- The ability to load code and data from ultra cheap 2MB-256MB external serial flash into internal SRAM, especially zero wait state tightly coupled internal SRAM.  (Executing at zero wait states from flash is nice, and ST's approaches to getting zero wait state performance from flash are superb, but if you have a lot of code and you put it all in internal flash, the cost per MB for that flash will be high.)
- The ability to move code AT RUNTIME into the RAM section of choice.
- A little bit of tight assembly language for DSP  (DSP coding with VMUL.F32 and VADD.F32 is even easier than coding in C.  Make good use of all 32 single precision floating point registers.  Make good use of the 64 bit AXI bus.  Make good use of the ability to execute loads and stores to and from TCM memory IN PARALLEL with most (but not MAC type) floating point calculations, all in a single clock cycle! 
- A little bit of tight assembly for moving data.
- A clear understanding of how to get single cycle execution of floating point instructions.  It appears that on the STM32F7 and H7, due to the nature of the pipeline that enables their incredible performance, you can't use the result of a floating point operation until THE THIRD clock cycle after the result is calculated.  This is a pretty serious constraint, but you can still get great results under many circumstances if you write your code around it, but you have to know about it first, and it's a major science project to figure out all this vital (but undocumented?) stuff.  Similarly, the cortex M7 floating point MAC instructions such as vfma.f32 and vmla.f32 CAN deliver single cycle performance, but not when loading/storing in parallel, and apparently, only when executing a sequence of instructions consisting only of MAC instructions.  Why aren't these characteristics in Arm's Cortex M7 Technical Reference Manual??  At least some of these characteristics ARE addressed for the M4 in the Cortex M4 Technical Reference Manual.  Are these factors for the M7 based devices addressed anywhere in ST documentation?  Shouldn't this information be in the programming manuals? The datasheet?  The reference manual?  If not, where should it be?  
- The ability to write assembly code in a separate file, rather than using inline assembly, avoiding all that inline messiness and chaos.
- The use of underscore underscore " __attribute__((section (".TCM_Code"), aligned(4))) " or similar specifiers before C function definitions and variable declarations to put code and data where you want it in RAM.  Similarly, the use of " .section .TCM_Code.MyASMFunction " above assembly code routines in .s assembly files to designate where in memory they should be located.  External assembly functions that are part of a project may be called from C after declaring them with " extern uint32_t  MyASMFunction(uint32_t n, float* pbuf, float a, float b, float c, float d) asm ("MyASMFunction"); ".
- Knowledge of the simple Arm EABI procedure call standards to efficiently call assembly code from C without wasted call overhead. 
- DMA! MDMA!  It's not a hassle.  It's a sublime luxury, and the H7 is DMA nirvana.  Cube is great for setting it up, but you need to be looking at the programming manual while you do it, at least the first couple of times.
- A cheap Saleae Logic probe for many reasons, including for counting cycles, because the numbers you get from the DWT cycle counter are often inaccurate.  (If it's not fundamentally impossible due to the pipeline, we need a cycle counter that provides an accurate count in CubeIDE.  Is this an Arm (Cortex) problem, an STM32 problem or a CubeIDE problem?)

Much of this approach to performance is contrary to prevailing mindsets.  Here's why the majority is wrong.  Most computationally intensive code doesn't come close to achieving a floating point operation per clock cycle.  It might be more like 20 clock cycles per floating point operation when all the application overhead is accounted for.  Writing assembly language to save a clock cycle per flop under such circumstances only speeds things up by about 5%, and most think it's not worth it.  But if you put in the effort to get it down to just two cycles per flop, the effort put into the last wasted clock cycle takes you from 275 Mflops to 550, effectively doubling the computational power of your design.  Unlike many things, in this case, the closer you get to perfect, the GREATER the rewards for improvements.  This isn't governed by the law of diminishing returns.  Clock cycles are in the denominator.  This is governed by the law of exploding returns.

If you recognize that the difference between a 200MHz device and a 400MHz device matters, you have to recognize that doubling performance "for free" by eliminating the last wasted clock cycle also matters.

But when you're starting out with one of these remarkable STM32 chips on a Nucleo board using MX and the CubeIDE, you're not really pointed in the right direction on the path to getting there, for a number of reasons, and I think they're rooted in the fact that the people developing CubeMX, the IDE, the low level drivers, the HAL and the examples aren't ever trying to make the devices do what they're capable of.  If they were, the system and process would unfold differently.  It's easy to blame it all on a bloated and buggy HAL that's nonetheless necessary and priceless as a means to timely basic functional success with a device that has 3000+ pages of documentation.  Cube provides a starting point from which a streamlined design may be developed.  But the knowledge necessary to make the H7, for example, go fast, doesn't unfold from the MX and Cube approach.  Mflops should be the top priority in some of the examples, especially for devices like the H7.  The "Blinky"s and "Hello world!"s should become "here's how we sum 64k floating point numbers in RAM in as close to one flop per max clock speed clock cycle".  Some should use the I and D caches.  Some should use TCM.  Some should use MDMA.  As fast as possible should be the default in many of the examples.  

What we have now is a bunch of newbies not getting past something akin to 16 MHz blinky with 7 wait states on ultra high performance hardware... (Okay, I'm exaggerating a bit).  Even the experts are saying you don't need assembly language, and in the rare instances that you do, inline assembly is all you'll ever need.  This is wrong.  A platform like the STM32H7 is made for the performance that comes with zero wait states and tight assembly.  In their development platforms, it's as if ST is using a Bugatti to pull a wagon on a hay ride. 

You get your fourth new Nucleo board weeks after the first Nucleo H7's become available, kind of set it up to go fast in CubeIDE, leaving the default devices on the board enabled, like Ethernet, for example, because you might need it soon, then you start to learn.  When you eventually figure out that you should make use of TCM and put code there, starting at 0x00000000 or course, everything comes crashing down, and you think it's because you really might not be smart enough to write code for an STM32H7.  Then you'll probably give up.  But if you don't, eventually you'll figure out that in its init function, the Ethernet implementation provided by MX is writing the MAC address to an unassigned NULL pointer aimed right at the code you're trying to run in ITCM.   You'll have to look at a memory dump to see what's going on.  It was a bad default configuration generated by CubeMX, one that guarantees that pretty much everyone trying to make the fast microcontroller on their Nucleo board go fast in a deterministic way will fail.  But that's the most important and valuable characteristic of the H7!  Not good for sales.  Yet years go by and nobody seems to care enough to figure it out, let alone fix it.  But they knew there was a problem.  That's why they put a warning in MX.  Perhaps Nucleo boards and CubeIDE really were  just for sales engineers who needed a snazzy way to run blinky.  No.  They're too valuable.  But how could you let this go so long?  It must be that virtually no one is making these boards go fast, whether they're customers or ST engineers.  If they were, bugs like this would have been uncovered before the board was released, or worst case, shortly afterward, and getting performance from these devices wouldn't require weeks or months of detective work and reverse engineering to understand what ST should have conveyed in a 20 page document.   This is a huge waste.  Your hardware engineers should be fuming.  Your software engineers should be dissatisfied.  Your marketing people should be livid, at least the ones who aren't *#@($)! enough to think that crippling high performance platforms enables upselling. 

There's a big problem with tech education in America.  The gap between textbook knowledge and its application is way too large.  People should be able to take a couple of DSP courses and, at home or as part of a lab, sit down with a Nucleo board and run their filters and algorithms at the speed that modern, cost effective hardware is capable of. 

The mindset should not be "Start with low performance on high performance hardware, because, for those getting started, high performance doesn't matter.  We'll save that for the elite experts who have the decades of experience to navigate our elite sophisticated product."

The mindset should be "Start with high performance on high performance hardware, because that's what were selling, that's what the customer is buying, and for those getting started (and everyone else), high performance does matter, especially to those who don't want to get left in the dust."  Extend the Cube philosophy by adding UI functionality for configuring devices appropriately and optimally for DSP, use of TCM, code location, assembly language, maximum floating point operations per second, etc.  If you did that, some of the ST software bugs that cripple new users of your hardware would never occur.

Maybe you could add a performance tab page to Cube.  On it, you could configure code location, runtime relocation with function pointers, assembly language stubs, C to assembly interfacing, alignment, linker script generation, startup file generation and whatever else is necessary to get as close as possible to one floating point operation per clock cycle out of both TCM and cache, and provide instruction timing characteristics, constraints and code examples.  Another tab page could be added for DSP, setting up biquads, basic IIR and FIR filters and matrix operations using C, assembly and CMSIS approaches.

Hope you can get the Ethernet bug fixed.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-02-15T09:46:00Z]
Hi @RJ-400,

Thank you for this post. It has been a real pleasure to read it till the last word. We do appreciate such a positive feedback about our products.

Regarding the issue you pointed out, we will forward it to our development teams for deeper analysis before having it fixed.

Regarding the other points you mentioned, a list will be forwarded to our technical committee to study the possibility to integrate such enhancements into our software offer.

Thank you again for all what you wrote. I will get back to you as soon as I have any news. In advance, thank you for your patience.

With regards,

[thomask77 - 2021-03-03T12:40:22Z]
Most likely it's the same bug that I reported a year ago:

[0-Pointer access in generated MX_ETH_Init code](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/33)

You really have to take this issue seriously and solve it in ST.
Otherwise it falls between the cracks of CubeMX and HAL Library.

[ALABSTM - 2021-03-23T03:52:07Z]
Hi @RJ-400,

I hope you are fine. Does the workaround suggested by @thomask77 solves the issue? Thank you for your reply.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2021-05-24T09:03:14Z]
Hi @RJ-400,

I hope you are fine. I also hope you could overcome the issue you were facing. Please allow me to close this issue as not reply from your side has been received since a couple of months. Do not hesitate to reopen it in case you think it is still relevant.

With regards,

[thomask77 - 2021-05-24T12:35:32Z]
@ALABSTM
Please do not auto-close such bugs before there are fixed.

Just because @RJ-400 and I lost interest in reporting it, doesn't mean the problem is solved for all other people :/

This bug and https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/33 is a serious defect that must be fixed by you.

title: [H7] #112: core_cm7.h:  loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]
content:
Issue #112: core_cm7.h:  loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]

Title: core_cm7.h:  loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]

Body:
Hi,
We are running clang-tidy check our code, and the following errors are generated: 

\STM32CubeH7\Drivers\CMSIS\Include\core_cm7.h:2468:24: error: cast from pointer to smaller type 'uint32_t' (aka 'unsigned int') loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]
    uint32_t op_addr = (uint32_t)addr;
                       ^
\STM32CubeH7\Drivers\CMSIS\Include\core_cm7.h:2495:24: error: cast from pointer to smaller type 'uint32_t' (aka 'unsigned int') loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]
    uint32_t op_addr = (uint32_t) addr;
                       ^
\STM32CubeH7\Drivers\CMSIS\Include\core_cm7.h:2522:24: error: cast from pointer to smaller type 'uint32_t' (aka 'unsigned int') loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]
    uint32_t op_addr = (uint32_t) addr;

This error aborts the clang-tidy check.  

If I modify the code like below, the compilation is ok:
 **uint32_t op_addr = * addr;**

Thank you for the help!
Attila Lantos

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2021-02-10T10:29:04Z]
Hi @lantiati,

Thank you for your contribution. In order to allow a better analysis of this problem, could you please give us more details about the application that you are using to reproduce this issue, so that our development teams can give you an answer.
Could you please confirm you are using the latest firmware version and that the issue is systematically reproducible on your side?

Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[lantiati - 2021-02-10T10:47:53Z]
We are using cmake, and it checks out the master branch from this repo. So the version is the latest one.

[RKOUSTM - 2021-02-10T17:33:24Z]
Hi @lantiati,

Thank you for this report. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to core_cm7 in our GitHub repositories. As the [CMSIS Core](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/cmsis_core/tree/master/Core/Include) files. These files come from Arm and we use them as is. So, these files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics what about reporting this issue to the [ARM](https://github.com/ARM-software) GitHub?

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

As the [CMSIS Core](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/cmsis_core/tree/master/Core/Include) files. These files come from Arm and we use them as is. So, these files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics what about reporting this issue to the [ARM](https://github.com/ARM-software) GitHub?
Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution.
issue_title: core_cm7.h:  loses information [clang-diagnostic-error]

title: [H7] #111: Impact of using TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG with macro __LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE  on macro __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE and the output Temperature
content:
Issue #111: Impact of using TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG with macro __LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE  on macro __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE and the output Temperature

Title: Impact of using TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG with macro __LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE  on macro __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE and the output Temperature

Body:
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

I analyzed your response to #72 and observed that your response is correct only when user uses the below code:
__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE(TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG, g_u32ReadTemperatureDigitalValue, ADC_RESOLUTION_16B)

However, the response is incorrect if TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG is replaced with actual VREF value read via the VREF analog input channel value read using the ADC.

On my hardware the read VREF value is 3275 which corresponds to 3.275V (and not 3300 i.e. TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG which would correspond to 3.3V)

I request you to review the macro __LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE declaration. Is it possible for you to remove the dependency of the macro on TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG and __VREFANALOG_VOLTAGE__?

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2021-02-10T06:59:41Z]
I am comparing result / output of __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE with result of _HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMS(2000, 620, 30, u16VRefMilliVoltsValue, g_u32ReadTemperatureDigitalValue, ADC_RESOLUTION_16B));


Output of both:

__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE(u16VRefMilliVoltsValue, g_u32ReadTemperatureDigitalValue, ADC_RESOLUTION_16B)
__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE(TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG, g_u32ReadTemperatureDigitalValue, ADC_RESOLUTION_16B)

are compared with output of __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMSoutputs()

__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMSoutputs() outputs Celsius Temperature value of 46.

Variable values and register values shared below for reference:
u16VRefMilliVoltsValue = 3275
g_u32ReadTemperatureDigitalValue = 13068
TS_CAL2 = 16234
TS_CAL1 = 12323

[RKOUSTM - 2021-02-10T07:57:15Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this contribution. Please let me introduce @ASELSTM, which has already reviewed your change to #72.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-02-19T17:18:34Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Glad to read from you again.

To answer your query, it is unfortunately impossible to remove the dependency of the macro on TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG which corresponds to the  Analog voltage reference (Vref+) voltage with which temperature sensor has been calibrated in production and __VREFANALOG_VOLTAGE__.  

Let me explain it in more detail,  as mentioned in the issue #72  the formula used to compute the temperature value within the __LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE is as following : 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47858836/108529790-23d9aa00-72d5-11eb-9a18-d42506fe4bcc.png)

My focus will be more on this part of the formula : 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47858836/108535390-93529800-72db-11eb-97a6-333c7b578538.png)

In the macro, the TS_DATA is obtained as following : 


((__LL_ADC_CONVERT_DATA_RESOLUTION((__TEMPSENSOR_ADC_DATA__),     \
                                                    (__ADC_RESOLUTION__),          \
                                                    LL_ADC_RESOLUTION_16B)         \
                   * (__VREFANALOG_VOLTAGE__))                                     \
                  / TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG) 


The ratio between  __VREFANALOG_VOLTAGE__ and TEMPSENSOR_CAL_VREFANALOG is essential to align the ADC conversion scale. 
The Analog reference voltage (Vref+) when calibrating the temperature sensor is 3.3V for STM32H7 case which is different from the the Analog reference voltage used in the application, **3.275V** in your case. 
The ADC conversion scales have a discrepancy of 0.8%, they have then to be aligned before comparing the ADC data with TS_CAL1.

Hopefully this would help you.

With regards

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-12T16:06:58Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Please allow me to close this thread as no activity has been registered since mostly a month. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.

Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution

With regards,

title: [H7] #108: Git Repository should be split up into submodules
content:
Issue #108: Git Repository should be split up into submodules

Title: Git Repository should be split up into submodules

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
I am experiencing long clone times due to all dependencies being copied in directly instead of using submodules

**Describe the bug**
lwIP, FreeRTOS and CMSIS are copied in directly instead of referencing other git repositories. The examples should be contained in another module instead of with all the drivers.

**How To Reproduce**
Clone the project using Git

Comments:
[CCASTM - 2021-02-08T07:55:38Z]
Hello WBAD

The first intent of the GitHub publication (in April 2019) was to propose an alternative to the zip format proposed in st.com servers.
You can find then all FW Packages series under configuration management. This is a first added value to manage the successive versions from the customer point of view, an benefit from the Issues and Pull Request features.
Since December 2019, you can find also the first components published individually (CMSIS Core and Device, HAL Drivers, a first set of MW, X-CUBE ). 
Please take a look to the repo  https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32Cube_MCU_Overall_Offer   to get a description of all STM32 git repos
Regards
Christophe

[CCASTM - 2021-02-08T08:19:48Z]
Just to complete my previous comments.
The sub-modules feature was not selected to publish the FW Packages as the full packages exist as is in our internal configuration management.
In the future, we have in mind to use git sub-repo to publish specific application projects
Regards
Christophe

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-05T09:26:54Z]
Hi @WBABD,

As explained by @CCASTM, not using submodules for Cube repos is a choice. On the other hand, choice has also been made to use them for other repos (a handful so far):
* [stm32u5-classic-coremw-apps](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32u5-classic-coremw-apps)
* [stm32l5-openbl-apps](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32l5-openbl-apps)
* [stm32wl-openbl-apps](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32wl-openbl-apps)

Regarding the time it may take to clone the full firmware published within this repo, please note we also published the different components (**i.e.**, CMSIS, HAL-LL, BSP, etc.) **separately** in dedicated repos.

As pointed out by @CCASTM, the complete list can be found in the [STM32Cube_MCU_Overall_Offer](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32Cube_MCU_Overall_Offer) repo, for instance, the [list](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32Cube_MCU_Overall_Offer/blob/master/README.md#stm32cube-hal-drivers) of all HAL-LL drivers published separately in dedicated repos.

Thank you again for having tackled the subject. Please allow me to close this thread.

With regards,

title: [H7] #107: Incorrect comments on SDMMC_GetCmdResp7
paragraphs: Issue #107: Incorrect comments on SDMMC_GetCmdResp7
Title: Incorrect comments on SDMMC_GetCmdResp7

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus)

**Describe the set-up**
 * Nucleo-H753ZI
 * STM32CubeIDE
 * H7 HAL 1.8.0

**Additional context**
This is a question on the comments on the SDMMC_GetCmdResp7 function. The first two /* Card is SD V2.0 compliant */ should be /* Card is not SD V2.0 compliant */ instead? When SDMMC_FLAG_CTIMEOUT and SDMMC_FLAG_CCRCFAIL flags are set.
return SDMMC_ERROR_NONE; } **Screenshots**

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-02-04T11:05:32Z]
Hi @C47D,
Thank you for this report. It has been forwarded to our development teams. I will get to you as soon as they provide their feedback.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2021-02-26T14:47:09Z]
Hi @C47D,
The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you once again for your contribution.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2021-02-26T14:47:29Z]
ST Internal Reference: 102186

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub as you can see below. Thank you again for having reported.
 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_sdmmc.c#L1570-L1571
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_sdmmc.c#L1578-L1579

title: [H7] #106: Export Ioc
content:
Issue #106: Export Ioc

Title: Export Ioc

Body:
Currently it is a bit difficult to modify the examples as there is no ioc file and one can not open them up on CubeMx to explore , these examples would be much more useful if you exported an ioc file with them.
That is basically a feature request and not exactly an issue , do you have any thoughts about that ?

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-02-01T08:29:00Z]
Related to [STM32CubeL4#21](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeL4/issues/21).

[ALABSTM - 2021-02-01T08:31:45Z]
Hi @DrunkenMastah,

 IOC files are published within [this](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32_open_pin_data) repository. Hopefully you will find the one(s) that would address your needs under the **./boards** directory.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2021-02-08T12:28:33Z]
Hi @DrunkenMastah,

Any feedback after my last comment? Could you find what you are looking for?

Thank you,

Yes thank you very much ,I still believe though that a lot of useful examples (e.g. for the Discovery board) are pretty much locked for everyone using a simple gcc toolchain.

title: [H7] #105: HAL_TIMEOUT in USB_CoreReset() due to missing volatile for count variable
content:
Issue #105: HAL_TIMEOUT in USB_CoreReset() due to missing volatile for count variable

Title: HAL_TIMEOUT in USB_CoreReset() due to missing volatile for count variable

Body:
Please consider making this variable volatile: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1298 and in all relevant places for other micro controllers.

In my custom board I have spent quite some time figuring out why USB_CoreReset() failed with timeout. It turned out to be a compiler optimization. Adding volatile for the loop variable resolved my issue.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-04-20T11:37:50Z]
Hi @adel-mamin,

Thank you for reporting. You described an issue with the USB_CoreReset() function (the prototype of which is shown below) and mentioned a *loop variable*.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1326

However, at line 1298 you indicate, there is a local variable gSNPSiD declared within function USB_EP0_OutStart(). Besides, this function contains no loop.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1293

Would you please clarify how setting gSNPSiD volatile in USB_EP0_OutStart() would solve the issue you experienced with USB_CoreReset()? Or maybe is there simply a typo somewhere in your comment? Thank you for the clarification.

With regards,

[adel-mamin - 2021-04-20T11:46:09Z]
> Would you please clarify how setting gSNPSiD volatile in USB_EP0_OutStart() would solve the issue you experienced with USB_CoreReset()? Or maybe is there simply a typo somewhere in your comment? Thank you for the clarification.

My bad. Apparently I pasted the link to a wrong place in the source code.
Here is the correct one:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1328

Declaring count variable as volatile fixed my issue. As I mentioned there are other places in the source code, where it could be needed. For example:

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L461

etc.

[ALABSTM - 2021-04-26T15:56:24Z]
Hello @adel-mamin,

Would you mind precising the IDE and the compiler you are using and their versions please?

Thank you,

[adel-mamin - 2021-04-26T16:12:18Z]
> Hello @adel-mamin,
> 
> Would you mind precising the IDE and the compiler you are using and their versions please?
> 
> Thank you,

\> arm-none-eabi-gcc --version
arm-none-eabi-gcc (Arch Repository) 10.3.0
Copyright (C) 2020 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
This is free software; see the source for copying conditions.  There is NO
warranty; not even for MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

[ALABSTM - 2021-04-28T12:20:35Z]
Hi @adel-mamin,

We used Cube IDE **v1.6.0** with C/C++ GCC Cross Compiler Support **9.11.1**.202004012021 to build the [USB_Device/CDC_Standalone](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/tree/master/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/USB_Device/CDC_Standalone) application with a high optimization level and **found no issue** at run-time. As hardware, we used a STM32H743I-EVAL board.

We also noticed the same use of a count local variable in a do-while loop elsewhere in the code. Actually, there are **5** functions this way implemented within the stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c file (listed below).

If the root-cause were the one you pointed out, you should also have had issues with all the below-listed functions. Should you not?

Would you mind checking again from your side. This reply may help you considering the issue from another point-of-view.

With regards,

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L437
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L459
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1326
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1864
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1961

[adel-mamin - 2021-04-28T13:01:36Z]
> If the root-cause were the one you pointed out, you should also have had issues with all the below-listed functions. Should you not?

Yes, in my project I have added volatile to all five places you pointed out.
FYI, I've been using a custom board based on STM32H747IIT6 SoC. 

Also here are the relevant command line parameters of my gcc invocation for that file:


/usr/sbin/arm-none-eabi-gcc -fdiagnostics-color=always -pipe -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -Wall -Winvalid-pch -O3 -g -Wpedantic -pedantic-errors -Wall -Wextra -Wchar-subscripts -Wformat -Wformat=2 -Wformat-nonliteral -Wformat-security -Wformat-y2k -Winit-self -Wmain -Wmissing-include-dirs -Wswitch-default -Wswitch-enum -Wunused -Wunused-parameter -Wunused-variable -Wuninitialized -Wunknown-pragmas -Wstrict-overflow=5 -Wmissing-format-attribute -Wfloat-equal -Wshadow -Wpointer-arith -Wundef -Wunused-macros -Wcast-qual -Wcast-align -Wwrite-strings -Wconversion -Wdangling-else -Wdate-time -Wmissing-declarations -Wpacked -Wredundant-decls -Winline -Wdisabled-optimization -Werror -Winvalid-pch -Wmissing-noreturn -Wunreachable-code -Wimplicit -Wstrict-prototypes -Wold-style-definition -Wmissing-prototypes -Wnested-externs -Wnull-dereference -fno-strict-aliasing -fno-common -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -fno-builtin -fshort-enums -fno-asynchronous-unwind-tables -ftrapv -mthumb -mabi=aapcs -Wdouble-promotion -mcpu=cortex-m7 -mno-unaligned-access -mfloat-abi=hard -ggdb -mfpu=fpv5-sp-d16 -DUSE_HAL_DRIVER -DSTM32H747xx -DUSE_FULL_LL_DRIVER -DCORE_CM7 -MD -MQ ... STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c

[radioflash - 2021-07-14T15:17:06Z]
Making the count variable volatile should not be necessary, because the loop condition itself is volatile, so the compiler should NEVER be allowed to return timeout after a single check.

@adel-mamin Maybe the __IO macro (expected to be #define __IO volatile) is empty/broken for some reason in your build? This macro would normally qualify the loop variable as volatile, defective definitions for it would explain why you need the volatile count instead.

[atsju - 2021-08-18T13:33:34Z]
I confirm what @radioflash explaines. The outside loop condition should be volatile and this removes the need for a volatile count variable.

There might be a problem and it might be "solved" with volatile but it's not the root cause.

[ALABSTM - 2022-01-12T10:46:13Z]

I hope you are doing well. We were not able to reproduce the problem you described. Please allow me to close this issue. Thank you for your comprehension.

@radioflash and @atsju thank you very much for your explanation and proposal.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-12T15:41:42Z]
Hi @adel-mamin,

I hope you are doing well. Back to you about this point you reported. Actually a fix has been made available in the frame of version **1.9.1** of this firmware, which is the same as the one you suggested: adding the _IO qualifier (an alias for volatile), as you can see in code snippet below.

With regards,

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e9472e471cc8974c4a2596fc86e565241871c20e/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1425-L1427
paragraphs: Issue #105: HAL_TIMEOUT in USB_CoreReset() due to missing volatile for count variable
Title: HAL_TIMEOUT in USB_CoreReset() due to missing volatile for count variable

Body:
Please consider making this variable volatile: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1298 and in all relevant places for other micro controllers.

In my custom board I have spent quite some time figuring out why USB_CoreReset() failed with timeout. It turned out to be a compiler optimization. Adding volatile for the loop variable resolved my issue.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-04-20T11:37:50Z]
Hi @adel-mamin,

Thank you for reporting. You described an issue with the USB_CoreReset() function (the prototype of which is shown below) and mentioned a *loop variable*.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1326

However, at line 1298 you indicate, there is a local variable gSNPSiD declared within function USB_EP0_OutStart(). Besides, this function contains no loop.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/5975bffae9358bc2b2890a35a203d940a395efef/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_usb.c#L1293

Would you please clarify how setting gSNPSiD volatile in USB_EP0_OutStart() would solve the issue you experienced with USB_CoreReset()? Or maybe is there simply a typo somewhere in your comment? Thank you for the clarification.

> Would you please clarify how setting gSNPSiD volatile in USB_EP0_OutStart() would solve the issue you experienced with USB_CoreReset()? Or maybe is there simply a typo somewhere in your comment? Thank you for the clarification.

Making the count variable volatile should not be necessary, because the loop condition itself is volatile, so the compiler should NEVER be allowed to return timeout after a single check.

title: [H7] #104: Ethernet error interrupt
content:
Issue #104: Ethernet error interrupt

Title: Ethernet error interrupt

Body:
Hello!
Into stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c used macro __HAL_ETH_MAC_GET_IT:

/* ETH MAC Error IT */
  if(__HAL_ETH_MAC_GET_IT(heth, (ETH_MACIER_RXSTSIE | ETH_MACIER_TXSTSIE)))
  {
.....
}

But, macro code is 
(((__HANDLE__)->Instance->MACISR &( __INTERRUPT__)) == ( __INTERRUPT__)) .

Need two active interrupt flags simultaneously to call error handler.

With best regards.

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2021-01-18T14:52:20Z]
Hi @ashchepkov,

Allow me first to thank you for your contribution. Your report has been forwarded to our development team. We will get back to you as soon as we have more details.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-10T10:22:27Z]
Hi @ashchepkov,

We just made available a fully reworked ETH HAL driver via pull-request [stm32h7xx_hal_driver#16](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/pull/16). You can use this new driver for your applications. Please note that this new ETH HAL driver **breaks the compatibility** with the previous one.

Regarding the point you raised, it has been addressed as you will notice.  Please feel free to provide your feedback [here](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/stm32h7xx_hal_driver/pull/16) once you have performed your tests. 

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2022-04-05T09:37:53Z]
Hi @ashchepkov,

The new ETH HAL driver has been made officially available in the frame of **v1.10.0** of the STM32CubeH7 firmware. Please allow me to close this issue.

With regards,

Need two active interrupt flags simultaneously to call error handler.

title: [H7] #103: Issue Entering DFU Mode after User code Sets ClockSource for SPI, Uarts, OR i2c
paragraphs: Issue #103: Issue Entering DFU Mode after User code Sets ClockSource for SPI, Uarts, OR i2c
Title: Issue Entering DFU Mode after User code Sets ClockSource for SPI, Uarts, OR i2c

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 Custom H750 Based Board

**Describe the bug**
H7 Wont enter DFU mode after Clock source has been set for Communication Peripherals

**How To Reproduce**
Init Clock Sources for various Peripherals
c 
RCC_PeriphCLKInitTypeDef rccPeriphStruct;
rccPeriphStruct.UsbClockSelection         = RCC_USBCLKSOURCE_HSI48;

rccPeriphStruct.QspiClockSelection        = RCC_QSPICLKSOURCE_PLL2; 
  rccPeriphStruct.SdmmcClockSelection       = RCC_SDMMCCLKSOURCE_PLL2;

rccPeriphStruct.Spi123ClockSelection      = RCC_SPI123CLKSOURCE_PLL;
  rccPeriphStruct.Spi45ClockSelection       = RCC_SPI45CLKSOURCE_PLL2;

rccPeriphStruct.Usart234578ClockSelection = RCC_USART234578CLKSOURCE_PLL2;
  rccPeriphStruct.Usart16ClockSelection     = RCC_USART16CLKSOURCE_PLL2;
rccPeriphStruct.AdcClockSelection         = RCC_ADCCLKSOURCE_CLKP;

rccPeriphStruct.I2c123ClockSelection      = RCC_I2C123CLKSOURCE_PLL3;
  rccPeriphStruct.I2c4ClockSelection       = RCC_I2C4CLKSOURCE_PLL3;
HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig(&rccPeriphStruct);

Set unique number in backup ram for startup code to look for
Call HAL_RCC_DeInit and NVIC_SystemReset
Startup Code checks for special code in ram and jumps to System Bootloader Location
FC is running in system bootloader but does not enumerate as USB device (Verified by pausing and checking current running location with debugger)

**Additional context**
I was able to fix this issue by setting the clock selections of any peripheral i changed back to their default value before resetting, This seems like something HAL_RCC_DeInit should do. Seems like this issue is that the sources are not set how the Bootloader expects and is getting false readings on the peripherals causing it to run a different routine than the USB one

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2021-02-09T12:32:37Z]
Hi @PGIII,

Thank you for your contribution. In order to allow a better analysis of this problem, could you please give us more details about the application that you are using to reproduce this issue, so that our development teams can give you an answer.
Could you please confirm you are using the latest firmware version and that the issue is systematically reproducible on your side?
Thank you again for your contribution. With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2021-03-12T16:32:42Z]
Hi @PGIII,
Please allow me to close this thread as no activity has been registered since mostly a month. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution

title: [H7] #102: Use Boot -> APP： HAL_ Delay() will be stuck in APP, But it is normal in Boot
paragraphs: Issue #102: Use Boot -> APP： HAL_ Delay() will be stuck in APP, But it is normal in Boot
Title: Use Boot -> APP： HAL_ Delay() will be stuck in APP, But it is normal in Boot

Body:
/ * Issue Description */ 
Boot -> APP： HAL_ Delay() will be stuck in APP, But it is normal in Boot.
In addition, it is normal （LL_mDelay()）to use LL Library in version 1.7.0 Pakeage 。

**Tools**
MCU:  STM32H743II
IDE:     KEIL 5.32
ARM Compiler: V6.14.1
MCU Pakeage for STM32H7 Series: 1.8.0
__set_MSP(*(__IO uint32_t *)APPLICATION_ADDRESS); JumpToApplication();

}
} /* BOOT  & APP Code*/ #define g_JumpInit *(uint32_t *)0x20000000

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-15T11:20:54Z]
Hi @fyyxxm,

Thank you for you contribution. In order to allow a better analysis of this problem, could you please give us more details about the project or the application that you are used to reproduce this issue, so that our development teams can give you an answer. Thank you again for your contribution.

[fyyxxm - 2020-12-16T02:34:28Z]
Template Project  : https://github.com/fyyxxm/BootAndApp.git
In application, if you initialize UART, HAL_Delay() will get stuck. But in BOOT, there is no problem.
In fact, HAL_Delay was the only one with a problem, and everything else was normal

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-17T16:11:48Z]
Hi @fyyxxm,

Thank you for your contribution. I cloned your [template project](https://github.com/fyyxxm/BootAndApp/tree/master/STM32H7xx_Driver) and modified it to correctly compiled and supported ARM V6, as follows :
1. Modification in the the target options is proposed :

- Optimization: -O3,
    - Language C: c11,
    - RAM for Algorithm: 
              - Size: 0x1000
2. Update the [stm32h7xx_hal_def.h](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_def.h#L110) file as follow:

patch
@@ -110,8 +110,8 @@ 
- #if  defined ( __GNUC__ )
+ #if defined (__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050) /* ARM Compiler V6 */
+   #ifndef __weak
+      #define __weak  __attribute__((weak))
+   #endif
+   #ifndef __packed
+      #define __packed  __attribute__((packed))
+   #endif
+ #elif defined ( __GNUC__ ) && !defined (__CC_ARM) /* GNU Compiler */

patch
@@ -121,8 +121,8 @@ /* Macro to get variable aligned on 4-bytes, for __ICCARM__ the directive "#pragma data_alignment=4" must be used instead */
- #if defined   (__GNUC__)        /* GNU Compiler */
+ #if defined (__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050) /* ARM Compiler V6 */
+   #ifndef __ALIGN_BEGIN
+     #define __ALIGN_BEGIN
+   #endif
+   #ifndef __ALIGN_END
+     #define __ALIGN_END      __attribute__ ((aligned (4)))
+   #endif
+ #elif defined ( __GNUC__ ) && !defined (__CC_ARM) /* GNU Compiler */

patch
@@ -181,16 +181,16 @@ 
+/**
+ * @brief  __NOINLINE definition
+ */
+ #if defined ( __CC_ARM   ) || (defined (__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050)) || defined   (  __GNUC__  )
+ /* ARM V4/V5 and V6 & GNU Compiler
+  -------------------------------
+ */
+ #define __NOINLINE __attribute__ ( (noinline) )

+ #elif defined ( __ICCARM__ )
+ /* ICCARM Compiler
+   ---------------
+ */
+ #define __NOINLINE _Pragma("optimize = no_inline")
After this proposed modification, there is no problem when initializing the UART by calling the BSP_Usart1Init() function in main_app.c file.
Please let us know your feedback so that we can help you. Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards, [fyyxxm - 2020-12-18T01:06:03Z]
Thanks

[fyyxxm - 2020-12-18T02:22:13Z]
After my test, there are still some problems with the modified project.
HAL_Delay() in main_app.c  is normal only if UART is initialized in main_boot.c.
If UART is not initialized in main_boot.c, HAL_Delay() will still be stuck in main_app.c.
It already does not affect the use for me, but it's still a problem that shouldn't exist

[minh7a6 - 2020-12-19T21:38:42Z]
What I'm seeing is that you have a BSP_Disable_IRQ() in your boot code, which in your comment is to disable the Systick Interrupt. HAL relies on this interrupt to do the HAL_Delay() while LL_mDelay() use the cnt of the Systick. I think the solution is to enable the irq in your main code or not disable the irq in the boot code.

[fyyxxm - 2020-12-20T03:22:30Z]
> What I'm seeing is that you have a BSP_Disable_IRQ() in your boot code, which in your comment is to disable the Systick Interrupt. HAL relies on this interrupt to do the HAL_Delay() while LL_mDelay() use the cnt of the Systick. I think the solution is to enable the irq in your main code or not disable the irq in the boot code.
Thank you for your answer. I have tried this solution before, but I still haven't solved the problem.
Thank you for your contribution. Please find attached an updated version for your template project.
[BootAndApp_Issue_102.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5726157/BootAndApp_Issue_102.zip)
I hope this fix addresses your need. Thank you for your feedback. With regards,
issue_title: Use Boot -> APP： HAL_ Delay() will be stuck in APP, But it is normal in Boot

title: [H7] #101: RCC_AHB2RSTR register for DCMI resetting on STM32H743VI
content:
Issue #101: RCC_AHB2RSTR register for DCMI resetting on STM32H743VI

Title: RCC_AHB2RSTR register for DCMI resetting on STM32H743VI

Body:
**Caution**
This not a bug report, I'm just trying to understand. 

**Describe the set-up**
 * The board : Custom board based on the STM32h743VI MCU.
 * Writing device tree while trying to port this custom board on Zephyr

**Additional context**
Hello, In the Application Note of the DCMI interface, it is said that It is recommended to reset the DCMI peripheral and the camera module before starting the configuration. The DCMI can be reset by setting the corresponding bit in the RCC_AHB2RSTR register, which resets the clock domains. Thought, I can't find the corresponding bit in the RCC_AHB2RSTR of the STM32H743VI MCU. Is it normal ?

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-08T15:26:42Z]
Hi @othmaneAZ,

Thank you for your contribution. According to [RM0433](https://www.st.com/content/ccc/resource/technical/document/reference_manual/group0/c9/a3/76/fa/55/46/45/fa/DM00314099/files/DM00314099.pdf/jcr:content/translations/en.DM00314099.pdf), the digital camera interface (DCMI) can be reset by setting the camera interface bit (Bit 0 CAMITFRST) in the RCC_AHB2RSTR register. Indeed, an update in the reference manual will be proposed to rename the CAMITFRST bit by the DCMIRST and will be available soon.

Thank you again for your contribution. 

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-08T15:27:15Z]
ST Internal Reference: 97861

[othmaneAZ - 2020-12-08T15:50:26Z]
Thank you for your answer, this update will for sure make things clearer. 

I have another question aside though, my custom board contains an OV77xx camera sensor and I'm trying to define it in my device tree as a node, but I don't know where to find the address of this device. I appreciate your help.

Best regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-09T13:43:28Z]
Hi @othmaneAZ,

Thank you for your contribution. You can adapt [this](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/tree/master/Projects/STM32H747I-DISCO/Examples/BSP) BSP example on STM32H747I-DISCO to drive the camera module  ov77xx for any other development board as STM32H734VI device. 

Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2022-02-23T11:28:17Z]
Hi @othmaneAZ,

In order to change all references from CAMITF to DCMI, a new version of RM0399 and RM0433 will be available as soon as possible on st.com site.

Now, as this issue is not directly related to some software component published within this repository (CMSIS, HAL, BSP, etc.) but rather to our ecosystem document, please allow me to close it.

Thank you for your contribution. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

title: [H7] #100: CMSIS_OS2.C: Call to enable critical section missing before a call to any Function that ends with FromISR.
content:
Issue #100: CMSIS_OS2.C: Call to enable critical section missing before a call to any Function that ends with FromISR.

Title: CMSIS_OS2.C: Call to enable critical section missing before a call to any Function that ends with FromISR.

Body:
Hello,

I am observing that the **cmsis_os2.c** file does not call taskENTER_CRITICAL() before it executes any XXXFomISR() function and also does not execute taskEXIT_CRITICAL() after function XXXFomISR() gets executed.

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1099
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1121
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1142
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1562
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1667
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1708
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS_V2/cmsis_os2.c#L1769

I assume the call to critical section enable and disable should be made when executing a function that acts upon queue, semaphore, or time, irrespective of whether the function is called from within a task (like xQueueReceive()) or from within an ISR (like xQueueReceiveFromISR()).

Please share your views.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2020-12-03T14:01:19Z]
Please review: Light weight example 3 – introducing preemption and interrupt safety

Available at location https://www.freertos.org/message_passing_performance.html

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-09T11:29:57Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. It will be forwarded to our development teams for analysis. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer.

Thank you for your patience and thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2021-03-23T05:22:48Z]
Hello @ALABSTM ,

Are there any findings / updates that you may be able to share with me.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[RKOUSTM - 2021-08-04T09:38:43Z]
Hi @rxa1031, 

I hope you are fine. Our development teams said your proposal could not be taken into consideration for the following reason:

> The xxxFromISR() functions are meant to be used exclusively within an interrupt context. Their use outside the interrupt context is not advised . But if a user decides to use them outside interrupt context, he has obviously to make the choice of protecting or not through critical sections. On the CMSIS_OS side, we cannot implement a change that is not generic and related to a non-recommended use case.

 Please allow me to close this issue now and thank you for your comprehension. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

title: [H7] #99: LL_USART_ClockInit issue.
content:
Issue #99: LL_USART_ClockInit issue.

Title: LL_USART_ClockInit issue.

Body:
Hello,

According to the 'slave' functionality of the USART IP you can set the clock phase and polarity, which I would expect. In LL_USART_ClockInit there is no implementation to set the clock phase and polarity in case CLK_EN is off, which is should be done and is valid valid in case the IP is used in slave mode. At least according to the RM.

Quote from RM: When the peripheral is used in SPI slave mode, the frequency of peripheral clock source
(usart_ker_ck_pres) must be greater than 3 times the CK input frequency.
The CPOL bit and the CPHA bit in the USART_CR2 register are used to select the clock
polarity and the phase of the external clock, respectively (see Figure 583).
An underrun error flag is available in slave transmission mode. This flag is set when the first
clock pulse for data transmission appears while the software has not yet loaded any value to
USART_TDR.
The slave supports the hardware and software NSS management.

Am I wrong or is this not correctly implemented ?

Thanks for the feedback.

Best Regards,
Bas

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-12-10T16:20:46Z]
Hi @basvloon,

First we would like to thank you for your contribution. 

No, you are not wrong. Indeed, the actual implementation of the LL_USART_ClockInit() is incorrect and won't allow the clock phase and the clock polarity configuration when CR2_CLKEN is cleared. 

A fix will be implemented  and will made available in the frame of a next release. 

Best regards,

[ASELSTM - 2020-12-10T16:21:06Z]
ST Internal Reference: 98043

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-09T13:55:43Z]
Hi @basvloon,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.

Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

According to the 'slave' functionality of the USART IP you can set the clock phase and polarity, which I would expect. In LL_USART_ClockInit there is no implementation to set the clock phase and polarity in case CLK_EN is off, which is should be done and is valid valid in case the IP is used in slave mode. At least according to the RM.

Quote from RM: When the peripheral is used in SPI slave mode, the frequency of peripheral clock source
(usart_ker_ck_pres) must be greater than 3 times the CK input frequency.
The CPOL bit and the CPHA bit in the USART_CR2 register are used to select the clock
polarity and the phase of the external clock, respectively (see Figure 583).
An underrun error flag is available in slave transmission mode. This flag is set when the first
clock pulse for data transmission appears while the software has not yet loaded any value to
USART_TDR.
The slave supports the hardware and software NSS management.
Am I wrong or is this not correctly implemented ? Thanks for the feedback.

Best Regards,
Bas Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-12-10T16:20:46Z]

First we would like to thank you for your contribution.
No, you are not wrong. Indeed, the actual implementation of the LL_USART_ClockInit() is incorrect and won't allow the clock phase and the clock polarity configuration when CR2_CLKEN is cleared.
A fix will be implemented  and will made available in the frame of a next release.

Best regards, [ASELSTM - 2020-12-10T16:21:06Z]
ST Internal Reference: 98043

[ASELSTM - 2021-03-09T13:55:43Z]

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.
Thank you again for having reported. With regards,

title: [H7] #96: No means to detect ASSERT trigger without a debugger for configASSERT(0)
content:
Issue #96: No means to detect ASSERT trigger without a debugger for configASSERT(0)

Title: No means to detect ASSERT trigger without a debugger for configASSERT(0)

Body:
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

Please refer to below line of code: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/include/FreeRTOSConfig_template.h#L149

If configASSERT(0) gets executed, then without a debugger it is impossible to know why code got stuck.

May you please add a weak function to the statement, which can later be coded by the user.

Is it possible to change the line of code to:

#define configASSERT( x ) if ((x) == 0) {taskDISABLE_INTERRUPTS(); rvUserAssertInformFunction(); for( ;; );}

Please share your views.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-01T13:23:45Z]
Hi @rxa1031,
 
Thank you for this report. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to FreeRTOS in our GitHub repositories. As the Third_Party files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics, any modification concerning these files is not implemented by our technical team. What about reporting this issue to the [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos)?  
 
Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution. 

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-12-01T13:35:51Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

The file STM32CubeH7/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/include/FreeRTOSConfig_template.h is provided in STM32CubeH7 Repository, and hence I raised the concern here.

Also the file **FreeRTOSConfig.h** is available with many examples which are being provided within the STM32CubeH7 repository. example:

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Projects/STM32H750B-DK/Demonstrations/STemWin/Config/FreeRTOSConfig.h#L164

Another example:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Demonstrations/STemWin/Config/FreeRTOSConfig.h#L121

Hence, I have raised the concern. Also it was earlier informed that ST uses customized version of FreeRTOS. I believe that the issue I am reporting must be addressed by STM32CubeH7 developers.

Regards,
Rajeev

[rxa1031 - 2020-12-01T13:40:59Z]
Please review **STM32CubeH7** provided various examples that include **FreeRTOSConfig.h** file. Please check the declaration of macro **configASSERT( x )** in above informed file(s).

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-01T16:44:57Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

ST proposes the usage of FreeRTOS in the context of the STM32Cube ecosystem. Any problem related to Third_Party files itself, we suggest to contact FreeRTOS supplier as any modification or update in these files is not implemented by our technical team. 

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your report.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-12-01T21:25:44Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM , @ALABSTM 

Thanks for the information. I assume you mean to inform that the **STM32CubeH7** community / github page takes core of the **STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series** only.

I am not aware which ST community takes care of integrating the FreeRTOS. Some ST community modified the FreeRTOS code when porting it for ST Microcontrollers. Example: Addition of enum osPriority_t which contains the value osPriorityISR. The enum is now used even by STM32CubeH7 repository.

May you please help me with contact (preferably email) or @ for the community who is involved with integrating the RTOS.

To me it appears that @ALABSTM added the file FreeRTOSConfig.h to the STM32CubeH7 repository.

I am not sure how the process works at ST, and which Enhancements (and bugs) to be reported to STM32CubeH7 community and which of them to be reported to STM32CubeMX community,

I plan to report this issue on STM32CubeMX ST community web page, probably they can help add the requested implementation.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-07T21:05:11Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report, once again. However, this issue is out of the scope of STMicroelectronics' activity as the concerned piece of software is a third party's one. As mentioned in a previous comment, aspects related to FreeRTOS are not handled in our GitHub repositories. They are rather treated at [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos). 

Indeed, all aspects related to Cube MX tool are not handled at GitHub level. They are rather treated at ST Community level, precisely within the [Cube MX](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx) dedicated section.

Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #95: STM32H7: Ethernet TX callback complete not called intermittently
content:
Issue #95: STM32H7: Ethernet TX callback complete not called intermittently

Title: STM32H7: Ethernet TX callback complete not called intermittently

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus) 

**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H747I DISCO
 * gcc version 9.2.0 (zephyr-crosstool-NG 1.24.0.4)
* This HAL version 1.8.0

**Describe the bug**
Please refer to https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/29915 and https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/29944
To summarize, TxCpltCallback is not called all the time, and it appears to be related to some incomplete memory transactions. A fix is described in provided links: adding __DSB() to a certain line in this HAL and before/after the HAL_ETH_Transmit_IT resolves it, depending on compiler optimization level. *However*, adding the line in this HAL works in all cases.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Zephyr ethernet driver waits for a semaphore from TxCpltCallback, but it never comes as the callback is never called. So it times out.

2. Ethernet driver for stm32h7xx (stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c)

3. Just transmit an ethernet package

4. Follow the scenario in the provided links.


**Additional context**
If you have a first analysis or patch correction, thank you to share your proposal.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

paragraphs: Issue #95: STM32H7: Ethernet TX callback complete not called intermittently
Title: STM32H7: Ethernet TX callback complete not called intermittently

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus)

**Describe the set-up**
 * STM32H747I DISCO
 * gcc version 9.2.0 (zephyr-crosstool-NG 1.24.0.4)
* This HAL version 1.8.0

**Describe the bug**
Please refer to https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/29915 and https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/29944
To summarize, TxCpltCallback is not called all the time, and it appears to be related to some incomplete memory transactions. A fix is described in provided links: adding __DSB() to a certain line in this HAL and before/after the HAL_ETH_Transmit_IT resolves it, depending on compiler optimization level. *However*, adding the line in this HAL works in all cases.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Zephyr ethernet driver waits for a semaphore from TxCpltCallback, but it never comes as the callback is never called. So it times out.
2. Ethernet driver for stm32h7xx (stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c)
4. Follow the scenario in the provided links.

**Additional context**
If you have a first analysis or patch correction, thank you to share your proposal.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.
