title: [H7] #94: The __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION macro in stm32h7xx.h is wrong
content:
Issue #94: The __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION macro in stm32h7xx.h is wrong

Title: The __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION macro in stm32h7xx.h is wrong

Body:
Hello!

stm32h7xx.h line 106.
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_MAIN   (0x01) /*!< [31:24] main version */
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB1   (0x09) /*!< [23:16] sub1 version */
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB2   (0x00) /*!< [15:8]  sub2 version */
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_RC     (0x00) /*!< [7:0]  release candidate */
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION        ((__CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_MAIN     << 24)\
                                      |(__CMSIS_DEVICE_HAL_VERSION_SUB1 << 16)\
                                      |(__CMSIS_DEVICE_HAL_VERSION_SUB2 << 8 )\
                                      |(__CMSIS_DEVICE_HAL_VERSION_RC))

The result of this macro will always be 0

Here is the correct version:
#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION  ((__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_MAIN << 24)\
                                          |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB1 << 16)\
                                          |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB2 << 8 )\
                                          |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_RC))

Comments:
[RobertoBenjami - 2020-11-22T23:16:55Z]
Already others have commented, I did not notice, sorry.

#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB2   (0x00) /*!< [15:8]  sub2 version */

                                      |(__CMSIS_DEVICE_HAL_VERSION_SUB1 << 16)\
                                      |(__CMSIS_DEVICE_HAL_VERSION_SUB2 << 8 )\

title: [H7] #93: LL_SYSCFG_ETH_RMII definition wrong.
content:
Issue #93: LL_SYSCFG_ETH_RMII definition wrong.

Title: LL_SYSCFG_ETH_RMII definition wrong.

Body:
Hello,

while debugging RMII functionality I've discovered a bug in the stm32h7xx_ll_system.h header. It sets all the bits (including 'reserved' bits) instead of just the bits necessary to enable RMII mode.

Patch looks like this:
@@ -130,7 +135,7 @@
 * @{
 */
 #define LL_SYSCFG_ETH_MII               0x00000000U                           /*!< ETH Media MII interface */
-#define LL_SYSCFG_ETH_RMII              SYSCFG_PMCR_EPIS_SEL                   /*!< ETH Media RMII interface */
+#define LL_SYSCFG_ETH_RMII              SYSCFG_PMCR_EPIS_SEL_2                /*!< ETH Media RMII interface */
 /**
   * @}
   */

Hope this helps.

Best Regards,
Bas

title: [H7] #92: null pointer dereference of lwip function ip_reass_free_complete_datagram
content:
Issue #92: null pointer dereference of lwip function ip_reass_free_complete_datagram

Title: null pointer dereference of lwip function ip_reass_free_complete_datagram

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * The board: STM32H7B3I-DK
 * IDE: STM32CubeIDE 1.4.0
 * Projects: stm_udp_echo_server, stm_udp_echo_client, stm_tcp_echo_server, stm_tcp_echo_client

**Describe the bug**
The lwip function ip_reass_free_complete_datagram() is used to free a datagram (struct ip_reassdata) and all its pbufs. It's called by the function ip_reass_tmr() timely or the function ip_reass_remove_oldest_datagram() to clear oldest datagram.

When trying to build _struct ip_reass_helper *iprh_, then function ip_reass_free_complete_datagram() dereference the pointer _ipr->p->payload_ as below. 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/LwIP/src/core/ipv4/ip4_frag.c#L178. 
However, it doesn't check if _ipr->p_ is a null pointer and there is a chance it could be. This will lead to a null pointer dereference bug.  

**How To Reproduce**
1. Run the demo stm_udp_echo_client which includes lwip.

2. Here is a test case which is a pcap structure package that could be used to reproduce the problem.

[testcase0.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5550488/testcase0.txt)


**Additional context**
1. To patch it, lwip should check if _ipr->p_ is null firstly.
2. This bug still exists in the latest lwip, we have reported it to lwip too.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-12-03T16:36:12Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Thank you for this report. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to Third_Party files in our GitHub repositories. As these files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics, any modifications concerning ip4_frag.c file are not implemented by our technical team.

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution.
 
With regards,

title: [H7] #91: TIM15 is missing in macro IS_TIM_SLAVE_INSTANCE
content:
Issue #91: TIM15 is missing in macro IS_TIM_SLAVE_INSTANCE

Title: TIM15 is missing in macro IS_TIM_SLAVE_INSTANCE

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
* I wanted to use TIM15 in slave mode on the NUCLEO-H743ZI2 board (featuring the STM32H743ZIT6 rev. V)
* Init code for TIM15 is generated by CubeIDE 1.4.0, the MCU Package for H7 used is version 1.8.0

**Describe the bug**
 *  runs into a failed assertion when it comes to calling the function HAL_TIM_SlaveConfigSynchro. Therein the macro IS_TIM_SLAVE_MODE checks the Timer Instance features the Slave Mode Control Register SMCR
* The actual bug is that TIM15 is missing in the macro hence the assertion will fail.

**How To Reproduce**
To reproduce the bug USE_FULL_ASSERT needs to be defined. Running the following code should yield the aforementioned error:
TIM_HandleTypeDef htim15;
TIM_SlaveConfigTypeDef sSlaveConfig = { 0 };
htim15.Instance = TIM15;
HAL_TIM_SlaveConfigSynchro(&htim15, &sSlaveConfig);


**Additional context**
Bug is fixed for me by extending the macro as printed below. The TIM15 is working as I would expect with regards to the Ref Manual.
/****** TIM Instances : Slave mode available (TIMx_SMCR.TS available )*********/
#define IS_TIM_SLAVE_INSTANCE(INSTANCE)   (((INSTANCE) == TIM1)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM2)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM3)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM4)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM5)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM8)   || \
                                           ((INSTANCE) == TIM12)  || \
					   ((INSTANCE) == TIM15))

Oddly enough some other header files (only speaking for H7 series) indeed do contain TIM15 in the macro definition (e.g. stm32h730.h). To me it wouldn't be much of a surprise if the same problem exists in even more files accross the STM32 series, assuming the "silicon" for TIM15 is by and large the same.

title: [H7] #90: Calling xQueueReceiveFromISR() from within Interrupt results in ASSERET trigger
paragraphs: Issue #90: Calling xQueueReceiveFromISR() from within Interrupt results in ASSERET trigger
Title: Calling xQueueReceiveFromISR() from within Interrupt results in ASSERET trigger

Body:
Hello, Microcontroller in use: STM32H753BIT6 My code uses:

HAL_NVIC_SetPriority(USART1_IRQn, 5, 0);
HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ(USART1_IRQn);
My code calls  xQueueReceiveFromISR() inside the USART1_IRQ. I am observing issue with xQueueReceiveFromISR() (which is called with help of osMessageQueueGet())

When I analyzed the issue it turned out that ASSERT was triggered at https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c#L754
inside function vPortValidateInterruptPriority().

I had to change lines:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c#L72-L73

#define portFIRST_USER_INTERRUPT_NUMBER       ( 6 )
#define portNVIC_IP_REGISTERS_OFFSET_16       ( 0xE000E3FA )

Also, I had to change line:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F/port.c#L729

to:
ucCurrentPriority = *(pcInterruptPriorityRegisters + ulCurrentInterrupt);

To fix the ASSERT issue. Please let me know your inputs. Thanks,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2020-11-15T04:55:42Z]
ulCurrentInterrupt = 0x25

[rxa1031 - 2020-11-19T07:50:40Z]
Errata:
Code was using TIM2 IRQ and not UART1 IRQ
The actual problem is not the portNVIC_IP_REGISTERS_OFFSET_16 or portFIRST_USER_INTERRUPT_NUMBER values.

The issue exists because of 
#define  TICK_INT_PRIORITY            ((uint32_t)0U) /*!< tick interrupt priority */
And its use during configuring the Timer that is used by FreeRTOS.
_If xQueueReceiveFromISR() is called inside the FreeRTOS Timer interrupt the value copied to ucCurrentPriority (inside function vPortValidateInterruptPriority()) is ZERO._
I am collecting some more details with regards to _HAL_InitTick() which seems to be the culprit for FreeRTOS timer's priority setting_ and also might help me elaborate on #71 observations.

I observe concerns with FreeRTOS file GCC\ARM_CM4\port.c. I am observing two ASSERTS / concerns in the file (triggered as a result of Reading from or Writing to a Queue from ISR):
 
**First Issue:**
Is observed for configASSERT( ucCurrentPriority >= ucMaxSysCallPriority ); when variable uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY (either assigned directly or with help of  variable TickPriority), where macro TICK_INT_PRIORITY is assigned a value of 0.
If I _do not use a software interrupt_ to process the Queue, then because of uwTickPrio value (i.e. 0) being used as priority for FreeRTOS Timer (i.e. Timer 2), the ASSERT gets raised.

If I _use the software interrupt_, then I observe the _second issue_ stated below
 
**Second issue:**
Is observed for configASSERT( ( portAIRCR_REG & portPRIORITY_GROUP_MASK ) <= ulMaxPRIGROUPValue ); especially when triggered software interrupts are being handled. The software interrupt is triggered by updating the related interrupt number NVIC->STIR
**Additional observations and concerns:**

1. May you please help me understand whether TICK_INT_PRIORITY value is user changeable?
2. Also should the NVIC_SetPriorityGrouping(4) or NVIC_SetPriorityGrouping(0) to be used? It is observed that HAL_Init() calls HAL_NVIC_SetPriorityGrouping(4), hence if the Priority Group is to be set to 0 then should NVIC_SetPriorityGrouping(0) called just after calling HAL_Init()?

**Flow:**
SW_FroTimer_IRQHandler() triggered -> SW_FroTimer_IRQHandler() serviced -> calls third party library function -> Third part library calls function rvAddEventToInterruptEvents() which I've coded -> rvAddEventToInterruptEvents() triggers another software interrupt -> SW_IRQHandler() executes for good number of times and then suddenly the issue 2 occurs.
**Important Observation:**  It is observed that **pxCurrentTCB->pcTaskName == "IDLE"** when any of the software interrupt is executing.

**My code has:**
// USART2 IRQ being used for software interrupt and is triggered from a third party library on which my code does not have any control. This execution is the result of execution of SW_FroTimer_IRQHandler() when reviewed in Call Stack.
#define SW_Int_IRQn			USART2_IRQn
#define SW_IRQHandler		USART2_IRQHandler

HAL_NVIC_SetPriority(SW_Int_IRQn, 6, 0);
			HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ(SW_Int_IRQn);

HAL_NVIC_SetPriority(SW_FroTimer_Int_IRQn, 6, 0);
			HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ(SW_FroTimer_Int_IRQn);
Inside HAL_TIM_PeriodElapsedCallback() below code is added:
The other piece of code that triggers the software interrupt is:
Is it possible for you to suggest some pointers on how to fix the issues.
I request your views on below observations.

Based on all the analysis, I found out that the at least one of the two configASSERT() present with function vPortValidateInterruptPriority() will get triggered when the Queue related code executes from within a Software Interrupt ISR that is triggered by writing Interrupt number of the interrupt to be triggered to NVIC->STIR (example NVIC->STIR = SW_FroTimer_Int_IRQn;).
To me it appears that the workaround for now is to use **vTaskNotifyGiveFromISR()** and **ulTaskNotifyTake()** _along with the software interrupt_ (as shared below):
1. Create a handle FroTimerTaskHandle for task T_FronTimer() in main

2. SW_FroTimer_IRQHandler() / Software Inerrupt function to call vTaskNotifyGiveFromISR(), instead of executing the functionalities were executed with help of call to function rLowSystemCInterrupt(). i.e.
3. rLowSystemCInterrupt();  (which was earlier called from SW_FroTimer_IRQHandler()) is modified to call ulTaskNotifyTake(). i.e.

Further the CMSIS wrapper function osMessageQueueGet() (now executing from inside a task) now calls FreeRTOS function xQueueReceive() in place of function xQueueReceiveFromISR() (which was earlier called as a result of ISR related code in execution).

Regards,
Rajeev [RKOUSTM - 2020-12-25T17:53:00Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to Third_Party files in our GitHub repositories. As these files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics, any modifications concerning port.c file are not implemented by our technical team. All aspects related to FreeRTOS are not handled in our GitHub repositories. They are rather treated at [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos).
Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you again for your contribution.
issue_title: Calling xQueueReceiveFromISR() from within Interrupt results in ASSERET trigger

title: [H7] #89: HAL_SDRAM_Init should take timing argument as const
content:
Issue #89: HAL_SDRAM_Init should take timing argument as const

Title: HAL_SDRAM_Init should take timing argument as const

Body:
**Describe the set-up**

STM32H747I-DISCO, using Zephyr RTOS.

**Describe the bug**


HAL_StatusTypeDef HAL_SDRAM_Init(SDRAM_HandleTypeDef *hsdram, FMC_SDRAM_TimingTypeDef *Timing)


Should ideally take Timing argument as const, i.e.


HAL_StatusTypeDef HAL_SDRAM_Init(SDRAM_HandleTypeDef *hsdram, const FMC_SDRAM_TimingTypeDef *Timing)


Ref.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_sdram.c#L171

Note that the same should happen for the LL counterpart, https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_fmc.c#L791

Other FMC functions can probably be adjusted.

**How To Reproduce**

Not causing any problem, just warnings if using a const structure.

**Additional context**

N/A

**Screenshots**

N/A

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-11-12T14:52:46Z]
Hi @gmarull ,

Thank you for this detailed report. This issue has also been previously reported by other users ([link](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeF4/issues/10)).

This is one of the main points in the tasks queue of our development teams. We cannot share a date regarding its deployment and publication. We count on your patience and comprehension.

Thank you again for this report. 

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-11-19T13:37:16Z]
Hi @gmarull, 

First, allow me to thank you for your contribution. The final decision of our technical committee is no plan to add a const inside the function. The reported point is already highlighted during the MISRA-C 2012 compliance rework (Rule-8.13). It has been decided, from the quality point of view, to derogate this rule as fixing it will break the compatibility with almost all HAL APIs and then update all examples and applications within the Cube deliverable and also on user application side.

Please, allow me to close this issue. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,
paragraphs: Issue #89: HAL_SDRAM_Init should take timing argument as const
Title: HAL_SDRAM_Init should take timing argument as const

Body:
**Describe the set-up** STM32H747I-DISCO, using Zephyr RTOS.
HAL_StatusTypeDef HAL_SDRAM_Init(SDRAM_HandleTypeDef *hsdram, FMC_SDRAM_TimingTypeDef *Timing)
Should ideally take Timing argument as const, i.e.
HAL_StatusTypeDef HAL_SDRAM_Init(SDRAM_HandleTypeDef *hsdram, const FMC_SDRAM_TimingTypeDef *Timing)

Ref.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_sdram.c#L171
Note that the same should happen for the LL counterpart, https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_ll_fmc.c#L791
Other FMC functions can probably be adjusted. **How To Reproduce**
Not causing any problem, just warnings if using a const structure.
**Additional context** N/A **Screenshots** N/A

title: [H7] #85: incorrect implementation of pvPortMalloc by the heap_4.c of FreeRTOS
content:
Issue #85: incorrect implementation of pvPortMalloc by the heap_4.c of FreeRTOS

Title: incorrect implementation of pvPortMalloc by the heap_4.c of FreeRTOS

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * Development board: STM32H7B3I-DK and STM32H7B3I-EVAL
 * IDE and version: STMCubeIDE 1.4.0

**Describe the bug**
The function pvPortMalloc() is used to allocate memory for the required size.
The parameter xWantedSize indicates the length of memory required. However, when the required memory block size is zero. The memory pointer variable pvReturn will keep as NULL. If the configuration configUSE_MALLOC_FAILED_HOOK is enabled, it will recognize the variable xWantedSize valued zero as an error and handle it with the function vApplicationMallocFailedHook() as shown in https://github.com/FreeRTOS/FreeRTOS-Kernel/blob/5fb26de0194b4ebfd09b37128bf9f3ff6d34c1b7/portable/MemMang/heap_4.c#L253 (also https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/MemMang/heap_4.c#L249)

**How To Reproduce**
1. Import the Domenstration example ClockAndWeather of the board STM32H7B3I-DK.

2. Enable macro configUSE_MALLOC_FAILED_HOOK. This is default enabled when I try to debug the program.

3. Run the firmware with a default configuration to reproduce and it will loop infinitely.

4. This problem is caused by the incorrect implementation of the function pvPortMalloc() by heap_4.c of FreeRTOS. This bug is already reported to FreeRTOS as shown in https://github.com/FreeRTOS/FreeRTOS-Kernel/issues/204. 


**Additional context**

To patch it, should return NULL or the next available heap address when the parameter xWantedSize is zero.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-11-11T13:12:32Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Thank you for this report. Unfortunately, for this point, we don't treat aspect related to FreeRTOS in our GitHub repositories. As the heap_4.c file is out of the scope of STMicroelectronics, any modifications concerning this file are not implemented by our technical team. 

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you for your contribution. 

With regards,

title: [H7] #84: Buffer Overflow due to the variable ep_descriptor->bLength
content:
Issue #84: Buffer Overflow due to the variable ep_descriptor->bLength

Title: Buffer Overflow due to the variable ep_descriptor->bLength

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
    - static void  USBH_ParseEPDesc(USBH_EpDescTypeDef  *ep_descriptor, uint8_t *buf)

- Location:
    - https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L466


- Type:
	- Buffer Overflow

- Result:
	- The system could be configured incorrectly with wrong parameters.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseEPDesc() parses endpoint descriptor.  It’s called by the function USBH_ParseCfgDesc() as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L423. 
    - It doesn’t check the validity of the variable ep_descriptor->bLength compared with the total length of the input buffer which may cause a buffer overflow by the following called function USBH_GetNextDesc() as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L419. 




**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug10.txt to replace the USB device packet. 
[Bug10.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381420/Bug10.txt)


**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if reach the end of the input buffer when plus ep_descriptor->bLength.

Comments:

ST Internal Reference: 99173

With regards,

> 
> With regards,

I'm glad to hear that all of the fixes have been issued. Actually, these bugs are found by an automatic fuzzing tool developed by my lab. We are now working on a research article that discusses our novel technique in detail. I'm wondering whether your team might assist us in obtaining CVE IDs for these issues so that we can confidently refer to these CVE IDs and indicate our responsible disclosure. Thanks for your help.

Hope you're fine. Just to inform you the fix has been published in the frame of **v1.10.0** release.

content:
Issue #83: Buffer Overflow due to the variable if_descriptor->bLength

Title: Buffer Overflow due to the variable if_descriptor->bLength

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if reach the end of the input buffer when plus if_descriptor->bLength.

title: [H7] #82: No checking if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero
content:
Issue #82: No checking if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero

Title: No checking if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void  USBH_ParseEPDesc(USBH_EpDescTypeDef  *ep_descriptor, uint8_t *buf)

- Location:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L471

- Type:
	- Denial-of-Service.

- Result:
	- The system will hang.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseEPDesc parses the endpoint descriptor of a USB device. 
    - It doesn’t check if the variable ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero. This variable will be used in the class to set the polling interval, for example as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/AUDIO/Src/usbh_audio.c#L858. If set zero, the system will hang when polling operation. 



**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug8.txt to replace the USB device packet. 
[Bug8.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381346/Bug8.txt)


**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-01-18T13:41:46Z]
ST Internal Reference: 99173

[ALABSTM - 2022-03-14T16:27:19Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Hope you're fine. Just to inform you the fix has been published in the frame of **v1.10.0** release.

With regards,
paragraphs: Issue #82: No checking if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero
Title: No checking if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void  USBH_ParseEPDesc(USBH_EpDescTypeDef  *ep_descriptor, uint8_t *buf)

- Location:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L471

- Type:
	- Denial-of-Service. - Result:
	- The system will hang.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseEPDesc parses the endpoint descriptor of a USB device. 
    - It doesn’t check if the variable ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero. This variable will be used in the class to set the polling interval, for example as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/AUDIO/Src/usbh_audio.c#L858. If set zero, the system will hang when polling operation.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

3. Use the attached Bug8.txt to replace the USB device packet. 
[Bug8.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381346/Bug8.txt)

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if ep_descriptor->bInterval is greater than zero.

title: [H7] #80: No checking on both IN and OUT pipe constructed
content:
Issue #80: No checking on both IN and OUT pipe constructed

Title: No checking on both IN and OUT pipe constructed

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static USBH_StatusTypeDef USBH_MSC_InterfaceInit(USBH_HandleTypeDef *phost)

- Location:
	- From line 180 to line 200 in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/MSC/Src/usbh_msc.c

- Type:
	- Denial-of-Service.

- Result:
	- The system will hang when try to communicate with the endpoint.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_MSC_InterfaceInit() inits the status of MSC handler. It initializes the IN endpoint and OUT endpoint as shown from line 180 to line 200 in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/MSC/Src/usbh_msc.c. 
    - However, when the variable bEndpointAddress of endpoint descriptor are both masked as IN or OUT without checking as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L468, the MSC handler will also only initialize the IN or OUT part as shown from line 180 to line 200 in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/MSC/Src/usbh_msc.c.  


**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug6.txt to replace the USB device packet. 
[Bug6.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381333/Bug6.txt)


**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check both IN and OUT pipe is constructed.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2020-11-24T11:28:33Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Thank you for this other report. To be sure I understood your point, please allow me these questions:
* We are dealing with the case of a **simultaneously** IN and OUT endpoint? This is what I understood from the expression *"the variable bEndpointAddress of endpoint descriptor are both masked as IN or OUT"*.
* We are requesting from the software to check both? This is what I understood from the expression *"the program should check **both** IN and OUT pipe is constructed"*

Thank you in advance for your clarifications.

With regards,

[TheSilentDawn - 2020-11-25T20:33:01Z]
Hi @ALABSTM,
Yes, the firmware should open an IN pipe and an OUT pipe to communicate with a device normally. However, as the report described, the driver codes do not check this. It will open two IN pipes or two OUT pipes unexpectedly which will block the communication in the subsequent execution of the firmware. If anything not clear, please let me know. Thanks for your help.^_^

[ALABSTM - 2020-12-02T11:37:02Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Thank you for the clarification. Hence, the case you are describing is an interface exposing 2x IN endpoints **or** 2x OUT endpoints **instead of** 1x IN endpoint and 1x OUT endpoint (which is the normal case for a **MSC**).

I will forward your report to our development teams. I will get back to you as soon as they provide me with their feedback.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2021-01-18T13:43:38Z]
ST Internal Reference: 99173

[ALABSTM - 2022-03-14T16:27:03Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Hope you're fine. Just to inform you the fix has been published in the frame of **v1.10.0** release.

With regards,

title: [H7] #79: Buffer Overflow due to wrong pdesc->bDescriptorType
content:
Issue #79: Buffer Overflow due to wrong pdesc->bDescriptorType

Title: Buffer Overflow due to wrong pdesc->bDescriptorType

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void USBH_ParseCfgDesc(USBH_CfgDescTypeDef *cfg_desc, uint8_t *buf, uint16_t length)

- Location:
  - https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L420

- Type:
	- Buffer Overflow.

- Result:
	- The system will parse the wrong descriptor.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseCfgDesc() parses the configuration descriptor, interface descriptor, and endpoint descriptor by input data from a USB device. 
    - It tries to parse the endpoint descriptor when the variable pdesc->bDescriptorType is equal to USB_DESC_TYPE_ENDPOINT as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L420. If the variable pdesc->bDescriptorType is not equal to USB_DESC_TYPE_ENDPOINT, the firmware will try to access further memory until reaching the number pif->bNumEndpoints as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L417. If the variable cfg_desc->wTotalLength is also malformed, the firmware will overflow. If not, some endpoint descriptors will be left unset as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L422. And the firmware behaves unpredictably as shown from line 180 to 200 in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Class/MSC/Src/usbh_msc.c.




**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug5.txt to replace the USB device packet. [Bug5.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381294/Bug5.txt)

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should return an error when pdesc->bDescriptorType is not equal to USB_DESC_TYPE_ENDPOINT.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2021-01-18T13:43:51Z]
ST Internal Reference: 99173

[ALABSTM - 2022-03-14T16:26:50Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Hope you're fine. Just to inform you the fix has been published in the frame of **v1.10.0** release.

With regards,

title: [H7] #78: Lack hardware wake-up support checking
content:
Issue #78: Lack hardware wake-up support checking

Title: Lack hardware wake-up support checking

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void USBH_ParseCfgDesc(USBH_CfgDescTypeDef *cfg_desc, uint8_t *buf, uint16_t length)

- Location:
  - https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L399

- Type:
	- Denial-of-Service.

- Result:
	- The system will hang when trying to set a remote wake-up feature.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseCfgDesc() parses the configuration descriptor, interface descriptor, and endpoint descriptor by input data from a USB device. 
    - And it set the variable cfg_desc->bmAttributes by the input data from the USB device. This variable will be used as part of a judgment in the function USBH_Process() as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_core.c#L643. With a malformed value, the remote wakeup may be enabled as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_core.c#L643. If the hardware doesn’t support this feature, the system will hang due to a FAIL return value by the function USBH_HandleControl(). 




**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug4.txt to replace the USB device packet. [Bug4.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381270/Bug4.txt)

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if the hardware supports a remote wake-up feature.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2020-11-24T11:43:54Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Thank you for this other report.  If I understood the idea we should ensure that the software embedded within the *host* checks whether the value *bmAttributes* provided by the *device* is correct? Which reference should the *host* base itself on to perform such a check? Should we not assume the data provided by the *device* is correct?

Otherwise, when to stop checking? In case the *host* has a reference to base itself on, then would it not also make sense to check the validity of this reference too?

I hope my questions make sense. My goal is to be sure I understood correctly your point before reporting it to our development teams. I am looking forward to reading your reply. Thank you again.

With regards,

[TheSilentDawn - 2020-11-25T20:39:33Z]
Hi @ALABSTM,
No, I think the software embedded within the host should check if the hardware or the board supports or enables the wake-up feature. If not, the firmware shouldn't check bmAttributes as the current logic which will lead the whole system to hang. And to patch it, the wake-up feature checking codes should be added before the bmAttributes is accessed in the function and position reported upper. If anything not clear, please let me know. Thanks for your help.^_^

[ALABSTM - 2020-12-02T11:43:49Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Please allow me this couple of questions:
* Is there any way for the host to check whether the device supports the wake-up feature other than the bmAttributes read from the device descriptor?
* You mentioned *"the wake-up feature checking codes"*. Are you referring to some piece of code already present in our source files that should be moved before the reading of the bmAttributes?

Thank you,

[ALABSTM - 2021-01-18T13:44:05Z]
ST Internal Reference: 99173

[ALABSTM - 2022-03-14T16:26:39Z]
Hi @TheSilentDawn,

Hope you're fine. Just to inform you the fix has been published in the frame of **v1.10.0** release.

With regards,

With a malformed value, the remote wakeup may be enabled as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_core.c#L643. If the hardware doesn’t support this feature, the system will hang due to a FAIL return value by the function USBH_HandleControl().

**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform
3. Use the attached Bug4.txt to replace the USB device packet. [Bug4.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381270/Bug4.txt)

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if the hardware supports a remote wake-up feature.

Otherwise, when to stop checking? In case the *host* has a reference to base itself on, then would it not also make sense to check the validity of this reference too?

title: [H7] #77: No validity checking on the variable cfg_desc->bLength
content:
Issue #77: No validity checking on the variable cfg_desc->bLength

Title: No validity checking on the variable cfg_desc->bLength

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void USBH_ParseCfgDesc(USBH_CfgDescTypeDef *cfg_desc, uint8_t *buf, uint16_t length)

- Location:
    - https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L393

- Type:
	- Buffer Overflow.

- Result:
	- The system could be configured incorrectly with the wrong parameters.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseCfgDesc() parses the configuration descriptor, interface descriptor, and endpoint descriptor by input data from a USB device. 
  - However, it doesn’t check the validity of the variable cfg_desc->bLength compared with the total length of the input buffer and the restriction of enumeration requirement by USB specification as shown in  https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L393. This will cause the following function USBH_GetNextDesc() and others access wrong memory region and the system will be configured incorrectly.



**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug3.txt to replace the USB device packet. [Bug3.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381301/Bug3.txt)


**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if reach the end of the input buffer when plus cfg_desc->bLength.

- Type:

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseCfgDesc() parses the configuration descriptor, interface descriptor, and endpoint descriptor by input data from a USB device.

title: [H7] #75: No validity chekcing on the variable dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize
content:
Issue #75: No validity chekcing on the variable dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize

Title: No validity chekcing on the variable dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
- Software:
	- STM32Cube MCU & MPU Packages
- Version:
	- STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0
- Verification Hardware Platform:
	- STM32H7B3

**Describe the bug**
- Function:
	- static void  USBH_ParseDevDesc(USBH_DevDescTypeDef *dev_desc, uint8_t *buf, uint16_t length)

- Location:
  - https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L355

- Type:
	- Denial-of-Service.

- Result:
  - A malformed USB device packet may cause the system to hang when it tries to communicate with the outside world.

- Description:
	- The function USBH_ParseDevDesc() parses the device descriptor by input data from a USB device. 
    - The valid max packet size of the device descriptor should be 8, 16, 32, and 64 as USB specification required. However, the function USBH_ParseDevDesc() doesn’t check the value of dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_ctlreq.c#L355. The variable dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize will be used as the size to construct the control pipe between host and device as shown in https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Middlewares/ST/STM32_USB_Host_Library/Core/Src/usbh_core.c#L828. If bMaxPacketSize is zero, the firmware will get the error status USBH_FAIL in the function USBH_HandleControl() called by the function USBH_CtlReq() when trying to communicate with the outside world by IN and OUT pipe in the future and the host will try to re-enumerate. This process will loop again and again.


**How To Reproduce**
1. Running MSC_Standalone application on the STM32H7B3I platform

2. Plug a USB disk

3. Use the attached Bug1.txt to replace the USB device packet. [Bug1.txt](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5381197/Bug1.txt)

**Additional context**
- To patch it, the program should check if dev_desc->bMaxPacketSize is equal to 8, 16, 32 or 64. At least, it should be greater than zero.

title: [H7] #73: Functions like osAbortDelay(), osMessageWaiting() wrappers unavailable in file cmsis_os2.c
content:
Issue #73: Functions like osAbortDelay(), osMessageWaiting() wrappers unavailable in file cmsis_os2.c

Title: Functions like osAbortDelay(), osMessageWaiting() wrappers unavailable in file cmsis_os2.c

Body:
Hello,

I was looking out for a CMSIS wrapper for FreeRTOS function xTaskAbortDelay() and found that the wrapper is only available with CMSIS_OS.C.

As I am using FreeRTOS with CMSIS Version 2 the wrapper is not available for use. The reason for this is that CMSIS_OS2.C (and CMSIS_OS2.H) do not implement the wrapper function.

_May ST please add the necessary wrapper functions that are available in CMSIS_OS.C to CMSIS_OS2.C file as well._

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-10-20T17:41:34Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your report. It has been forwarded to our development teams. We will get back to you as soon as they provide us with their feedback.

Best regards,

[rxa1031 - 2021-03-23T05:24:19Z]
Hello @ALABSTM ,

Are there any findings / updates that you may be able to share with me.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[RKOUSTM - 2021-08-04T09:14:50Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

I hope you are fine. Our development teams said your proposal could not be taken into consideration for the following reason:

> CMSIS_RTOS is not meant to cover every single API from every RTOS. When a feature is needed for a specific use case, a user can add its macro in the application code. On CMSIS_RTOS_V1 implemented the osAbortDelay and then in CMSIS_RTOS_V2 this choice has not been continued in order to keep the interface as simple and as generic as possible.

We really wished to provide you with a positive answer, but could unfortunately not. Please allow me to close this issue now and thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2025-04-25T14:11:57Z]
See also [STM32CubeF4#170](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeF4/issues/170)

title: [H7] #72: STM32H753BIT6 Temperature Sensor Channel different reported values
paragraphs: Issue #72: STM32H753BIT6 Temperature Sensor Channel different reported values
Title: STM32H753BIT6 Temperature Sensor Channel different reported values

I have enabled the ADC3 to read VBAT, VREF and Temperature Sensor Channel in continuous mode. After initialization of the ADC3, my code calls the calibration function once before it starts reading the channels.
I am observing that __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE() outputs a lower temperature value compared to __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMS().
The value returned by __HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE() seems to be closer to the temperature measured on the microcontroller's casing.

I assume the temperature of the _microcontroller DIE_ would be much higher. Please help me understand _which of the two formulas to use, and why is there a difference in the reported Temperature values_?
Below values will be helpful for analyzing the Temperature outputs:

VRef voltage is 3291 mV.
VBAT voltage is 712 mV.
40, and 45 are Temperature in Degree C calculated using two different formulas.
/* USER CODE BEGIN Init */ /* USER CODE END Init */

/* Configure the system clock */
  SystemClock_Config();
/* USER CODE BEGIN SysInit */ /* USER CODE BEGIN 2 */

MX_ADC3_Init();
//--- user code continues ---
// Reading ADC3 inputs VBAT, VRef, and Internal Temperature Sensor Channel.

/**
  * @brief ADC3 Initialization Function
  * @param None
  * @retval None
  */
static void MX_ADC3_Init(void)
{
/* USER CODE BEGIN ADC3_Init 0 */ /* USER CODE END ADC3_Init 0 */
ADC_ChannelConfTypeDef sConfig = {0}; /* USER CODE BEGIN ADC3_Init 1 */

#endif // of defined(_DRA_VBAT_VREF_AND_TEMPERATURE_ADC_READ_)
}

Please help with resolving temperature output valueconcerns. Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2020-10-13T08:58:20Z]
As the Battery had got discharged I have removed the battery. I am observing the VBAT reading around 712 mV and not Zero.

The VSS and VSSA ground are same. The battery negative is connected to VSS and battery +ve is connected to Anode of IN5819. The Cathode of 1N5819 is then connected to microcontroller's VBAT pin (i.e. Pin 6).
Thank you for this report. Your request has been forwarded to our development teams. We will get back to you as soon as they provide us with their feedback.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2020-11-16T15:55:17Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

For a further understanding of the problem, would you please check whether the chip is calibrated or not.  
For that, you have to access to the memory addresses where **TS_CAL1** and **TS_CAL2** values are stored and check if these values are not equal to zero and share them with us. 
You may find these memory addresses in the [datasheet](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h753vi.pdf) under the **Temperature sensor characteristics** section.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47858836/99272638-4ec0e780-2828-11eb-9e92-c93195b1c196.png)

With regards, [rxa1031 - 2020-11-17T05:12:38Z]
Hello @ASELSTM  ,

| Address | Value |
| 0x1FF1E820 | 0x78 |
| 0x1FF1E821 | 0x30 |
| 0x1FF1E840 | 0x7A |
| 0x1FF1E841 | 0x3F |

Regards, [ASELSTM - 2020-12-15T12:28:35Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank for these details. Unfortunately we couldn't reproduce this issue. We used the same code and configuration that you shared and the temperature values returned by both macros are quite similar. The difference is minor, only 1°C.
However, to answer your question regarding the difference between both macros :

- **__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE** : The temperature computation within this macro is using temperature sensor calibration values stored in system memory for each device during production. These calibration values are the temperature sensor raw data acquired value at certain temperature calibrated in factory (30°C and 110°C for STM32H7 case) . The formula used for the temperature computation is detailed in the section **"25.4.33 Temperature sensor"** of the [reference manual](https://www.st.com/resource/en/reference_manual/dm00314099-stm32h742-stm32h743-753-and-stm32h750-value-line-advanced-arm-based-32-bit-mcus-stmicroelectronics.pdf). ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47858836/102211859-8af17180-3ed4-11eb-9261-a215b5e132fa.png)
The calibration is not performed on some device during production, which is not your case since there is calibration values stored in the dedicated memory address.

- **__HAL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMS** : The temperature computation within this macro is based on the temperature sensor typical values detailed in the [datasheet ](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h753vi.pdf) within the section **"6.3.23 Temperature sensor characteristics"** and the formula is described at the beginning of the **__LL_ADC_CALC_TEMPERATURE_TYP_PARAMS** macro description.
These two macros give quite similar temperature values, and the difference is minimal.
I hope this will help you better understand the difference between these 2 macros.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2021-01-12T09:45:02Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Please allow me to close this thread as no activity has been registered since a month. You can reopen it at any time if you have details to provide us to help you solve this issue.
Thank you for your comprehension and thank you once more for your contribution
issue_title: STM32H753BIT6 Temperature Sensor Channel different reported values

title: [H7] #71: Calling SystemClock_Config() results in call to assert_failed() because of missing assignment uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY
content:
Issue #71: Calling SystemClock_Config() results in call to assert_failed() because of missing assignment uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY

Title: Calling SystemClock_Config() results in call to assert_failed() because of missing assignment uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY

Body:
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

After a generated code with help of STM32CubeMX (with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0), which has FreeRTOS enabled and uses CMSIS V2, I _added endless while loops_ to both the assert_failed() and the Error_Handler() functions.

When debugging the code it is observed that assert_failed() triggers when SystemClock_config() is called / executes.

It looks like the HAL library got updated to use the variable 'uwTickPrio' in the function HAL_RCC_ClockConfig, instead of what was used in the older library, TICK_INT_PRIORITY.

May ST please help me understand the reason why, 'uwTickPrio' is intentionally initialized to an invalid value.

In order to fix the (assert_failed() function called) issue I have added 

	uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY;

inside function main() body, just before the comment:

  /* USER CODE END Init */s


The code now works fine.

Please help me with your inputs.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-11-02T17:25:02Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. We tried to reproduce the issue you described, but we couldn't notice the problem.

According to the following process of initializing the "uwTickPrio" variable to an invalid value, an update of the time base management is proposed to take into account low frequency systems where, at low frequency, a 1 ms systick interrupt does not allow to do anything else than a treated interrupt. 

uint32_t uwTickPrio   = (1UL << __NVIC_PRIO_BITS); /* Invalid PRIO */

In HAL_InitTick() function, when checking the tick priority, it is better to relay on __NVIC_PRIO_BITS to check the tick priority limit of ticks instead of the initialized number (3 for M0, 15 for M4/M7).

Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-11-02T17:33:50Z]
Hi @RKOUSTM ,

I believe the reason for not observing the issue is that, either the FULL ASSERT related macro was not enabled or the tester did not place a break point inside the assert_failed() function body (or both).

Please confirm whether, the tester enable FULL ASSERT in your test code? 

stm32h7xx_hal_conf.h must include:

 #define USE_FULL_ASSERT    1U

Did the tester place a break point or a debug output message inside assert_failed() function?

The declaration for the function is:

void assert_failed(uint8_t *file, uint32_t line)

Regards,
Rajeev

[rxa1031 - 2020-11-02T18:01:48Z]
Hi @RKOUSTM ,

I observe below in STM32CubeH7/Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h753xx.h

#define __NVIC_PRIO_BITS          4       /*!< CM7 uses 4 Bits for the Priority Levels       */

Based on your input I assume the macro should have been assigned the value 15 instead of 4.

Please suggest.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[rxa1031 - 2020-11-02T18:03:49Z]
1<<3 is 8 and 1<<4 is 16. In your comment I observe values as 3 and 15 and hence not sure whether you are asking me to left shift 1 by 15 or by 4 (for M7).

[RKOUSTM - 2020-11-03T09:03:07Z]
Hi @rxa1031, 

Thank you for this report, once again. Indeed, concerning  cm4 and cm7, **__NVIC_PRIO_BITS** is configured to 4. Regarding the point you reported, we couldn't reproduce the issue you described. In this case, it is rather related to **Cube MX** issue or even way you think to design you application. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to **CubeMx** tool in our GitHub repositories. Please allow me to redirect you to the **ST Community**. You can report this issue in the CubeMX related [section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx). 

Please allow me to close this ticket as it is out of the scope of this GitHub organization. 
Thank you again for your report. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-11-26T16:19:14Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

I found out that the reason for the primary / first concern is the issue mentioned under #23 

Regards,
@rxa1031

[jnewcomb - 2021-01-02T20:39:58Z]
Update:
I think I may have fixed it - at least the problem has gone away...
It is possible I was using an older local copy of stm32g4xx_hal_timebase_tim.c - The latest version had some edits to HAL_InitTick() which may have fixed the issue. 

_______________
@rxa1031 
Hi.. I'm also experiencing this issue - and I fixed it by following your suggestion.

uwTickPrio = TICK_INT_PRIORITY;

Did you ever find out the root cause of this? I just followed the exact path you took!
You mention #23 but I don't see how it relates.

I found some year old discussions on this - maybe its just one of those ongoing CubeMX issues:
[https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000C6duToSQI/](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000C6duToSQI/halnvicsetpriority-assert-issue-and-incorrect-preempt-priority-value-for-the-stm32f4-stm32g4-)
and
[https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000BrBv8LSQS/](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000BrBv8LSQS/timebase-source-tim1-wrong-initialization-in-mx-code)

Guess I just want to understand as it killed a whole day of my productivity - or should I just move on, none-the-wiser!
Thanks.
Jon.

[rxa1031 - 2021-01-04T04:47:02Z]
Hello Jon @jnewcomb ,

I am unable to figure out how to share the links to the concerns that I had raised regarding this issue on ST Community. Below is my profile page on ST Community.

https://community.st.com/s/profile/0050X000009yi2mQAA

**Concern 1:**
Is there a setting with STM32CubeMX to change the Tick Init Priority / TICK_INT_PRIORITY value?

Raised on November 27, 2020

Answer: No

**Concern 2:**
TickPriority not assigned to uwTickPrio

Contains two issues

_Raised on  November 19, 2020_

Requested modification of code present with Function **HAL_InitTick()**

Solution to the informed problem is to replace code:

    /*Configure the TIM2 IRQ priority */
      HAL_NVIC_SetPriority(TIM2_IRQn, TickPriority, 0);
     
      /* Enable the TIM2 global Interrupt */
      HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ(TIM2_IRQn);
     
      /* Enable TIM2 clock */
      __HAL_RCC_TIM2_CLK_ENABLE();
 
with

    /*Configure the TIM2 IRQ priority */
      if (TickPriority < (1UL << __NVIC_PRIO_BITS))
      {
        HAL_NVIC_SetPriority(TIM2_IRQn, TickPriority ,0U);
     
        /* Enable the TIM2 global Interrupt */
        HAL_NVIC_EnableIRQ(TIM2_IRQn);

        uwTickPrio = TickPriority;
     
        /* Enable TIM2 clock */
        __HAL_RCC_TIM2_CLK_ENABLE();
      }
      else
      {
        return HAL_ERROR;
      }


We need the assignment uwTickPrio = TickPriority; added to function to fix the issue.

This issue is (possibly) fixed in STM32CubeMX for STM32H7XX Series Microcontrollers.

Regards,
Rajeev

title: [H7] #70: Compile time linker errors
content:
Issue #70: Compile time linker errors

Title: Compile time linker errors

Body:
Hello,

Using Latest version of STM32CubeMx and STM32H7 package.

After enabling FreeRTOS and making minor changes to its settings within the STM32CubeMX, code is generated for MDK-ARM (minimum version 5.27).

On compiling the code below ERRORS are observed:

linking...
Gateway\Gateway.axf: Error: L6200E: Symbol vApplicationIdleHook multiply defined (by cmsis_os2.o and freertos.o).
Gateway\Gateway.axf: Error: L6200E: Symbol vApplicationMallocFailedHook multiply defined (by cmsis_os2.o and freertos.o).
Gateway\Gateway.axf: Error: L6200E: Symbol vApplicationStackOverflowHook multiply defined (by cmsis_os2.o and freertos.o).
Gateway\Gateway.axf: Error: L6200E: Symbol vApplicationTickHook multiply defined (by cmsis_os2.o and freertos.o).
Not enough information to list image symbols.
Not enough information to list load addresses in the image map.
Finished: 2 information, 0 warning and 4 error messages.


Attached IOC file for your reference.

[Gateway.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5345757/Gateway.zip)

Before compiling code add below to main.c under /* USER CODE BEGIN PV */


uint8_t u8MaxStandardCAN1Filters;
uint8_t u8MaxExtendedCAN1Filters;
uint8_t u8MaxStandardCAN2Filters;
uint8_t u8MaxExtendedCAN2Filters;


Please help fix the issues.

Thanks,
Rajeev

Comments:
[rxa1031 - 2020-10-08T07:33:15Z]
Hello,

Fix for: Gateway\Gateway.axf: Error: L6200E: Symbol vApplicationIdleHook multiply defined (by cmsis_os2.o and freertos.o).

When configUSE_IDLE_HOOK == 0 the function is only available in cmsis_os2.c and is not considered during compilation.

When configUSE_IDLE_HOOK == 1 the linker error is triggering.

Please remove below from cmsis_os2.c the cmsis_os2.c file.


#if (configUSE_IDLE_HOOK == 1)
__WEAK void vApplicationIdleHook (void){}
#endif

The function body is available in _Src\freertos.c starting from line number 78_

[ASELSTM - 2020-10-20T17:41:56Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this other report. It has been forwarded to our development teams. We will get back to you as soon as they provide us with their feedback.

Best regards,

[rxa1031 - 2021-03-23T05:26:16Z]
Hello @ALABSTM ,

Are there any findings / updates that you may be able to share with me.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[ASELSTM - 2021-07-15T08:36:16Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you once again for your contribution.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2021-07-15T08:36:39Z]
ST Internal Reference: 110640

[ASELSTM - 2021-10-20T11:19:39Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

The freertos.c is actually a user file. In order to clear the error, you need to remove __weak.

Please allow me then to close this thread as not in the scoop of STM32Cube firmware. Thank you for your comprehension and for your contribution.

With regards,

title: [H7] #68: HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT not used by Asynchronous UART / USART driver
paragraphs: Issue #68: HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT not used by Asynchronous UART / USART driver
Title: HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT not used by Asynchronous UART / USART driver
I am trying to implement Interrupt-dependent-Asynchronous-UART Receive-with-Timeout, and observe that searching for HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT only shows one line of code which is the macro declaration.
I observe below when searching for HAL_UART_StateTypeDef which is used in function HAL_UART_GetState():

* @brief HAL UART State definition
 * @note HAL UART State value is a combination of 2 different substates: gState and RxState (see @ref UART_State_Definition).
 *    - gState contains UART state information related to global Handle management
 *     and also information related to Tx operations.
 *     gState value coding follow below described bitmap :
 *     b7-b6 Error information
 *       00 : No Error
 *       01 : (Not Used)
 *       10 : Timeout
 *       11 : Error
 *    b5     Peripheral initialization status
 *        0  : Reset (Peripheral not initialized)
 *        1  : Init done (Peripheral not initialized. HAL UART Init function already called)

The words **"Peripheral not initialized"** present with explanation of bit b5 = 1 are confusing. I assume the UART RX related API is called only after the UART Driver is initialized, along with enabling required interrupts.

Also, based on above information I expect value of HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT should be 0x80U (i.e. bits [7:6] = 10) Without a clarity with bit b5 explanation I am not sure whether the HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT should be 0xA0U.
Please help me understand, why HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT is set to 0xA0U and places in code where it must have been used.

Initialization: static void MX_USART1_UART_Init(void)
{
/* USER CODE BEGIN USART1_Init 0 */ /* USER CODE END USART1_Init 0 */
Asynchronous USART Receive call: uint8_t ucTestKey[2] = " \0";
Please suggest a fix or a method for enabling Asynchronous UART Receive (interrupt) timeout.

Regards,
Rajeev Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-10-05T14:09:07Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Actually, the point you raised has already been raised in another issue #31. We have discussed this internally and we are working on the implementation of a service to enable Reception Timeout interrupt. A fix will be made available in the frame of a future release.
Regarding the second point, indeed, an error has been introduced in the comment which should rather be :

*    b5     Peripheral initialization status
 *        0  : Reset (Peripheral not initialized)

diff
-*        1  : Init done (Peripheral not initialized. HAL UART Init function already called)
+*        1  : Init done (Peripheral initialized. HAL UART Init function already called)
The same correction should be applied in the description of **RxState** bitmap.  Thus, HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT should be set to **0xA0U** and not to  0x80U since b5=1.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2020-10-05T14:10:01Z]
ST Internal Reference: 86997

[ASELSTM - 2020-10-05T14:22:19Z]
ST Internal Reference: 93918
I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.
Thank you again for having reported. With regards,
issue_title: HAL_UART_STATE_TIMEOUT not used by Asynchronous UART / USART driver

title: [H7] #67: STM32CubeH7 MDK-ARM Code behaves abnormally when MicroLIB is unselected
content:
Issue #67: STM32CubeH7 MDK-ARM Code behaves abnormally when MicroLIB is unselected

Title: STM32CubeH7 MDK-ARM Code behaves abnormally when MicroLIB is unselected

Body:
Hello,

STM32CubeH7 Keil Code behaves abnormally when MicroLIB is unselected. Currently using STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0.

Toolchain IDE: MDK-ARM (minimum version 5.27).

I have tried confirming the behavior both with Keil Compiler Version 5 and Keil Compiler Version 6.

This abnormal behavior is NOT OBSERVED when ULINK Pro debugger is used for debugging code. Hence, unable to find the reason for the issue.

Please help.

Regards,
Rajeev

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-07T16:01:14Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution. In order to allow a better analysis of this problem, could you please give us more details on how to reproduce it and could you please give us the name of the project that is used to reproduce this issue.  

Thank you again for your contribution.
 
With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-07T19:56:26Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

I am sharing both the IOC files that I use.

[STM32CubeMX_Issues.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5343423/STM32CubeMX_Issues.zip)

When MicroLIB is not selected and uGnu is 1 (along with uC99 being 1), the code may crash even before it jumps to main function.

I am not in position of share the generated code along with propitiatory code. But these IOC files should help with:

1. Confirming that MicroLIB setting is not set when code is generated for MDK-ARM (minimum version 5.27) 
2. MicroLIB if already checked in UVPROJX / UVOPTX file (with say Keil Compiler Version 6 selected) then on regenerating the code with help of IOC file the MicroLIB check box get deselected. (Same is the case when Keil Compiler Version 5 is selected).
3. In generated file UVPROJX, both uC99 and uGnu tags are set to 1. I believe that uGnu should be set to 0.
4. STM32CubeMX does not allow generating code which can be compiled using Keil Compiler version 6, so one needs to open UVPROJX and UVOPTX files to change folder name RVDS to GCC and then copy the FreeRTOS GCC\ARM_CM4F to appropriate path based on the path name which has the folder GCC name mentioned. The FreeRTOS code then compiles without issues with Keil Compiler Version 6. However on regenerating the code with help of IOC file he GCC folder is deleted and RVDS folder and its paths are added back to project.

You may call me on my mobile number or have a ZOOM call, in case you may want a live demo.

Thanks and regards,
@rxa1031

[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-08T08:53:35Z]
Hi @rxa1031, 

Thank you for this report. However, this is rather a Cube MX related issue and I am afraid we do not address it here. Please submit it to the **ST Community**, in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx). There you shall find people who will address your request and whom you will hopefully find a solution with.

Now, as this issue is not directly related to some software component published within this repository (CMSIS, HAL, BSP, etc.) but rather to our ecosystem (namely the Cube MX tool), please allow me to close it. 

Thank you for your contribution.  We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

title: [H7] #66: STM32CubeMX adds incorrect value to XTAL
content:
Issue #66: STM32CubeMX adds incorrect value to XTAL

Title: STM32CubeMX adds incorrect value to XTAL

Body:
Hello,

I have been observing this issue with previous as well as latest STM32CubeMX versions, wherein the generated MDK-ARM (minimum version 5.27) project file (UVPROJX) has incorrect value set to Options for Target -> Target -> Xtal (MHz).
In the IOC file I have informed that used XTAL value is 25 MHz.

In Clock configuration, I have set SYSCLK to 480 MHz and D1CPRE Prescaler to Divide by 1. thus FCLK is 480 MHz.

STM32CubeMX copying SYSCLK value to Xtal (MHz) is a BUG.

Please fix the bug.

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-02T08:24:38Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution. Our development teams confirmed that this issue is rather related to CubeMX. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to CubeMx tool in our GitHub repositories.

Please allow me to redirect you to our ST Community. You can report this issue in the [CubeMX related section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx). There, you will hopefully find people from our CubeMX development teams who will take in charge your request.

Thank you for your contribution.

With regards,

title: [H7] #65: STM32CubeMX 6.0.1 with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0 adds bad value for Keil UVPROJX file
content:
Issue #65: STM32CubeMX 6.0.1 with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0 adds bad value for Keil UVPROJX file

Title: STM32CubeMX 6.0.1 with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0 adds bad value for Keil UVPROJX file

Body:
Hello,

I am currently using STM32CubeMX version 6.0.1 with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.8.0. The generated code executes when using ULINKpro, but does not execute when Reset signal is given to microcontroller or Power to Microcontroller is recycled.

When I open a IOC project that was created with below STM32CubeMX version with STM32CubeMX latest version 6.0.1, it asks for migrating the project.

MxCube.Version=5.6.1
MxDb.Version=DB.5.0.60

Once I migrate the project I save and close it. Then open the IOC again and generate code.

Below **issue** is observed when Toolchain / IDE: **MDK-ARM** (minimum version 5.27) is selected and Source code is generated.

						<uC99>1</uC99>
						<uGnu>1</uGnu>


When the earlier STM32CubeMX (version 5.6.1 was in use along with STM32Cube MCU Package for STM32H7 Series version 1.7.0) the generated Keil UVPROJX file had below values:

						<uC99>1</uC99>
						<uGnu>0</uGnu>


Please fix the bug by ensuring that <uGnu>0</uGnu> is added to Keil UVPROJX file.

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-01T15:05:06Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Glad to read from you again. Thank you for your contribution. However, this issue is related to a generation problem with Cube MX. In fact, aspects related to Cube MX tool are not handled at GitHub level. They are rather treated at **ST Community** level, precisely within the Cube [MX](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx) dedicated section.

Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-01T15:08:41Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

Thanks for the information. I will share my concern on St Community.

Thanks,
@rxa1031

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-01T15:11:04Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM 

I am not sure whether closing this issue was a right step from my side.

Is there a way that this issue gets internally reported to appropriate team?

You may take appropriate action for this issue.

Regards,
@rxa1031

[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-01T17:17:01Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution. Allow me to close this ticket as it is out of the scope of this GitHub organization.

With regards,

title: [H7] #64: Incorrect value assigned to __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION and adding __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to a header file
content:
Issue #64: Incorrect value assigned to __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION and adding __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to a header file

Title: Incorrect value assigned to __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION and adding __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to a header file

Body:
Hello,

Please refer to below lines of code:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h7xx.h#L110-L113

Please change it to:

#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION        ((__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_MAIN     << 24)\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB1 << 16)\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB2 << 8 )\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_RC))


Also if possible please add __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to one of the header files so that the value can be used during conditional compile of C code.

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-02T10:19:23Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you for your contribution.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-02T10:28:40Z]
ST Internal Reference: 93826

[RKOUSTM - 2021-03-11T13:05:50Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.

Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,
paragraphs: Issue #64: Incorrect value assigned to __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION and adding __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to a header file
Title: Incorrect value assigned to __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION and adding __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to a header file

Please refer to below lines of code:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h7xx.h#L110-L113

#define __STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION        ((__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_MAIN     << 24)\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB1 << 16)\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_SUB2 << 8 )\
                                      |(__STM32H7xx_CMSIS_DEVICE_VERSION_RC))
Also if possible please add __STM32H7xx_HAL_VERSION to one of the header files so that the value can be used during conditional compile of C code.

Regards,
@rxa1031 Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-02T10:19:23Z]
Hi @rxa1031,
Thank you for this report. The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you for your contribution.

With regards, [RKOUSTM - 2020-10-02T10:28:40Z]
ST Internal Reference: 93826
I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.
Thank you again for having reported. With regards,

title: [H7] #63: REV_ID_Z missing from file stm32h7xx_hal.h
content:
Issue #63: REV_ID_Z missing from file stm32h7xx_hal.h

Title: REV_ID_Z missing from file stm32h7xx_hal.h

Body:
Hello,

The User reference manual of STM32H753 informs value DBGMCU->IDCODE == 0x1001 corresponds to Revision Z microcontroller. However the macro REV_ID_Z is missing from the header file.

Please add the macro.

Thanks,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-10-06T09:49:39Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. It will be forwarded to our development teams for analysis. We will be back to you as soon as we get feedback.

Thank you for your patience and thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[ASELSTM - 2020-10-06T10:27:47Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

These defines are used to customize the HAL behavior according to the current revision. In fact, Rev.Z behavior is same as Rev.Y this why REV_ID_Z has not been added in the  stm32h7xx_hal.h file.

More over, only **Revision Y** and **Revision V** are manufactured, other revisions are either internal or intermediate revisions. 

Please allow me then to close this thread. Thank you for your comprehension and thank you again for your contribution.
