title: [H7] #62: Requesting change to HAL_FDCAN_GetProtocolStatus() and FDCAN_COM_XXX macros
content:
Issue #62: Requesting change to HAL_FDCAN_GetProtocolStatus() and FDCAN_COM_XXX macros

Title: Requesting change to HAL_FDCAN_GetProtocolStatus() and FDCAN_COM_XXX macros

Body:
Hello,

Please review the function HAL_FDCAN_GetProtocolStatus() implementation and the values assigned to macros FDCAN_COM_STATE_SYNC, FDCAN_COM_STATE_IDLE, FDCAN_COM_STATE_RX, and FDCAN_COM_STATE_TX (henceforth referred to as FDCAN_COOM_XXX).

Some observations:
1. Within function HAL_FDCAN_GetProtocolStatus() body, only the assignment made to ProtocolStatus->Activity is not right shifted. I understand this implementation is correct based on current values for FDCAN_COM_XXX. However, the values do not coincide with the values in datasheet, and without an API document containing this information, coder / user using the API may assume Activity value to be incorrect.

Requests:
1. Could the values assigned to FDCAN_COM_XXX macros be changed so that the valid values are 0 through 3:

#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_SYNC ((uint32_t)0x00000000U) /*!< Node is synchronizing on CAN communication */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_IDLE ((uint32_t)0x00000001U) /*!< Node is neither receiver nor transmitter   */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_RX   ((uint32_t)0x00000002U) /*!< Node is operating as receiver              */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_TX   ((uint32_t)0x00000003U) /*!< Node is operating as transmitter           */


2. May you please change ProtocolStatus->Activity = (StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_ACT) to ProtocolStatus->Activity = (StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_ACT) >> FDCAN_PSR_ACT_Pos for ease of understanding.

Please share your views.

Thanks,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-05T15:39:22Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution.  Indeed, for stm32h7xx_hal_fdcan.c and  stm32h7xx_hal_fdcan.h driver code, there is no problem. As noted in the reference manual, the Activity bites that are used to monitors the module controller area network communication state are defined in the FDCAN protocol status register (FDCAN_PSR) as follows: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/63848590/95063098-ccf08100-06f5-11eb-8a39-d74e00cd57c4.png)

So, we don't need to shift these bits in the stm32h7xx_hal_fdcan.c file and the writing to the LSE[2:0] bits has no effect on the bit value. Since the values assigned to FDCAN_COM_XXX macros in the stm32h7xx_hal_fdcan.h file are already correctly set based on the following configuration the Bits 4:3 **ACT[1:0]**:
- 00: Synchronizing: node is synchronizing on CAN communication
- 01: Idle: node is neither receiver nor transmitter
- 10: Receiver: node is operating as receiver
- 11: Transmitter: node is operating as transmitter 

Please allow me then to close this thread. Thank you for your comprehension and thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-05T16:30:21Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM 

I am looking at structure FDCAN_ProtocolStatusTypeDef and analyzing which macros to use for anayzing variable values for variables like ProtocolStatus->BusOff, ProtocolStatus->ErrorPassive, ProtocolStatus->TDCvalue etc.

ST has provided @ref / references only for few variables like LastErrorCode, DataLastErrorCode, and Activity.

As per ST shared explanation for FDCAM_COM_XXX, one would try to look for different macros declared for LEC[2:0] and DLEC[2:0] with different values (based on bit position) even for same error, (which I do not observe.).

In my earlier shared concern, I am discussing in terms of _code readability and coder understanding_. I am NOT reporting a bug in existing code, but I expect a change for ease of reading and understanding for all coders.

I am also informing that ST is following two different styles for declaring macros and using them. _I am only requesting ST to use one style out of the two._

I am unable to understand why ST want to have a different style of "declaring macro and assignment sequence for ACT[1:0] bits position [4:3]" and another style for declaring macros and assignment for Data Last Error Code[2:0] bits position [10:8].

I believe only one of the below two approaches should be used:
1. Either do not right shift any value that is assigned to any variable present with structure FDCAN_ProtocolStatusTypeDef (variable name ProtocolStatus) and use the macros that declare values corresponding to the bits position in use. (example ProtocolStatus->Activity and current value assigned to FDCAN_COM_STATE_TX)
2. Or right shift the value before assigning to any variable present structure FDCAN_ProtocolStatusTypeDef (variable name ProtocolStatus) and use the macros that define the values (and not place values) for the bits (example ProtocolStatus->DataLastErrorCode and current value assigned to FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_NO_CHANGE)


Please refer to the below macros and code pieces and decide on its readability and understanding for a coder, assuming he / she does not refer to comments provided for the variables.


/** @defgroup FDCAN_protocol_error_code FDCAN protocol error code
  * @{
  */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_NONE      ((uint32_t)0x00000000U) /*!< No error occurred         */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_STUFF     ((uint32_t)0x00000001U) /*!< Stuff error               */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_FORM      ((uint32_t)0x00000002U) /*!< Form error                */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_ACK       ((uint32_t)0x00000003U) /*!< Acknowledge error         */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_BIT1      ((uint32_t)0x00000004U) /*!< Bit 1 (recessive) error   */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_BIT0      ((uint32_t)0x00000005U) /*!< Bit 0 (dominant) error    */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_CRC       ((uint32_t)0x00000006U) /*!< CRC check sum error       */
#define FDCAN_PROTOCOL_ERROR_NO_CHANGE ((uint32_t)0x00000007U) /*!< No change since last read */
/**
  * @}
  */

/** @defgroup FDCAN_communication_state FDCAN communication state
  * @{
  */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_SYNC ((uint32_t)0x00000000U) /*!< Node is synchronizing on CAN communication */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_IDLE ((uint32_t)0x00000008U) /*!< Node is neither receiver nor transmitter   */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_RX   ((uint32_t)0x00000010U) /*!< Node is operating as receiver              */
#define FDCAN_COM_STATE_TX   ((uint32_t)0x00000018U) /*!< Node is operating as transmitter           */
/**
  * @}
  */



  ProtocolStatus->LastErrorCode = (StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_LEC);
  ProtocolStatus->DataLastErrorCode = ((StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_DLEC) >> FDCAN_PSR_DLEC_Pos);
  ProtocolStatus->Activity = (StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_ACT);
...
  ProtocolStatus->ProtocolException = ((StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_PXE) >> FDCAN_PSR_PXE_Pos);
  ProtocolStatus->TDCvalue = ((StatusReg & FDCAN_PSR_TDCV) >> FDCAN_PSR_TDCV_Pos);


Regards,
Rajeev

title: [H7] #61: Max value for configMAX_PRIORITIES
content:
Issue #61: Max value for configMAX_PRIORITIES

Title: Max value for configMAX_PRIORITIES

Body:
Hi,

As per https://www.freertos.org/RTOS-task-priority.html:

> Each task is assigned a priority from 0 to ( configMAX_PRIORITIES – 1 )

It is informed under #59 that the reason to choose value 56 for configMAX_PRIORITIES is because the value corresponds to osPriorityISR.

However the above statement violates the FreeRTOS guideline stated above. Possibly, the value of configMAX_PRIORITIES  should be:

configMAX_PRIORITIES = osPriorityISR + 1

Please suggest.

Thanks,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-24T16:06:48Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your contribution. Please note, that FreeRTOSConfig_template.h configuration file contain:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/include/FreeRTOSConfig_template.h#L99-L107
Thus all user configurable priorities in CMSIS RTOS2 can be used, from osPriorityLow to osPriorityRealtime7.
I hope this answers your question. Unfortunately we don't treat aspect related to FreeRTOS in our GitHub repositories. As the Third_Party files are out of the scope of STMicroelectronics what about reporting this issue to the [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos)?

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-09-25T10:18:25Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM,

Does ST develope its own flavor of FreeRTOS which it bundles with STM32CubeMX. Where is the documentation for ST provided flavor of FreeRTOS?

I would like to have a copy of your document, so that I become aware of differences between your flavor of FreeRTOS and the FreeRTOS available on GitHub (Which also provided sample codes that execute on various STM32 series microcontrollers).

Regards,
@rxa1031

[CCASTM - 2020-10-28T08:14:41Z]
Hello rxa1031,

ST proposes the usage of FreeRTOS in the context of the STM32Cube ecosystem. As mentioned in the st_readme.txt (under the Source directory, beside the original readme.txt)  ST insures only the adaptation to the cmsis_os layer and the validation of our examples projects, for any problem related to the OS itself we suggest to  contact FreeRTOS supplier as proposed by  my colleague RKOUSTM.
You can find more information on the porting and the usage in the context of STM32Cube in the Application Note UM1722
https://www.st.com/resource/en/user_manual/dm00105262-developing-applications-on-stm32cube-with-rtos-stmicroelectronics.pdf

Regards
Christophe

title: [H7] #60: STM32CubeMX IDE issue. Throws warning when using macros
content:
Issue #60: STM32CubeMX IDE issue. Throws warning when using macros

Title: STM32CubeMX IDE issue. Throws warning when using macros

Body:
Hello,

Microcontroller used STM32H753BI

Issue: STM32CubeMX 6.0.0 Macro concerns

Case Number informed by ST (internal community): 00112804

I have still not heard back on this from ST Team and hence raising the concern here.

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-24T14:35:16Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

First, allow me to thank you for your contribution.

However, this issue is rather related to **CubeMX** than to the firmware published within this repository.
In fact, aspects related to CubeMX tool are not handled at GitHub level. To speed up the analysis procedure, this issue has been transferred to the CubeMx development teams for deeper analysis and to  obtain more details about the solution. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer. 

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-10-09T08:43:05Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

 Thank you for your contribution.  In order to allow a better understanding of the problem, could you please give us more details to allow the **CubeMX development teams** to reproduce this issue.

Thank you again for your contribution. 

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-09T13:37:59Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM ,

Please refer to below link for more details:

https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00000JhsU4SAJ/stm32cubemx-ide-issue-throws-warning-when-using-macros

I do not want to make modifications to the IOC file just for changing / updating number of Standard and extended filters that must be used. Similarly I do not want to make changes to IOC file just for updating UART baudrate, and hence using macros.

In case the macro is an alias for a variable, the variable is declared globally in Main.C file and desired value is updated to the variable before the Init Function / Function using macro is called.

Regards,
Rajeev

[rxa1031 - 2020-10-09T13:41:22Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM 

The reason I reported this as an issue is because there are two different behaviors shown by STM32CubeMX.

The correct behavior is shown by STM32CubeMX when the IOC file is open and user first adds the macro (like dRS485PowerSupplyBaudRate) and then assigns the macro to one of the property (i.e. to Baud Rate). If the IOC file is saved and code is generated without closing the project, then STM32CubeMX does not throw any error.

The same STM32CubeMX version shows a different and incorrect behavior when the project is closed and reopened. Now:

if one has an already defined macro, which one wants to assign to a property, the STM32CubeMX will not let you assign the macro name to the property (try this out with macro named MaxStdFIFOs). (This is different from behavior stated earlier).
If one generates code, the STM32CubeMX throws warning for Devices which have macro names assigned to properties. (Again this behavior is different from earlier stated behavior).
 
My concern is that when the STM32CubeMX allows me to add Macros and then assign them to a property, why can it not leave them as is and:
- Allow me use / assign the existing macro names even after I reopen project.
- Inform in the warning about which macro it is considering to be a concern, so that I may decide on whether to disregard the warning or abort code generation.

 
Regards,
Rajeev

[RKOUSTM - 2021-02-09T12:05:03Z]
Hi @rxa1031, 

Thank you for this report. This issue has been reported to the CubeMX team who will take in charge your request in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00000JhsU4SAJ/stm32cubemx-ide-issue-throws-warning-when-using-macros) of ST Community.

As this is a CubeMX-related issue and not directly related to the firmware published in this repository, please allow me to close it. Thank you for your comprehension and contribution.

With regards,

https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00000JhsU4SAJ/stm32cubemx-ide-issue-throws-warning-when-using-macros

I do not want to make modifications to the IOC file just for changing / updating number of Standard and extended filters that must be used. Similarly I do not want to make changes to IOC file just for updating UART baudrate, and hence using macros.
In case the macro is an alias for a variable, the variable is declared globally in Main.C file and desired value is updated to the variable before the Init Function / Function using macro is called.

Regards,
Rajeev [rxa1031 - 2020-10-09T13:41:22Z]
Hello @RKOUSTM
The reason I reported this as an issue is because there are two different behaviors shown by STM32CubeMX.

The correct behavior is shown by STM32CubeMX when the IOC file is open and user first adds the macro (like dRS485PowerSupplyBaudRate) and then assigns the macro to one of the property (i.e. to Baud Rate). If the IOC file is saved and code is generated without closing the project, then STM32CubeMX does not throw any error.
The same STM32CubeMX version shows a different and incorrect behavior when the project is closed and reopened. Now:

if one has an already defined macro, which one wants to assign to a property, the STM32CubeMX will not let you assign the macro name to the property (try this out with macro named MaxStdFIFOs). (This is different from behavior stated earlier).
If one generates code, the STM32CubeMX throws warning for Devices which have macro names assigned to properties. (Again this behavior is different from earlier stated behavior).
 
My concern is that when the STM32CubeMX allows me to add Macros and then assign them to a property, why can it not leave them as is and:
- Allow me use / assign the existing macro names even after I reopen project.
- Inform in the warning about which macro it is considering to be a concern, so that I may decide on whether to disregard the warning or abort code generation.

Regards,
Rajeev [RKOUSTM - 2021-02-09T12:05:03Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. This issue has been reported to the CubeMX team who will take in charge your request in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W00000JhsU4SAJ/stm32cubemx-ide-issue-throws-warning-when-using-macros) of ST Community.
As this is a CubeMX-related issue and not directly related to the firmware published in this repository, please allow me to close it. Thank you for your comprehension and contribution.
issue_title: STM32CubeMX IDE issue. Throws warning when using macros

title: [H7] #59: Issue with chosen MAX value (i.e. 56) of configMAX_PRIORITIES
content:
Issue #59: Issue with chosen MAX value (i.e. 56) of configMAX_PRIORITIES

Title: Issue with chosen MAX value (i.e. 56) of configMAX_PRIORITIES

Body:
Hello,

I was reviewing the FreeRTOS code and the CMSIS_OS2.C file and the macro configMAX_PRIORITIES

I assume the below statement is incorrect and I have a reason for it:


/*  CMSIS-RTOSv2 defines 56 levels of priorities. To be able to use them
 *  all and avoid application misbehavior, configUSE_PORT_OPTIMISED_TASK_SELECTION
 *  must be set to 0 and configMAX_PRIORITIES to 56
 *
 */


Even the declarations like below are incorrect:
#define configMAX_PRIORITIES                     ( 56 )

**Observation 1:**
The FreeRTOS code version 10.3.1 (and maybe earlier) have a file named blocktim.c at location FreeRTOSv10.3.1\FreeRTOS\Demo\Common\Minimal\blocktim.c
The file declares the macro:

#ifndef bktSECONDARY_PRIORITY
	#define bktSECONDARY_PRIORITY	( configMAX_PRIORITIES - 4 )
#endif


I am not sure whether STM32CubeMX is aware of the above file or whether it has a different file in which above macro is created.

**Observation 2:**
File CMSIS_OS2.C declares the enum named osPriority_t, with the last three enumerated values as:

  osPriorityISR           = 56,         ///< Reserved for ISR deferred thread.
  osPriorityError         = -1,         ///< System cannot determine priority or illegal priority.
  osPriorityReserved      = 0x7FFFFFFF  ///< Prevents enum down-size compiler optimization.



**Conclusion:**

In order to allow code being ported to other microcontrollers (maybe non ST Microcontrollers) or debugged using MSVC, changes must be made to code.

From observation 1, I believe all user implementations should only be allowed to use MAXIMUM PRIORITY value which is one less than bktSECONDARY_PRIORITY i.e.  configMAX_PRIORITIES - 5

From observation 2 and above conclusion, I believe the value assigned to configMAX_PRIORITIES should be osPriorityISR + 5


Please share your opinion.

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-23T14:08:46Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for this report. Unfortunately, for first point, we don't treat aspect related to FreeRTOS in our GitHub repositories. As the blocktim.c file is out of the scope of STMicroelectronics what about reporting this point to the [FreeRTOS GitHub](https://github.com/freertos)? Concerning the second point, the configMAX_PRIORITIES is set to 56 to support the osPriority in CMSIS V2  (see [this](https://www.keil.com/pack/doc/CMSIS_Dev/RTOS2/html/group__CMSIS__RTOS__ThreadMgmt.html) link).

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you for your contribution. 

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-09-23T14:49:05Z]
@RKOUSTM 

Thanks for sharing the response.

I understand the point that you shared.

The reason I reported the blocktim.c concern here is because I use Simulator (FreeRTOS\Demo\WIN32-MSVC\Win32.SLN) (and Visual Studio 2019) to sometimes debug my (STM32H753BIT6) code.
Also if one wants to port the STM32CubeMX / ST provided FreeRTOS code to any other microcontroller or simulator then there might become a concern, as the informed file may get used and the person porting the code will not be aware that configMAX_PRIORITIES will have to be updated.

Regards,
@rxa1031

title: [H7] #57: CMSIS_OS.C
content:
Issue #57: CMSIS_OS.C

Title: CMSIS_OS.C

Body:
Hello,

C:\STM32CubeMX\Repository\STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.8.0\Middlewares\Third_Party\FreeRTOS\Source\CMSIS_RTOS\CMSIS_OS.C

Line number 214
Code:

     if (xTaskCreate((TaskFunction_t)thread_def->pthread,(const portCHAR *)thread_def->name,
              thread_def->stacksize, argument, makeFreeRtosPriority(thread_def->tpriority),
              &handle) != pdPASS)  {


_**warning C4244**: 'function': conversion from 'const uint32_t' to 'const uint16_t', possible loss of data_

Other similar reporting:
[Similar issue link](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59161343/has-cmsis-os-c-of-stm32cubemx-to-be-fixed)

Regards,
@rxa1031

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-17T14:32:45Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

First of all we would like to thank you for your report. In order to allow a better understanding of the problem, could you please give us more details allowing our development teams to reproduce the warning? 

In fact, for the first issue related to use correct TimerCallbackFunction_t type for timer creation  mentioned in the [link](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59161343/has-cmsis-os-c-of-stm32cubemx-to-be-fixed), it is already fixed: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS/cmsis_os.c#L377-L390

Thank you for your contribution.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-09-18T04:20:18Z]
Hi @RKOUSTM ,

May you please help me with the version which has the fix for TimerCallbackFunction_t are these files a part of the code generated by latest STM32CubeMX?

I use STM32CubeMX to generate initialization and FreeRTOS base code for STM32H753BIT6. 

The **disadvantages** being that:
1. The STM32CubeMX imports the FreeRTOS version 10.2.1 with CMSIS-RTOS version 2.00 (and not FreeRTOS version 10.4.0)
2. FreeRTOS Kernel cannot be compiled using Keil Compiler version 6. (I did not get help on this from any source).
3. STM32CubeMX generated code uses the functions from **CMSIS_OS.C file** (which are rappers over the FreeRTOS functions) for creating tasks.

**Current scenario:**
I am trying to test a piece of legacy 16 bit RTXC code by porting it to STM32H7 and FreeRTOS. I found that I need more break points than that currently supported by Keil, so I switched to FreeRTOS 10.3.1 simulator (FreeRTOS\DEMO\Win32-MSVC\WIN32.sln).
I imported the CMSIS_OS.C and CMSIS_OS.H files (that I retrieved from one of the STM32 FreeRTOS examples that is generated by STM32CubeMX) to the FreeRTOS simulator project / code.

After compilation, I am observing the shared concern / _warning_ for xTaskCreate()
The third argument is of type _configSTACK_DEPTH_TYPE_ which is typedef as below:
	#define configSTACK_DEPTH_TYPE uint16_t

The _reason for the issue_ is that thread_def->stacksize is of type uint32_t, which is the right type when passing to xTaskCreateStatic, but is not the write type when passing to xTaskCreate. Either the two FreeRTOS functions need to update their API argument type (in which case it may be better to update the xTaskCreate argument type) (_For this a request must be raised from your side to FreeRTOS_) or otherwise typecase the variable while passing it to argument 3 of xTaskCreate.

I am UNABLE to use the latest released FreeRTOS version 10.4.0, unless STMicroelectronics immediately upgrade their STM32CubeMX to allow using FreeRTOS version 10.4.0. I have already been asking them to move to FreeRTOS version 10.3.1 for quite a long time now. The biggest issue with Replacing /updating the FreeRTOS files is that the moment someone opens the IOC (project file) in STM32CubeMX and regenerates the code, the FreeRTOS 10.4.0 files will again get overwritten with FreeRTOS 10.2.1 files.

Regards,
@rxa1031

[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-21T13:44:24Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

I hope you are doing well. First of all, the version V6.0.0 of the STM32CubeMX  supports the fix of TimerCallbackFunction_t type for timer creation which is used in the CMSIS_OS.c file. Secondly, concerning the issue related to the compilation error using Keil when switching to compiler generation 6, you should update the lines below:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS/cmsis_os.c#L62
 
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/CMSIS_RTOS/cmsis_os.c#L73

As follows:

diff
-#if   defined ( __CC_ARM )
+#if defined ( __CC_ARM ) || (defined(__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050))

 diff
-#if   defined ( __GNUC__ )
+#if defined (GNUC) && !defined (__CC_ARM) && !(defined(__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050))

Finally, to answer your question, the configSTACK_DEPTH_TYPE correspond to third argument  for xTaskCreate() to be defined in the FreeRTOSConfig.h file of your application. This update is already noted in the comment below:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/beced99ac090fece04d1e0eb6648b8075e156c6c/Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/include/FreeRTOS.h#L839-L843

Thank you for your contribution.

With regards,

[rxa1031 - 2020-09-21T15:17:50Z]
Hi,

Does the below line of code has a typo?

#if defined (GNUC) && !defined (__CC_ARM) && !(defined(__ARMCC_VERSION) && (__ARMCC_VERSION >= 6010050))

Probably github changed "Underscore Underscore GNUC Underscore Underscore" to just GNUC.

[FreeRTOS_ThreadCreation.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5256322/FreeRTOS_ThreadCreation.zip)
Attached code (for STM32H743I-EVAL board) does not compile when using Arm / Keil Compiler version 6 even after making above changes.

I tied with both c99 and gnu99 as the informed C Language under Options for Target -> C/C++(AC6)

Regards,
@rxa1031

[rxa1031 - 2020-09-21T15:26:00Z]
@RKOUSTM 
Please check below link:
https://github.com/FreeRTOS/FreeRTOS-Kernel/issues/154

and my previous message.

Regards,
@rxa1031

[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-22T10:48:09Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

Thank you for your interest. We are presently working on this. A fix will be implemented and made available in future release. We will keep you informed.

With regards

[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-22T10:49:00Z]
ST Internal Reference: 93121

[rxa1031 - 2021-03-23T05:25:36Z]
Hello @ALABSTM ,

Are there any findings / updates that you may be able to share with me.

Thanks,
Rajeev

[RKOUSTM - 2021-06-24T08:33:04Z]
Hi @rxa1031,

In order to support the ARM V6 for the FreeRTOS application, we need to use the GCC port files for Keil Compiler Version 6 as follows: 

- Including this path ../../../../../../Middlewares/Third_Party/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/GCC/ARM_CM4F in the options for target of your project.

- Updating the port.c file to use the GCC corresponding definitions that are required for Keil Compiler Version 6. This update is available on the FreeRTOS Kernel [V10.4.0](https://www.freertos.org/2020/09/freertos-kernel-v10-4-0-is-now-available.html) version.

Thank you again for your contribution. Regarding the point for which you opened the issue, as it is resolved, please allow me to close the issue.

With regards,
Rihab

title: [H7] #55: OB_USER_SWAP_BANK Undeclared on H750 Target
content:
Issue #55: OB_USER_SWAP_BANK Undeclared on H750 Target

Title: OB_USER_SWAP_BANK Undeclared on H750 Target

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 Custom STM32H750 Board
arm-none-eabi-gcc (GNU Arm Embedded Toolchain 9-2020-q2-update) 9.3.1 20200408 (release)

**Describe the bug**
OB_USER_SWAP_BANK is Undeclared in expansion of OB_USER_ALL, OB_USER_SWAP_BANK is only declared when DUAL_BANK is defined and since the H750 does not declare DUAL_CORE, FLASH_OPTSR_VDDMMC_HSLV, or FLASH_OPTSR2_TCM_AXI_SHARED the H750 uses the default Declaration of OB_USER_ALL which includes OB_USER_SWAP_BANK 
see https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/master/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_flash_ex.h#L656

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-03T17:36:03Z]
Hi @PGIII,

Thank you for having reported this point. This issue is confirmed by our development teams. A fix will be implemented and published in the frame of a future release. Thank you once more.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-09-03T17:36:08Z]
ST Internal Reference: 92119

[RKOUSTM - 2021-03-11T13:03:06Z]
Hi  @PGIII,
 
I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.

Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

title: [H7] #54: Unable to import CDC Device Examples for CubeH7.
content:
Issue #54: Unable to import CDC Device Examples for CubeH7.

Title: Unable to import CDC Device Examples for CubeH7.

Body:
Step 1 - Install STM32 Cube IDE

Step 2 - Install (this package) for the STM32H7, installed via the IDE - it downloads the software package.

Step 3 - Attempt to install ANY USB Device Project, there are two, named "CDC_Standalone" - 

SELECT: Import STM32 Cube Example

Under MCU, select STM32H7
Under Board, select Evaluation board
Under middleware, select: USB_Device

Select project name: CDC_Standalone,
Location states:  Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/USB_Device/CDC_Standalone
Description states: This application is part of the USB Device Library Package

Step 4: Click NEXT... project imports...

EXPECTED RESULT:  Source code would speak about "USB Device"

ACTUAL RESULT:  The HOST application is imported.

Step 5: Examine the project, you find that you have the HOST version instead.

NOTE: I do not think it matters, but I am running this on a MAC using the MAC version of tools.

Comments:
[duaneellissd - 2020-09-01T06:40:02Z]
NOTE: I think - I am not sure - that the problem is due to the HOST and DEVICE examples having the same name. These names should instead be unique.

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-07T15:46:27Z]
Hi @duaneellissd,

Thank you for this report. However, this is rather a Cube MX related issue and I am afraid we do not address it here. Please submit it to the **ST Community**, in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx). There you shall find people who will address your request and whom you will hopefully find a solution with.

Please allow me to close this issue now. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #51: Version control management
content:
Issue #51: Version control management

Title: Version control management

Body:
**Caution**
Version had not been managed in the proper way.
**Describe the set-up**
Version 1.7.0 is not backward compatible with 1.5.0. So as per version control system, major number should be increased in that scenario. So while upgrading the version of Cube package, user will be able to know whether to update or not.

**Additional context**
Semantic version control can be introduced for release.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2020-07-28T08:53:23Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

Thank you for your comment. May I ask you more details about the broken backward compatibility you mentioned? Would you mind list one or two examples of major changes you found causing compatibility break?

Thank you,

[NGXTech - 2020-07-28T09:24:08Z]
> Hi @NGXTech,
> 
> Thank you for your comment. May I ask you more details about the broken backward compatibility you mentioned? Would you mind list one or two examples of major changes you found causing compatibility break?
> 
> Thank you,

Hi,

In stm32h750xx.h, GPIO_TypeDef structure earlier version, there were two members each uint16_t named BSRRH & BSRRL but in latest version, there are only 1 register named BSRR. This is a breaking the compatibilty.

[ALABSTM - 2020-08-27T14:51:33Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

We compared the stm32h750xx.h file published in both **v1.5.0** and **v1.7.0** of the **STM32CubeH7** package but found no difference in the definition of the BSRR register.

Moreover, the stm32h750xx.h file has been **first** published in the frame of version **v1.5.0** of the firmware package and ever since, the definition of the BSRR register is the same (see below).
c
  __IO uint32_t BSRR;     /*!< GPIO port bit set/reset,               Address offset: 0x18      */

Would you mind checking again from you side?

Thank you,

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-07T09:52:02Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

Any feedback?

Thank you,

[NGXTech - 2020-09-07T10:04:07Z]
> Hi @NGXTech,
> 
> Any feedback?
> 
> Thank you,

Hi,

I have checked it, sorry for the mistake, if you check the CubeFW version 1.3.0, in that GPIO structure is defined as BSRRH and BSRRL.

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-17T16:29:26Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

May I ask you whether you experienced any issue at compilation- or run-time due to this update?

Examining the change I see it is **enclosed within** the body of the  function without any impact on its prototype as shown below. Such a change is **transparent** at user application-level and shall yield **no compatibility break**.

diff
void HAL_GPIO_WritePin(GPIO_TypeDef* GPIOx, uint16_t GPIO_Pin, GPIO_PinState PinState)
{
  /* Check the parameters */
  assert_param(IS_GPIO_PIN(GPIO_Pin));
  assert_param(IS_GPIO_PIN_ACTION(PinState));

  if(PinState != GPIO_PIN_RESET)
  {
+    GPIOx->BSRR = GPIO_Pin;
-    GPIOx->BSRRL = GPIO_Pin;
  }
  else
  {
+    GPIOx->BSRR = (uint32_t)GPIO_Pin << GPIO_NUMBER;
-    GPIOx->BSRRH = GPIO_Pin ;
  }
}


With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-10-12T10:46:07Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

Any feedback?

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-10-13T10:48:12Z]
Hi @NGXTech,

Please allow me to close this issue. You can reopen it if you have anything else relevant to add regarding this topic.

With regards,

title: [H7] #50: ADC example on stm32H745ZI gives DMA error
content:
Issue #50: ADC example on stm32H745ZI gives DMA error 

Title: ADC example on stm32H745ZI gives DMA error 

Body:
When I test the ADC DMA demo's on stm32H745 I only get a DMA error. Transfer error. I tried all kind of changes but do not get it working. Also other simple adc with dma fail on the same error. HAL_ADC_ConvCpltCallback is not called. I don't use the drivers from supplied by this example but the one's generated by cubemx in stm32CubeIDE.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35396688/87864179-6749ef80-c965-11ea-8f0f-2a6e3692eb98.png)

I have to change the clock frequency to 200 Mhz because of the power restriction with USB.

Comments:
[paulh002 - 2020-07-19T10:50:30Z]
I found the issue. The buffer ram was placed in the wrong area. had to change both linker memory settings. 
Put ram in 0x24.. not in 0x20..
 MEMORY
{
FLASH (rx)      : ORIGIN = 0x08000000, LENGTH = 1024K
RAM (xrw)      : ORIGIN = 0x24000000, LENGTH = 128K
ITCMRAM (xrw)      : ORIGIN = 0x00000000, LENGTH = 64K
}
paragraphs: Issue #50: ADC example on stm32H745ZI gives DMA error
Title: ADC example on stm32H745ZI gives DMA error

Body:
When I test the ADC DMA demo's on stm32H745 I only get a DMA error. Transfer error. I tried all kind of changes but do not get it working. Also other simple adc with dma fail on the same error. HAL_ADC_ConvCpltCallback is not called. I don't use the drivers from supplied by this example but the one's generated by cubemx in stm32CubeIDE.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35396688/87864179-6749ef80-c965-11ea-8f0f-2a6e3692eb98.png)
I have to change the clock frequency to 200 Mhz because of the power restriction with USB.

Put ram in 0x24.. not in 0x20..
 MEMORY
{

RAM (xrw)      : ORIGIN = 0x24000000, LENGTH = 128K

title: [H7] #47: ExtMem_Boot: hardfault caused by unaligned memory access
paragraphs: Issue #47: ExtMem_Boot: hardfault caused by unaligned memory access

Title: ExtMem_Boot: hardfault caused by unaligned memory access Body:
Hello!

I'm playing with the STM32h7B3I-DK with the ExtMem_Boot and the LedToggling application. 
I'm trying to load bootload the both DATA and CODE in the internal ram as follows:

#define DATA_AREA           USE_INTERNAL_SRAM  	//    //
#define CODE_AREA          USE_INTERNAL_SRAM		 // USE_EXTERNAL_SDRAM  //
#define BINARY_AREA        USE_SPI_NOR         // USE_SDMMC //

But after flashing both the boot and app binaries, the extboot application rise an hardfault caused by unaligned memory access when accesing the OSPI. While if I return in the original configuration with #define CODE_AREA  USE_EXTERNAL_SDRAM, everythings goes fine.
I do not know if it is caused by and error in the bootloader app or in the linking of the LedToggling app, heres how I updated the ld file of the LedToggling:

/* Used by the startup to initialize data */
  _sidata = LOADADDR(.data);

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-07-13T12:39:20Z]
Hi @Paullake85,
Thank you for reporting this issue. It will be forwarded to our development teams for analysis. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer. Thank you in advance for your patience.

With regards, [Paullake85 - 2020-07-14T06:45:23Z]
Hello!
I recently solved the problem because for make it working you must change:
SCB->VTOR = APPLICATION_ADDRESS | VECT_TAB_OFFSET; /* Vector Table Relocation to APPLICATION_ADDRESS in preprocessor defines */

Can you explain me a way to do this realocation from the bootloader?
Moreover I do not understand the value BINARY_SIZE 33000U, How much byte are? and how to set it?

Thx
Paul [ASELSTM - 2020-07-23T10:14:54Z]
Hi @Paullake85,
According to our development team, the configuration you have used

#define DATA_AREA USE_INTERNAL_SRAM
#define CODE_AREA USE_INTERNAL_SRAM

is not supported in the application. The available configurations are detailed in the readme file in the [Table 1. IAP implementation on STM32H7B3I-DISCO](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Projects/STM32H7B3I-DK/Applications/ExtMem_CodeExecution/ExtMem_Boot/readme.txt#L123).
In order to be able to run the application with the chosen configuration ,which is the use of the internal SRAM for both Code and Data area,  you have to perform some modification as following:
1. Identify different address for each zone for example :

#define INTERNAL_SRAM1  0x24010000UL  for data  
#define INTERNAL_SRAM   0x24000000UL  for code
2.  Insert the APPLICATION_ADDRESS to preprocessor defines and set an appropriate value
3. Copy the project.bin to OSPI memory and follow the same steps as BOOTROM example
Finally, to answer your question, the BINARY_SIZE correspond to the size of the generated project.bin of the application to be executed.

[Paullake85 - 2020-08-18T13:55:09Z]
Hello,
Thx for your replay. 
As I mentioned before the issue seems solved when I just modify:

SCB->VTOR = APPLICATION_ADDRESS | VECT_TAB_OFFSET; /* Vector Table Relocation to APPLICATION_ADDRESS in preprocessor defines */
in the system_stm32h7xx.c

What do you mean that I have define:
#define INTERNAL_SRAM1  0x24010000UL  for data  
#define INTERNAL_SRAM   0x24000000UL  for code
Do you mean that I have to define the new data address in the LD file for the linker? or should I modify the application system_stm32h7xx.c or the bootloader?

Finally, I would like to apply this bootloader to bigger binary size, unfortunately the resulting binary of the new application is too big to be flashed at 0x90000000, even after using the NOLOAD, is it possible to force the programmer to flash the HEX format at 0x90000000 (the hex is in interal ram space)

Thank you,
Paolo [ASELSTM - 2020-08-24T16:18:11Z]
Hi @Paullake85,

First, the dedicated address for the data and the code should be different if you use the same internal SRAM (0x24000000) for both data and code execution and it should be added within the memory.h file.

Second , you can change binary size manually within memory.h file or use the **STM32CubeProgrammer** to load the binary within the OSPI memory (0x90000000) with the dedicated memory flash loader mentioned in the readme file of the application.

Hope this would help you.
With regards, [Paullake85 - 2020-08-26T06:45:08Z]
Hi!
My problem is that the binary size of the application is too big (about 1.6GB) to be loaded in the flash.

[ASELSTM - 2020-08-27T13:22:03Z]
Hi @Paullake85,

Unfortunately,  there's no possibility to force the programmer to flash the HEX  if the binary size of the application is too big to be loaded in the flash. You have to use another memory with greater size to load the entire code.

With regards, [ASELSTM - 2020-09-24T07:47:11Z]
Hi @Paullake85,
Please allow me to close this thread as no activity has been registered since a month. You can reopen it at any time if you need other information. Thank you for your comprehension.
issue_title: ExtMem_Boot: hardfault caused by unaligned memory access

title: [H7] #46: HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig not setting the HRTIM1 clock source
paragraphs: Issue #46: HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig not setting the HRTIM1 clock source
Title: HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig not setting the HRTIM1 clock source

Body:
STM32 Nucleo-144 for STM32H743
Cube IDE 1.3.0 with STM32CubeH7 1.7.0

Description:
Using HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig with PeriphClkInitStruct.Hrtim1ClockSelection = RCC_HRTIM1CLK_CPUCLK; with the intent of setting the clock source for HRTIM1 peripheral to the CPU clock has no effect.

The problem seems to originate in stm32h7xx_hal_rcc_ex.c in line 1474. The function tries to check if the HRTIM1 peripheral does exist but it uses the wrong non-existing define HRTIM for that purpose:
The file stm32h743xx.h defines HRTIM1 in line 2393. Changing the above code to use this define instead fixes the problem. The right clock source gets applied by HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2020-07-01T13:30:21Z]
Hi @andigandi,
Thank you for this detailed report. It will be forwarded to our development teams. I will get back to you as soon as I get their answer.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2020-08-06T12:56:22Z]
ST Internal Reference: 81500

[ALABSTM - 2020-08-06T12:58:27Z]
Hi @andigandi,
Thank you for your report. The issue you reported has been already fixed and the fix is already available in the frame of version **v1.8.0** of the STM32CubeH7 firmware package.
issue_title: HAL_RCCEx_PeriphCLKConfig not setting the HRTIM1 clock source

title: [H7] #45: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)
content:
Issue #45: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)

Title: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)

Body:
At register level selecting ADC trigger event is done thru EXTSEL with many internal sources (timers) and one external (EXTI Line 11). Consistent approach is in LL API, where selecting trigger source by "LL_ADC_REG_SetTriggerSource" function or thru LL_ADC_REG_InitTypeDef TriggerSource. But in CubeMX is that configuration splited in two places with confusing labels.

![menu1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20212110/85111553-c99fbb00-b214-11ea-98b5-ff93f3f12e75.jpg)
![menu2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20212110/85111557-cad0e800-b214-11ea-9a35-c37b02b63d2c.jpg)

If i want to select internal triggers i have to select literaly "Conversion trigger Disable" (realy confusing because by this option in reality i am enabling internal trigger source selections - or in other words i am disabling only one specific trigger source (EXTI)). In addition internal trigger sources are called "External Trigger Conversion Sources". I suppose that both these options should be merged into one configuration field to correspond with logic of reference manual... 

version of CubeMX 5.6.1

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-06-19T10:27:34Z]
Hi @gripennn,

Thank you for your contribution. According to our analysis, this issue is rather related to CubeMX, may I ask you to submit it to the ST Community in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx)? There, you will hopefully find people from our CubeMX development teams who will take in charge your request.

Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,
paragraphs: Issue #45: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)
Title: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)

Body:
At register level selecting ADC trigger event is done thru EXTSEL with many internal sources (timers) and one external (EXTI Line 11). Consistent approach is in LL API, where selecting trigger source by "LL_ADC_REG_SetTriggerSource" function or thru LL_ADC_REG_InitTypeDef TriggerSource. But in CubeMX is that configuration splited in two places with confusing labels.

![menu1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20212110/85111553-c99fbb00-b214-11ea-98b5-ff93f3f12e75.jpg)
![menu2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20212110/85111557-cad0e800-b214-11ea-9a35-c37b02b63d2c.jpg)

If i want to select internal triggers i have to select literaly "Conversion trigger Disable" (realy confusing because by this option in reality i am enabling internal trigger source selections - or in other words i am disabling only one specific trigger source (EXTI)). In addition internal trigger sources are called "External Trigger Conversion Sources". I suppose that both these options should be merged into one configuration field to correspond with logic of reference manual...

version of CubeMX 5.6.1 Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-06-19T10:27:34Z]
Hi @gripennn,

Thank you for your contribution. According to our analysis, this issue is rather related to CubeMX, may I ask you to submit it to the ST Community in the dedicated [section](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BTr8WAG/stm32cubemx)? There, you will hopefully find people from our CubeMX development teams who will take in charge your request.
Thank you again for your contribution. With regards,
issue_title: Confusing configuration of ADC trigger at CubeMX (STM32H750)

title: [H7] #43: USART fails to compile
content:
Issue #43: USART fails to compile

Title: USART fails to compile

Body:
**Caution**
The Issues are strictly limited for the reporting of problem encountered with the software provided in this project.
For any other problem related to the STM32 product, the performance, the hardware characteristics and boards, the tools the environment in general, please post a topic in the [ST Community/STM32 MCUs forum](https://community.st.com/s/group/0F90X000000AXsASAW/stm32-mcus) 

**Describe the set-up**
 * Custom board with STM32H743
 * STM32CubeIDE 1.3.0
 * STM32CubeMX 5.6.1

**Describe the bug**
Compiling stm32h7xx_hal_usart.c fails

**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project
Use USART1 as Synchronous master

2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)
USART1

3. The use case that generates the problem
Compiling

4. How we can reproduce the problem
STM32CubeMX 5.6.1
- Start new project fot STM32H743VITx
- From Connectivity, add USART1 as Synchronus master
- In Project Manager, give name, select STM32CubeIDE
- Firmware package is STM32Cube FW_H7 V1.7.0
- Generate code
STM32CubeIDE 1.3.0
- Build project
Gives:
../Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_usart.c: In function 'HAL_USART_IRQHandler':
../Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_usart.c:2153:7: warning: implicit declaration of function '__HAL_UART_CLEAR_FLAG'; did you mean '__HAL_USART_CLEAR_FLAG'? [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
       __HAL_UART_CLEAR_FLAG(husart, UART_CLEAR_RTOF);
       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
       __HAL_USART_CLEAR_FLAG
../Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_usart.c:2153:37: error: 'UART_CLEAR_RTOF' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'USART_CLEAR_RTOF'?
       __HAL_UART_CLEAR_FLAG(husart, UART_CLEAR_RTOF);
                                     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                                     USART_CLEAR_RTOF



**Additional context**
Pull-request will follow, it seems to be just a typo.

**Screenshots**
None

Comments:
[ASELSTM - 2020-06-12T16:34:50Z]
Hi @fnadeau,

Thank you for your contribution.

Actually, we are already aware of this issue and have fixed it internally. However, the fix has not been published yet. It is scheduled for next release.

Since the issue is already fixed and planned for the next release, please allow me to close this issue. Thank you for your comprehension.

Best regards,

title: [H7] #42: Missing include in eth.c
content:
Issue #42: Missing include in eth.c

Title: Missing include in eth.c

Body:
How to:
- STM32CubeMX used to generate NUCLEO-H743ZI basic setup with Ethernet
- Generate code for Makefile project using make clean all

Result:
- Compiling the generated project will generate warnings concerning memset function

Solution:
- Add <string.h> to the eth.c file

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2020-07-21T08:01:22Z]
Hi @rolandvs,

Thank you for this report. We could find no file named eth.c. May I ask you the complete name of the file and where exactly the call to memset() could be found?

Thank you,

[ALABSTM - 2020-08-03T15:13:09Z]
Hi @rolandvs,

Any feedback?

Thank you,

[ALABSTM - 2020-08-26T12:57:48Z]
Hi @rolandvs,

I hope you are fine. As there has been no feedback since more than a month, please allow me to close this issue. You can reopen it in case you think it is still relevant.

With regards,

[rolandvs - 2020-09-09T20:03:23Z]
Dear @ALABSTM ,
I was preoccupied in some projects so it took me a while, but just generated a project to show you the "problem" I reported earlier.

I have included the  [GENERIC.ioc.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/5197718/GENERIC.ioc.zip)

It is a makefile project and compiling it reveals the missing string.h...


Core/Src/eth.c: In function 'MX_ETH_Init':
Core/Src/eth.c:79:3: warning: implicit declaration of function 'memset' [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
   79 |   memset(&TxConfig, 0 , sizeof(ETH_TxPacketConfig));
      |   ^~~~~~
Core/Src/eth.c:79:3: warning: incompatible implicit declaration of built-in function 'memset'
Core/Src/eth.c:22:1: note: include '<string.h>' or provide a declaration of 'memset'
   21 | #include "eth.h"
  +++ |+#include <string.h>
   22 |

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-16T13:40:21Z]
Dear @rolandvs,

Good to read your message. Thank you for these extra details. The issue is reopened and will be forwarded to our development teams. I will get back to you as soon as they provide me with feedback.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-28T15:16:08Z]
Hi @rolandvs,

Our development teams confirmed what you pointed out. This is a Cube MX-related issue. It will be fixed and made available in a future Cube MX release.

Now, as this issue is not directly related to some software component published within this repository (CMSIS, HAL, BSP, etc.) but rather to our ecosystem (namely the Cube MX tool), please allow me to close it.

Thank you again for your contribution and thank you for your patience. We are looking forward to reading from you again.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-09-28T15:17:01Z]
ST Internal Reference: 82323

title: [H7] #41: Uninitialized local variable VidCfg
content:
Issue #41: Uninitialized local variable VidCfg

Title: Uninitialized local variable VidCfg

Body:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Drivers/BSP/STM32H747I-DISCO/stm32h747i_discovery_lcd.c#L703

Sometimes might cause undefined behaviour of DSI host controller (incorrect configuration),
especially might happen when **stack** area is situated out of ZI-section.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-06-17T14:40:11Z]
Hi @cornway,

Thank you for reporting this issue. 

In order to allow a better understanding of the problem, could you please describe how you did to detect this issue and how to reproduce it, so that our development teams can give you an answer.

With regards,

[cornway - 2020-06-18T10:53:25Z]
Hi @RKOUSTM , that issue happened during custom project developing.
Project is totally based on Stm32 Cube layers, for my case STR was as following :

> Keil MDK IDE, using custom scatter file (used by linker), -O0 -Otime, everything else is default
> Mark STACK area as UNINIT in scatter file, any ram region can be used
> Build project

In case when produced image size is lower than 130kbytes - no issue present,
otherwise - DSI is configured to fill frame buffer with colour bar, due garbage in  VidCfg variable (in uninitialized fields exactly).

So i don't have a STR based on template projects provided with H7 Cube library, but i suppose -
such issue can occur when at least one condition met - STACK should be uninitialized.

Note: for my case the fix was simple - just do DSI_VidCfgTypeDef VidCfg = {0};

Thank, best regards.

[RKOUSTM - 2020-06-22T09:40:18Z]
Hi @cornway ,

Thank you for your contribution. You request will be forwarded to the development team in charge of BSP driver. I will let you updated once they provide me with their answer. Thank you for your patience and thank you for your message once again.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-07-02T15:55:40Z]
Hi @cornway,

The issue you pointed out has been confirmed, a fix will be implemented and made available in future release. Thank you once again for your contribution.

With regards,

[RKOUSTM - 2020-07-02T15:55:50Z]
ST Internal Reference: 89424

[RKOUSTM - 2020-07-06T15:08:14Z]
Hi @cornway,

Thank you for your suggestion and contribution.  According to our development teams, the issue should be related to the non-initialization of the **FrameBTAAcknowledgeEnable** parammeter in **VidCfg** structure which is declared at the following BSP weak function: 

- ___weak HAL_StatusTypeDef MX_DSIHOST_DSI_Init(DSI_HandleTypeDef *hdsi, uint32_t Width, uint32_t Height, uint32_t PixelFormat)._

As consequence this field can take any value and can lead to the inspect behavior. A suggested solution for this code below : 

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Drivers/BSP/STM32H747I-DISCO/stm32h747i_discovery_lcd.c#L747-L750

will be published as soon in the future release looks like the following:

diff
  VidCfg.LPVerticalBackPorchEnable     = DSI_LP_VBP_ENABLE;
  VidCfg.LPVerticalSyncActiveEnable    = DSI_LP_VSYNC_ENABLE;
+  VidCfg.FrameBTAAcknowledgeEnable    = DSI_FBTAA_DISABLE;

  if (HAL_DSI_ConfigVideoMode(hdsi, &VidCfg) != HAL_OK)


Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[cornway - 2020-07-07T10:29:50Z]
You're welcome!
Regards.

[RKOUSTM - 2021-03-11T13:01:16Z]
Hi @cornway,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version v1.9.0 of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub.

Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

title: [H7] #39: FreeRTOS-MPU fails to run if its privileged RAM is in a cacheable memory region
content: Issue #39: FreeRTOS-MPU fails to run if its privileged RAM is in a cacheable memory region Title: FreeRTOS-MPU fails to run if its privileged RAM is in a cacheable memory region Body: ## The setup - Hardware: Nucleo-H743ZI2 - IDE: IAR for Arm v8.32.1 ## The bug FreeRTOS-MPU faults during initialization because of a cache coherency issue with pxCurrentTCB; allocated in the Privileged RAM region. The SDK example only works because the Privileged RAM section is allocated in DTCM, a non-cacheable memory region. To solve it, that memory region should be set as non-cacheable in the MPU. That way it can be allocated in cacheable memory regions. In more technical detail: - FreeRTOS-MPU allocates pxCurrentTCB in the privileged RAM section. - During xPortStartScheduler, the privileged RAM section is allocated in the MPU as cacheable - As a result, if the DCache is enabled, pxCurrentTCB will be cached. - Cache coherency is lost during the initialization of the RTOS' scheduler, the assembly routine vPortRestoreContextOfFirstTask attempts to access a Reserved memory region, and results in a hard fault ## How To Reproduce 0. Grab the FreeRTOS-MPU sample application from the SDK 1. Fix the FreeRTOS-MPU bug described in issue #38 so the MPU is enabled 2. Update the linker script and main.c to allocate the Privileged Data section into AXI ram instead of DTCM 3. If you're using different hardware than me, make sure the DCache is enabled and has access to the AXI SRAM region you set up in the previous step 4. Try to run the application, and you should get a crash right away. Or perhaps some other serious misbehavior, depending on which incorrect values are loaded into your registers from vPortRestoreContextOfFirstTask ## Additional context For a minimum viable fix, a couple of things need to be updated in each FreeRTOS-MPU port affected by this bug: 1. portmacro.h needs to support "normal, shareable, non-cacheable" configurations of MPU->RASR registers. That's TEX=001 S=1 B=0 C=0 according to [the docs](https://developer.arm.com/docs/dui0646/a/cortex-m7-peripherals/optional-memory-protection-unit/mpu-access-permission-attributes#BIHHEDAE): #define portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ( 0x0cUL << 16UL ) 2. Privileged RAM needs to be configured using that portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE **instead of** portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE. - In prvSetupMPU: portMPU_REGION_ATTRIBUTE_REG = ( portMPU_REGION_PRIVILEGED_READ_WRITE ) | #if 0 /* Original code */ ( portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE ) | #else /* fix */ ( portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ) | #endif prvGetMPURegionSizeSetting( ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_end__ - ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_start__ ) | ( portMPU_REGION_ENABLE ); - In vPortStoreTaskMPUSettings: xMPUSettings->xRegion[ 1 ].ulRegionAttribute = ( portMPU_REGION_PRIVILEGED_READ_WRITE ) | #if 0 /* Original code */ ( portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE ) | #else /* fix */ ( portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ) | #endif prvGetMPURegionSizeSetting( ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_end__ - ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_start__ ) | ( portMPU_REGION_ENABLE ); Alternatively, memory barriers could be used to ensure the coherency of pxCurrentTCB, but I was not successful in my attempts, and I'm not sure if other FreeRTOS variables would still be affected in more insidious ways. ## More context The fix described earlier got me past this problem, but I'm still running into other crashes shortly after. These may or may not have related causes; I'm still investigating. If you uncover any other issues while fixing this one, a heads-up would be greatly appreciated. Thanks, JC Comments: [Palantir555 - 2020-05-25T16:12:18Z] Heads-up: I'm still finding this problem. The fix mentioned above does not seem to be working -it apparently did at first, so it must have been a problem in my debug setup. I'm still investigating the cause and solution, so any insight would be appreciated. The description of the bug and replication steps remain the same. [RKOUSTM - 2020-05-27T14:55:52Z] Hi @Palantir555, Nice to hear from you again. Thank you for these details. You message will be forwarded to our development teams for deeper analysis. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer. Thank you for your patience and thank you, once more, for having reported the issue. With regards, [RKOUSTM - 2020-06-01T15:26:17Z] ST Internal Reference: 87360 [Palantir555 - 2020-06-15T10:54:21Z] Hi there, I understand these problems take time, but any chance you've made progress on this? Do you have any hints on what the root of the problem is? This issue is halting progress on a couple of our projects, and we can't wait until the next SDK release. I can fix the issue myself if you can help me find it. paragraphs: Issue #39: FreeRTOS-MPU fails to run if its privileged RAM is in a cacheable memory region
Title: FreeRTOS-MPU fails to run if its privileged RAM is in a cacheable memory region

Body:
## The setup - Hardware: Nucleo-H743ZI2
- IDE: IAR for Arm v8.32.1
FreeRTOS-MPU faults during initialization because of a cache coherency issue with pxCurrentTCB; allocated in the Privileged RAM region.
The SDK example only works because the Privileged RAM section is allocated in DTCM, a non-cacheable memory region.
To solve it, that memory region should be set as non-cacheable in the MPU. That way it can be allocated in cacheable memory regions.

- FreeRTOS-MPU allocates pxCurrentTCB in the privileged RAM section.
- During xPortStartScheduler, the privileged RAM section is allocated in the MPU as cacheable
- As a result, if the DCache is enabled, pxCurrentTCB will be cached.
- Cache coherency is lost during the initialization of the RTOS' scheduler, the assembly routine vPortRestoreContextOfFirstTask attempts to access a Reserved memory region, and results in a hard fault

0. Grab the FreeRTOS-MPU sample application from the SDK
1. Fix the FreeRTOS-MPU bug described in issue #38 so the MPU is enabled
2. Update the linker script and main.c to allocate the Privileged Data section into AXI ram instead of DTCM
3. If you're using different hardware than me, make sure the DCache is enabled and has access to the AXI SRAM region you set up in the previous step
4. Try to run the application, and you should get a crash right away. Or perhaps some other serious misbehavior, depending on which incorrect values are loaded into your registers from vPortRestoreContextOfFirstTask
For a minimum viable fix, a couple of things need to be updated in each FreeRTOS-MPU port affected by this bug:

1. portmacro.h needs to support "normal, shareable, non-cacheable" configurations of MPU->RASR registers. That's TEX=001 S=1 B=0 C=0 according to [the docs](https://developer.arm.com/docs/dui0646/a/cortex-m7-peripherals/optional-memory-protection-unit/mpu-access-permission-attributes#BIHHEDAE):
    #define portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ( 0x0cUL << 16UL )
2. Privileged RAM needs to be configured using that portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE **instead of** portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE.
    - In prvSetupMPU:
    
         portMPU_REGION_ATTRIBUTE_REG =  ( portMPU_REGION_PRIVILEGED_READ_WRITE ) |
    #if 0 /* Original code */
                                         ( portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE ) |
    #else /* fix */
                                         ( portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ) |
    #endif
                                         prvGetMPURegionSizeSetting( ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_end__ - ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_start__ ) |
                                         ( portMPU_REGION_ENABLE );
    
    - In vPortStoreTaskMPUSettings:
    
        xMPUSettings->xRegion[ 1 ].ulRegionAttribute =
                ( portMPU_REGION_PRIVILEGED_READ_WRITE ) |
    #if 0 /* Original code */
                ( portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE ) |
    #else /* fix */
                ( portMPU_REGION_SHAREABLE_NON_CACHEABLE ) |
    #endif
                prvGetMPURegionSizeSetting( ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_end__ - ( uint32_t ) __privileged_data_start__ ) |
                ( portMPU_REGION_ENABLE );
    
Alternatively, memory barriers could be used to ensure the coherency of pxCurrentTCB, but I was not successful in my attempts, and I'm not sure if other FreeRTOS variables would still be affected in more insidious ways.
The fix described earlier got me past this problem, but I'm still running into other crashes shortly after. These may or may not have related causes; I'm still investigating.
If you uncover any other issues while fixing this one, a heads-up would be greatly appreciated.

The description of the bug and replication steps remain the same.

Thanks for your time,
JC [rahmanih - 2020-06-15T14:36:11Z]
Hi @Palantir555,
Is it possible to share with us a "sample" project reproducing the issue?

[Palantir555 - 2020-06-15T18:47:15Z]
Sure thing, appreciate the quick response.
I've reproduced the issue in [my fork (branch reproduce_freertos_cache_problem)](https://github.com/demux-labs/STM32CubeH7/tree/reproduce_freertos_cache_problem)
Please check the commit history to see the changes it required.

1. I had to move (copy) the ARM_CM4_MPU port folder to the SDK's root dir, because the relative path to portmacro.h was too long for IAR's compiler (🤦‍♂️)
2. Implemented the change described in #38
3. Copied the FreeRTOS_MPU example to [demux_FreeRTOS_MPU](https://github.com/demux-labs/STM32CubeH7/tree/reproduce_freertos_cache_problem/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/FreeRTOS/demux_FreeRTOS_MPU), updated its linker config and main.c to move all data to AXI

Thanks,
JC [rahmanih - 2020-06-17T14:04:54Z]
Issue reproduced!

looking at it, get back to you as soon as possible. regards
Haithem.

[rahmanih - 2020-07-02T18:30:55Z]
Hi @Palantir555,
root cause found and issue fixed. it is indeed a bug in FreeRTOS kernel. to apply the fix, do the following: in the IAR/ARM_CM4_MPU/portmacro.h replace the code:
#define portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE						( 0x07UL << 16UL ) with
#define portMPU_REGION_CACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE						( 0x03UL << 16UL )
the original code sets the 'S' bit to 1 which disables the cache, this is the root cause of the issue.

could try and let us know? regards
haithem.

[Palantir555 - 2020-07-03T15:22:04Z]
Hey Haithem, that's great news! Thanks a lot for your time and support.

I've run the example from my fork using your fix and it seems to be working correctly. When I run our more advanced codebase (LwIP included), it still crashes with a memory error, but it's different. It could be completely unrelated. Let me look further into that and report back.

BTW, you mentioned that enabling 'S' enabled the cache. Could you please elaborate on that?  If I'm understanding [this table](https://developer.arm.com/documentation/dui0646/a/cortex-m7-peripherals/optional-memory-protection-unit/mpu-access-permission-attributes#ftn.CIHIEJAJ) correctly, in that scenario 'S' only affects the shareability of the region; not its cacheability. I'm trying to figure out whether I misunderstood something, or if I'm looking at the wrong piece of documentation.

Thanks again for your effort! It's really appreciated. Cheers,
JC

[rahmanih - 2020-07-03T19:33:36Z]
the Cache L1 is per core, and can't be shared with other cores, thus enabling the 'S' bit leads to cache disablement.

regards
Haithem. [rahmanih - 2020-07-06T07:21:42Z]
Hi @RKOUSTM,

this is resolved now, we can close this ticket. regards
Haithem.

[ALABSTM - 2020-07-27T11:07:13Z]
Ticket reopened. Will be closed once the fix is published.

[RKOUSTM - 2021-06-23T14:27:31Z]
Hi @Palantir555, I hope you are fine.
The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of FreeRTOS Kernel V10.3.1. In addition, this version of FreeRTOS is integrated on the STM32CubeH7 V1.9.0 published on GitHub.
Thank you again for having reported. With regards,

title: [H7] #38: FreeRTOS-MPU does not use the MPU on M7 cores
content:
Issue #38: FreeRTOS-MPU does not use the MPU on M7 cores

Title: FreeRTOS-MPU does not use the MPU on M7 cores

Body:
I've reported this bug through your [forums](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W000003yAyDSAU/unable-to-set-up-noncacheable-mpu-regions-for-lwipethernet-when-using-freertosmpu-on-stm32h743zi) and the support portal. Feel free to close the issue if it's already been escalated/solved internally.

- Hardware: Nucleo-H743ZI2
- IDE: IAR for Arm v8.32.1

All M7 cores are affected, regardless of the compiler used.

## The bug

The FreeRTOS-MPU ports provided for M7 cores (ARM_CM4_MPU and ARM_CM7_MPU), and possibly others, do not actually use the MPU.

From ARM_CM4_MPU/port.c:


    #define portEXPECTED_MPU_TYPE_VALUE             ( 8UL << 8UL ) /* 8 regions, unified. */


M7 cores support 16 MPU regions, so that MPU->TYPE value is incorrect. As a result, the port silently fails to initialize the memory protection unit.

The fix:


    #if defined(CORE_CM4)
        #define portEXPECTED_MPU_TYPE_VALUE         ( 8UL << 8UL ) /* 8 regions, unified. */
    #else
        #define portEXPECTED_MPU_TYPE_VALUE         ( 16UL << 8UL ) /* 16 regions, unified. */
    #endif /* defined(CORE_CM4) */


That #if defined(CORE_CM4) is in line with the implementation at stm32h7xx_hal_cortex.h

## How to reproduce

1. Run the FreeRTOS-MPU example from the SDK on an M7 MCU
2. Use your debugger of choice to check the MPU->CTRL register. It will show that the MPU is disabled
3. Implement the fix mentioned above, and MPU->CTRL will show the MPU is now enabled, as expected

## After this fix

After fixing this bug, a different one came up. When I try to use FreeRTOS-MPU and the MPU HAL on the same app, I run into a crash within the assembly routine vPortRestoreContextOfFirstTask of the ARM_CM4_MPU port. I'm still debugging that new one, and awaiting response from your support guys.

If you'd like any more info, let me know.

Comments:
[RKOUSTM - 2020-05-14T09:37:29Z]
Hi @Palantir555

Thank you for your contribution. Could you please confirm you are using the latest firmware version (STM32Cube H7 v1.7.0) and that the issue is systematically reproducible on your side?

With regards,

[Palantir555 - 2020-05-14T13:56:10Z]
Yeah, I'm at 1.7.0 (HEAD of master) at the moment.

The issue is 100% reproducible.

[RKOUSTM - 2020-05-14T14:27:40Z]
Hi @Palantir555,

Thank you for having reported this point. It will be forwarded to our development teams for analysis. We will be back to you as soon as we get feedback.

Thank you for your patience and thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[Palantir555 - 2020-05-25T16:42:49Z]
To add to this, other parts of the port rely on Cortex M4-specific MPU capabilities. e.g:


#define portLAST_CONFIGURABLE_REGION            ( 7UL )


expects the MPU to support up to 8 regions, while the Cortex M7 supports up to 16. IF you are planning on expanding the port's awareness of those other 8 regions, please  remove the 2 pieces of code within vPortStoreTaskMPUSettings that wipe out any MPU region not allocated by FreeRTOS.

Or provide an alternative way to allocate regions taking full advantage of the MPU HAL's capabilities.

[rahmanih - 2020-06-01T10:39:17Z]
Hi @Palantir555,
Sorry for the delay,
Thanks indeed for the time spent to analyse and report the issue.
The issue is indeed confirmed, we are working to fix it.

keep you updated.

regards
Haithem.

[Palantir555 - 2020-06-01T11:56:17Z]
Thanks for the update Haithem. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help.

BTW, in case you haven't seen it, there's more info on that "after this fix" note here: https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/issues/39

[RKOUSTM - 2020-06-01T15:26:58Z]
ST Internal Reference: 87360

[RKOUSTM - 2021-06-23T14:29:32Z]
Hi @Palantir555,
 
I hope you are fine.

The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of FreeRTOS Kernel V10.3.1. In addition, this version of FreeRTOS is integrated on the STM32CubeH7 V1.9.0 published on GitHub.

Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,
