title: [H7] #10: In BSP driver lan8742: endless loop in case of timeout
content:
Issue #10: In BSP driver lan8742: endless loop in case of timeout

Title: In BSP driver lan8742: endless loop in case of timeout

Body:
Hello there

After this line:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/BSP/Components/lan8742/lan8742.c#L170

break is needed, like in line 165. Else, in case of PHY reset timeout, endless loop occurs.
As reported in the forum [here](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000BmleyqSQA/stm32h747idisco-with-lwip-not-working).

-- pa

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-12-16T09:46:02Z]
Hi Pavel,

Glad to hear from you again. On the forum, I could not find the firmware version you are using and whether your code is generated by Cube MX or not.

Actually, we provided a template to follow suggesting the different pieces of information that could be needed by our development teams for their investigations. Would you mind restating this issue according to this template? (essentially for the software- and hardware-related details and other aspects that may not have been precised on the forum).

Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

[pavel-a - 2019-12-16T14:05:42Z]
Ali, this was reported by other person on the forum and I've asked him to join here. 
Below please find description per your template. Best regards,  P.

**Describe the set-up**
 * The board: STM32H747I-DISCO
 * IDE or compiler: unknown. The project was built with the Cube library. 

**Describe the bug**
When LAN8742 Ethernet PHY reset operation times out for any reason, the LAN8742 driver loops instead of returning with proper status LAN8742_STATUS_RESET_TIMEOUT.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project

The program hangs.

2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)

lan8742.c

https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/BSP/Components/lan8742/lan8742.c#L158-L172

3. The use case that generates the problem

LAN8742 Ethernet PHY reset operation times out. Maybe because of wrong pinout, or electrical problem.

4. How we can reproduce the problem

paragraphs: Issue #10: In BSP driver lan8742: endless loop in case of timeout

Title: In BSP driver lan8742: endless loop in case of timeout Body:
Hello there

After this line:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/BSP/Components/lan8742/lan8742.c#L170

break is needed, like in line 165. Else, in case of PHY reset timeout, endless loop occurs.
As reported in the forum [here](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000BmleyqSQA/stm32h747idisco-with-lwip-not-working).

-- pa Comments:

Glad to hear from you again. On the forum, I could not find the firmware version you are using and whether your code is generated by Cube MX or not.

Actually, we provided a template to follow suggesting the different pieces of information that could be needed by our development teams for their investigations. Would you mind restating this issue according to this template? (essentially for the software- and hardware-related details and other aspects that may not have been precised on the forum).
Thank you for your comprehension. With regards,

[pavel-a - 2019-12-16T14:05:42Z]
Ali, this was reported by other person on the forum and I've asked him to join here.

**Describe the set-up**
 * The board: STM32H747I-DISCO
 * IDE or compiler: unknown. The project was built with the Cube library.

**Describe the bug**
When LAN8742 Ethernet PHY reset operation times out for any reason, the LAN8742 driver loops instead of returning with proper status LAN8742_STATUS_RESET_TIMEOUT.

**How To Reproduce**
1. Indicate the global behavior of your application project
2. The modules that you suspect to be the cause of the problem (Driver, BSP, MW ...)
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/BSP/Components/lan8742/lan8742.c#L158-L172
3. The use case that generates the problem
LAN8742 Ethernet PHY reset operation times out. Maybe because of wrong pinout, or electrical problem.
4. How we can reproduce the problem Do visual review of the code. --------------

I reported this issue in ST Community. I'm using OpenSTM32 IDE (Ac6) and example code from STM32H7 repository version V1.5.0. I used the application Projects/STM32H743I-EVAL/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_UDPTCP_Echo_Server_Netconn_RTOS as reference.

title: [H7] #9: FreeRTOS cannot enable timer
content:
Issue #9: FreeRTOS cannot enable timer

Title: FreeRTOS cannot enable timer

Body:
Hi ST,

I try to enable freeRTOS timer by set the configUSE_TIMERS = 1 in the FreeRTOSConfig.h, but it can't work. The same method in STM32L031, it works normal.
Did I miss something?

I used project is STM32CubeH7/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/FreeRTOS/FreeRTOS_SemaphoreFromISR/

Regards,
Wei

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-11-29T09:26:21Z]
Hi Cloud019,

Welcome to our GitHub organization. Please allow me to introduce @rahmanih who will answer your question.

With regards,

[rahmanih - 2019-11-29T09:52:06Z]
Hi,

> I try to enable freeRTOS timer by set the configUSE_TIMERS = 1 in the FreeRTOSConfig.h, but it can't work.

Could you please give more details about "can't work"?
- Are you using the CMSIS-OS API or the FreeRTOS native one?
- Are you able to create the timer?
- are getting a crash, hang ?

regards
Haithem.

[cloud019 - 2019-11-29T10:09:26Z]
> Hi,
> 
> > I try to enable freeRTOS timer by set the configUSE_TIMERS = 1 in the FreeRTOSConfig.h, but it can't work.
> 
> Could you please give more details about "can't work"?
> 
> * Are you using the CMSIS-OS API or the FreeRTOS native one?
FreeRTOS native
> * Are you able to create the timer?
Below is my code to create timer
	/* Create Timer */
	osTimerDef(LEDFlashTimer, driverLEDFlashTimerCallback);
	driverLEDFlashTimerHandle = osTimerCreate(osTimer(LEDFlashTimer), osTimerPeriodic, NULL);
	/* Start Timer */
	osTimerStart(driverLEDFlashTimerHandle, 200);
> * are getting a crash, hang ?
I set a breakpoint in the "driverLEDFlashTimerCallback" callback function, but the breakpoint does not active.
> 
> regards
> Haithem.

[rahmanih - 2019-12-02T14:52:27Z]
> I set a breakpoint in the "driverLEDFlashTimerCallback" callback function, but the breakpoint does not active.

is the **_driverLEDFlashTimerHandle_**   **NULL** ?, if it is the case then you should set a breakpoint in  the function "osTimerCreate()" and check why the timer is not correctly created.

[cloud019 - 2019-12-02T18:08:00Z]
> is the **_driverLEDFlashTimerHandle_** **NULL** ?, if it is the case then you should set a breakpoint in the function "osTimerCreate()" and check why the timer is not correctly created.

Yes, the handle is NULL.
But I trace the function

	TimerHandle_t xTimerCreate(	const char * const pcTimerName,			/*lint !e971 Unqualified char types are allowed for strings and single characters only. */
								const TickType_t xTimerPeriodInTicks,
								const UBaseType_t uxAutoReload,
								void * const pvTimerID,
								TimerCallbackFunction_t pxCallbackFunction )
	{
	Timer_t *pxNewTimer;

		pxNewTimer = ( Timer_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( Timer_t ) );

		if( pxNewTimer != NULL )
		{
			prvInitialiseNewTimer( pcTimerName, xTimerPeriodInTicks, uxAutoReload, pvTimerID, pxCallbackFunction, pxNewTimer );

			#if( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 1 )
			{
				/* Timers can be created statically or dynamically, so note this
				timer was created dynamically in case the timer is later
				deleted. */
				pxNewTimer->ucStaticallyAllocated = pdFALSE;
			}
			#endif /* configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION */
		}

		return pxNewTimer;
	}

I add a breakpoint at the "prvInitialiseNewTimer", it can stop on it, so the pxNewTimer  should not be NULL, but the **_driverLEDFlashTimerHandle_** is NULL, I don't know why.

[rahmanih - 2019-12-03T10:14:52Z]
>I add a breakpoint at the "prvInitialiseNewTimer", it can stop on it, so the pxNewTimer should not be NULL, but the driverLEDFlashTimerHandle is NULL, I don't know why.

I suspect that the pxNewTimer = ( Timer_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( Timer_t ) ); call returns '0' but the compiler is not considering the '0' as "NULL"

Could you check that the pxNewTimer is equal to 0 after the pvPortMalloc() call?
which IDE are you using?

regards
Haithem.

[rahmanih - 2019-12-06T10:01:37Z]
any updates?

[cloud019 - 2019-12-08T00:47:59Z]
Hi Haithem,

1) pxNewTimer is not NULL, it has an address value.
2) Return to main code, driverLEDFlashTimerHandle become 0

Regards,
Wei

[ALABSTM - 2020-04-14T09:35:12Z]
Hi @cloud019,

Any update regarding your question? Could you be able to enable the timer?

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-06-05T14:05:18Z]
Hi @cloud019 and @rahmanih,

Any new comments to move this discussion forward?

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-07-22T15:42:08Z]
Hi @cloud019 and @rahmanih,

Please allow me to close this thread as no activity has been registered since a couple of months. Thank you for your comprehension.

With regards,

title: [H7] #8: enabling unwanted global interrupts
content:
Issue #8: enabling unwanted global interrupts

Title: enabling unwanted global interrupts

Body:
In file stm32h7xx_hal_lptim.c function void LPTIM_Disable(LPTIM_HandleTypeDef *hlptim) use __disable_irq() and __enable_irq() without checking PRIMASK state.

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-11-21T12:05:22Z]
Hi Ashchepkov,

Thank you for your remark. It will be forwarded to our development team to check its relevance. You will be updated as soon as the provide us with feedback. Thank you once more.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-26T10:24:03Z]
ST Internal Reference: 76518

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-26T10:26:38Z]
Hi Ashchepkov,

Our development teams confirmed the issue you pointed out. A fix will be implemented and made available in the frame of a future release.

We cannot share a date for the moment, but hopefully it should be soon. Thank you for your patience and thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-04-15T08:19:27Z]
Hi @ashchepkov,

I hope you are doing well. I simply want to share a **sketch** of the fix proposed by development teams. Please note also that the same issue has been identified at the level of the LL_LPTIM_Disable() function.

Unfortunately, the fix is still not planned to be released in the next couple of weeks. We count on your patience and comprehension. Take care and stay safe.

With regards,

diff
void LPTIM_Disable(LPTIM_HandleTypeDef *lptim)
{
  ...
+  uint32_t prim;

+  /* Read PRIMASK register, check interrupts status before disabling them */
+  prim = __get_PRIMASK();

  /* Disable interrupts */
  __disable_irq();

  ...

+  /* In case interrupts were enabled, set them back */
+  if (prim == 0)
+  {
    __enable_irq();
+  }
}

[ashchepkov - 2020-04-16T13:18:43Z]
Hello!
I use the same method.

With best regards.

[ALABSTM - 2021-12-14T18:27:46Z]
Hi @ashchepkov,

I hope you are fine. The issue you reported has been fixed in the frame of version **v1.9.1** of the STM32CubeH7 published recently on GitHub. Thank you again for having reported.

With regards,

title: [H7] #7: Bug in ETH_DMARxDescListInit - reported in the forum
content:
Issue #7: Bug in ETH_DMARxDescListInit - reported in the forum

Title: Bug in ETH_DMARxDescListInit - reported in the forum

Body:
Please see description and fix here:
https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000Bea7dNSQQ/hellomay-be-it-is-wrong-ethernet-dma-tail-calculation

In STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c, line 2704

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-11-21T10:45:30Z]
Hi Pavel,

Thank you for having reported this point. It will be transmitted to our development teams. We will be back to you as soon as they provide us with a feedback. Thank you once more.

With regards,

[pavel-a - 2019-11-21T21:33:28Z]
Thank you Ali. Kudos for finding this issue go to [ashchepkov581 (on the forum)](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000Bea7dNSQQ/hellomay-be-it-is-wrong-ethernet-dma-tail-calculation).

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-28T09:24:25Z]
Hi Pavel,

Our development teams confirmed this issue. A fix will be implemented and published in the frame of a future release. Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-29T11:26:04Z]
ST Internal Reference: 76913

[pavel-a - 2020-02-06T20:59:40Z]
Still not fixed in v. 1.6.0
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/3d3ffbe6e15fc7e4d8a4d275e9fb3a334e31c8a3/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L2706

[ALABSTM - 2020-02-07T13:29:00Z]
Hi Pavel,

This bug is intended to be fixed in the frame of package v1.7.0 release (or a later one).

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2020-03-10T11:31:41Z]
Hi Pavel,

The fix of this issue will **not** be part of the v1.7.0 pacakge which will be published soon but part of a later one.

In the meanwhile, here is how the erroneous line will be updated:
diff
-WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))*sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef)))));
+WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) + ((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))));


We hope this helps and we thank you once more for your contribution.

With regards,

[pavel-a - 2020-03-22T10:27:19Z]
Ali, could they review it again, please? Per the docum (RM0433 page 2889) ETH_DMACRXDTPR indicated location of the last RX descriptor. I understand this as it contains address of the last descriptor, that is (base addr) + sizeof (one descr) * (number of desc. -1)
But in their variant: (base addr) + (number of desc. -1).
-- pa

[ALABSTM - 2020-03-30T11:12:06Z]
Hi Pavel,

I had a look to the ETH_DMADescTypeDef structure type and I think I understand better your point. Would you confirm please before I loop back with our development teams? Thanks.

Below is the definition of the ETH_DMADescTypeDef structre type:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.h#L57-L65

Now, sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) would return 24 (6 members x 4 bytes per member).

And here is the point:
* On the one hand, **removing** the sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) from the expression would not yield the correct result, as you are saying (resulting address **less than** the targeted one).
* On the other hand, **leaving** the sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) **as it is** would not yield the correct result neither (resulting address **greater than** the targeted one).

Rules of pointer arithmetic require rather the following:
* sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) / sizeof(uint32_t)

Correct implementation would then be:
diff
-WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))*sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef)))));
+WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) + (((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1)) * (uint32_t)(sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) / sizeof(uint32_t)))));


With regards,

[pavel-a - 2020-03-30T16:40:17Z]
Ali, sorry for delay. So now there are two "correct" variants. (I assume that red line with - is wrong and green line with + is proposed correction).

I'm confused.... where is pointer arithmetic in (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) ? If it were (uint32_t*)(heth->Init.RxDesc) then maybe... Lets' ask help from the ethernet experts on the forum.

Regards,
Pavel

[kele14x - 2020-03-31T06:46:44Z]
I think @ALABSTM is right. The correct calculation should be **(uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) + offset_bytes**

 I meet this issue and debug for half of day. I can't believe this bug is not fixed for this long time.

[ALABSTM - 2020-03-31T09:10:39Z]
Hi Pavel,

Pointer arithmetic arrives on the scene because structure member RxDesc is actually a **pointer** to the first DMA Rx descriptor.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.h#L378
Hence, as RxDesc points on a ETH_DMADescTypeDef structure, expression (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (uint32_t)1), for instance, will be automatically reevaluated @firstRxDesc + sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef).

To conclude, first fix suggestion is the correct one as confirmed by @kele14x.
diff
-WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))*sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef)))));
+WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) + ((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))));

The above expression (in green) will be automatically reevaluated @firstRxDesc + (ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1) * sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef).

I hope this makes things clearer. Please do not hesitate if you still have questions about the suggested fix.

With regards,

[pavel-a - 2020-03-31T10:53:50Z]
Ali, @kele14x, with all respect it seems there's some misunderstanding.
I put a little example here to explain: [rextester.com/LRKA74918](https://rextester.com/LRKA74918)
As soon as you cast a pointer to a number (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc), it becomes a number and pointer stuff no longer applies to it.

However, as @alister[ commented on the forum](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W000003vOM1SAM/stm32h7-ethernet-please-help-to-review-rx-descriptor-init-fix), it looks like there are multiple variants of setting this register, depending on the overall design of the driver. If the person who proposed the correction will provide also a thorough fix of the ETH driver, he/she likely knows better. If such fix won't be provided, this issue alone is not important.

Regards,
Pavel A.

[ALABSTM - 2020-03-31T12:51:03Z]
Hi Pavel,

I confirm there is a misunderstanding and a **typo** (from my side) too. In the expression I reported of the suggested fix, **parenthesis** are not correctly placed. With such an expression, pointer arithmetic applies no more. You are absolutely right about it.

The fix would rather be:
diff
-WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (((uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))*sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef)))));
+WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (uint32_t)(ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1))));


I hope you agree on what is written here. I do apologize for the confusion caused.

With regards,

[pavel-a - 2020-04-01T15:03:58Z]
Ali, no problem, thanks for checking. The bigger question now is, how about a deeper revision of the eth driver? When this could be expected?
- P.

[tushartp - 2020-04-13T16:21:23Z]
Hello Guys
I have question if we are changing the RX descriptor tail pointer address.
Do you think the TX descriptors should also be change from 

  /* Set Transmit Descriptor Tail pointer */
(Line 2663)  WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACTDTPR, (uint32_t) heth->Init.TxDesc);
to 
  /* Set Transmit Descriptor Tail pointer */
(Line 2663)  WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACTDTPR, ((uint32_t)(heth->Init.TxDesc + (uint32_t)(ETH_TX_DESC_CNT - 1))));


Please let me know if there is any difference in TX and RX descriptors. I am not expert on this Ethernet HAL handling on stm32h7.

Thanks
Tushar

[pavel-a - 2020-04-14T00:50:46Z]
@tushartp You're welcome to ask this in the [forum](https://community.st.com/s/topic/0TO0X000000BSqSWAW/stm32-mcus).

[thomask77 - 2020-05-03T18:20:08Z]
To make the code's intention clearer, I would suggest

c
WRITE_REG(heth->Instance->DMACRDTPR, (uint32_t)(&heth->Init.RxDesc[ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1]));


Much clearer, and doesn't require knowledge of pointer arithmetic rules.
paragraphs: Issue #7: Bug in ETH_DMARxDescListInit - reported in the forum
Title: Bug in ETH_DMARxDescListInit - reported in the forum

Please see description and fix here:

Comments:

Thank you for having reported this point. It will be transmitted to our development teams. We will be back to you as soon as they provide us with a feedback. Thank you once more.

Thank you Ali. Kudos for finding this issue go to [ashchepkov581 (on the forum)](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X0000Bea7dNSQQ/hellomay-be-it-is-wrong-ethernet-dma-tail-calculation).

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-28T09:24:25Z]
Hi Pavel,
Our development teams confirmed this issue. A fix will be implemented and published in the frame of a future release. Thank you again for your contribution.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2019-11-29T11:26:04Z]
ST Internal Reference: 76913

Still not fixed in v. 1.6.0
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/3d3ffbe6e15fc7e4d8a4d275e9fb3a334e31c8a3/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L2706

[ALABSTM - 2020-02-07T13:29:00Z]
Hi Pavel,
This bug is intended to be fixed in the frame of package v1.7.0 release (or a later one).

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2020-03-10T11:31:41Z]
Hi Pavel,
The fix of this issue will **not** be part of the v1.7.0 pacakge which will be published soon but part of a later one.
We hope this helps and we thank you once more for your contribution.

[pavel-a - 2020-03-22T10:27:19Z]
Ali, could they review it again, please? Per the docum (RM0433 page 2889) ETH_DMACRXDTPR indicated location of the last RX descriptor. I understand this as it contains address of the last descriptor, that is (base addr) + sizeof (one descr) * (number of desc. -1)
But in their variant: (base addr) + (number of desc. -1).
-- pa

[ALABSTM - 2020-03-30T11:12:06Z]
Hi Pavel,
I had a look to the ETH_DMADescTypeDef structure type and I think I understand better your point. Would you confirm please before I loop back with our development teams? Thanks.

Below is the definition of the ETH_DMADescTypeDef structre type:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/79196b09acfb720589f58e93ccf956401b18a191/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.h#L57-L65
Now, sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) would return 24 (6 members x 4 bytes per member).

And here is the point:
* On the one hand, **removing** the sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) from the expression would not yield the correct result, as you are saying (resulting address **less than** the targeted one).
* On the other hand, **leaving** the sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) **as it is** would not yield the correct result neither (resulting address **greater than** the targeted one).

Rules of pointer arithmetic require rather the following:
* sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef) / sizeof(uint32_t)

[pavel-a - 2020-03-30T16:40:17Z]
Ali, sorry for delay. So now there are two "correct" variants. (I assume that red line with - is wrong and green line with + is proposed correction).
I'm confused.... where is pointer arithmetic in (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc) ? If it were (uint32_t*)(heth->Init.RxDesc) then maybe... Lets' ask help from the ethernet experts on the forum.

I meet this issue and debug for half of day. I can't believe this bug is not fixed for this long time.

Hence, as RxDesc points on a ETH_DMADescTypeDef structure, expression (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc + (uint32_t)1), for instance, will be automatically reevaluated @firstRxDesc + sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef).
The above expression (in green) will be automatically reevaluated @firstRxDesc + (ETH_RX_DESC_CNT - 1) * sizeof(ETH_DMADescTypeDef).
I hope this makes things clearer. Please do not hesitate if you still have questions about the suggested fix.

[pavel-a - 2020-03-31T10:53:50Z]
Ali, @kele14x, with all respect it seems there's some misunderstanding.
I put a little example here to explain: [rextester.com/LRKA74918](https://rextester.com/LRKA74918)
As soon as you cast a pointer to a number (uint32_t)(heth->Init.RxDesc), it becomes a number and pointer stuff no longer applies to it.

However, as @alister[ commented on the forum](https://community.st.com/s/question/0D53W000003vOM1SAM/stm32h7-ethernet-please-help-to-review-rx-descriptor-init-fix), it looks like there are multiple variants of setting this register, depending on the overall design of the driver. If the person who proposed the correction will provide also a thorough fix of the ETH driver, he/she likely knows better. If such fix won't be provided, this issue alone is not important.

Regards,
Pavel A. [ALABSTM - 2020-03-31T12:51:03Z]
Hi Pavel,

I confirm there is a misunderstanding and a **typo** (from my side) too. In the expression I reported of the suggested fix, **parenthesis** are not correctly placed. With such an expression, pointer arithmetic applies no more. You are absolutely right about it.
I hope you agree on what is written here. I do apologize for the confusion caused.

[pavel-a - 2020-04-01T15:03:58Z]

Do you think the TX descriptors should also be change from

Title: Broken I2S extension module when using the 1.5.0 version.

Body:

These are the errors that occur when simply including the stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.h file after defining SPI_I2S_FULLDUPLEX_SUPPORT:

Here is a simplified list of the errors after removing duplicates and paths:

'HAL_I2S_STATE_BUSY_TX_RX' undeclared

Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:  CLEAR_BIT(I2SxEXT(hi2s->Instance)->CR2, (SPI_CR2_RXDMAEN | SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN));


rgrep SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:    SET_BIT(hi2s->Instance->CR2, SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN);
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:    SET_BIT(I2SxEXT(hi2s->Instance)->CR2, SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN);
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:        CLEAR_BIT(hi2s->Instance->CR2, SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN);
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:        CLEAR_BIT(I2SxEXT(hi2s->Instance)->CR2, SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN);
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:  CLEAR_BIT(hi2s->Instance->CR2, (SPI_CR2_RXDMAEN | SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN));
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:  CLEAR_BIT(I2SxEXT(hi2s->Instance)->CR2, (SPI_CR2_RXDMAEN | SPI_CR2_TXDMAEN));



As you can see there are indeed no definitions of these identifiers.
There is also the issue of 'SPI_TypeDef' has no member named 'DR'. I assume this is a leftover
from some other driver version. There is no such member in the SPI_TypeDef structure.
We are working with the STM32H743 line so the relevant header is Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h743xx.h.

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-12T11:49:04Z]

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-13T08:23:49Z]
Hi Liarokapisv,

Here is what the reply of our development teams:
- The I2S full duplex was mentioned as a missed feature in the release notes of the v1.4.0 (***Known Limitations*** section).
- The I2S full duplex feature is not available in the v1.5.0 neither. The files stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c/.h are protected with a compilation define #if defined (SPI_I2S_FULLDUPLEX_SUPPORT).
- This SPI_I2S_FULLDUPLEX_SUPPORT define is **no set by default**. This is to avoid compiling the full duplex feature implemented in these files but not yet ready to be used.

You have probably activated it (and you were right as we provided this feature within the driver). Hence,  you got these errors. We do apologize for this "ambiguity".

This issue will be fixed in two steps:
1. In the next release (v1.6.0), we will remove the stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c/.h from the package.
2. Once the full duplex correctly implemented, we will put these files back into the package to make this feature available to use.

Thank you once more for having tackled this point.

With regards,

Title: Broken I2S extension module when using the 1.5.0 version.

Body:

These are the errors that occur when simply including the stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.h file after defining SPI_I2S_FULLDUPLEX_SUPPORT:

Here is a simplified list of the errors after removing duplicates and paths:

'HAL_I2S_STATE_BUSY_TX_RX' undeclared
'SPI_TypeDef' has no member named 'DR'
'I2S2ext_BASE' undeclared

Here are the results of running relevant rgreps in the repository's root:

rgrep I2S2ext_BASE
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.h:#define I2SxEXT(__INSTANCE__) ((__INSTANCE__) == (SPI2)? (SPI_TypeDef *)(I2S2ext_BASE): (SPI_TypeDef *)(I2S3ext_BASE))

rgrep SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.h:#define __HAL_I2SEXT_ENABLE(__HANDLE__) (I2SxEXT((__HANDLE__)->Instance)->I2SCFGR |= SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE)
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Inc/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.h:#define __HAL_I2SEXT_DISABLE(__HANDLE__) (I2SxEXT((__HANDLE__)->Instance)->I2SCFGR &= ~SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE)
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:    if ((hi2s->Instance->I2SCFGR & SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE) != SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE)
Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_i2s_ex.c:    if ((hi2s->Instance->I2SCFGR & SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE) != SPI_I2SCFGR_I2SE)

from some other driver version. There is no such member in the SPI_TypeDef structure.
We are working with the STM32H743 line so the relevant header is Drivers/CMSIS/Device/ST/STM32H7xx/Include/stm32h743xx.h.

title: [H7] #5: TCP/IP sample for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo is unstable
content:
Issue #5: TCP/IP sample for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo is unstable

Title: TCP/IP sample for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo is unstable

Body:
The TCP/IP sample code for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo doesn't work, as intended: (STM32CubeH7 v. 1.5) 
It's best described here:
https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X00009ZExEkSAL/stm32h743-nucleo-ethernet-stops-working

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-09-30T14:26:06Z]
Hi Illishar,

The issue you pointed out has been communicated to our development teams. It will be investigated soon. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-08T08:29:23Z]
Hi Illishar,

Would you mind providing us with a simple test case other than the one you described on ST Community allowing to reproduce the issue?

Thank you,

[illishar - 2019-10-08T09:20:39Z]
Actually, I have a junior developer working on the issue. So far, he's tracked it down to a missing SYN message in Wireshark, when using multiple TCP connections, to eg. the web-server. It's rather difficult to reproduce. But perhaps we can post the wireshark logs, as a start.

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-08T10:44:02Z]
Hi Illishar,

Could you please confirm you are using the latest firmware version (STM32CubeH7 v1.5.0) and that the issue is systematically reproducible on your side?

Thank you,

[Sprutten - 2019-10-09T08:37:19Z]
Hi ALABSTM

I am the junior developer mentioned in this tread. Just to clarify, we are using the STM32H743ZI2_Nucleo boards and not STM32H743ZI_Nucleo. The latest firmware version (STM32CubeH7 v1.5.0) is installed. I am using this example for reference STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.5.0\Projects\NUCLEO-H743ZI\Applications\LwIP\LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS 

What I did to reproduce the issue:
Run the program and make a request to the server, then wait about 5-10 seconds and make a new request. Then the browser is loading forever and can’t get the HTML from the server. The problem is consistent after waiting 5-10 seconds. The example works fine if you make a request every 1-2 seconds.

What I have tested:
•	Use multiple STM32H743ZI2_Nucleo boards 
•	When testing the sample code on different PC setups, the issue is inconsistent. (More than one PC have this issue)
•	Use different browsers. Browsers differs in stability, but Chrome is more unstable.
•	Tried with static and DHCP option, issue occurs on both
•	Use CycloneTCP example. I can’t reproduce the error in this TCP stack. 
•	Use different network adapters and cables (USB, PCI, switch/no switch and built-in)

I have some Wireshark logs to confirm the issue. I have added some comments on the logs from the ST example, see below. 

In line 7 we can see the GET / HTTP/1.1 request isn’t answered by the server. I did a new request at line 12 about 5 seconds after the first request and that got answered. Then in line 69 I did a new request after about 10 seconds, to make sure it was reproduceable and that didn’t get answered by the server. 

I have attached the Wireshark logs from the ST example and CycloneTCP example. (CycloneTCP is included for reference)
[Wireshark logs.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/3706304/Wireshark.logs.zip)

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-10T08:50:26Z]
Hi Sprutten,

Thank you for all these details. We will communicate them to our development team in charge of investigating this issue. We may eventually ask you for other details if needed. Thank you in advance for your patience and help.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-18T09:53:28Z]
Hi Sprutten,

We tried to reproduce the issue you described using the same example and the same board as you. Unfortunately we could not. Investigation is ongoing.

Just to be sure we understood the sequence you described, by "doing a new request", do you mean reloading the Web page on the browser by pressing F5?

Thank you for your answer and in case something new has been spotted from your side, that could shed more light about this behavior, do not hesitate to inform us. Thank you once more.

With regards,

[Sprutten - 2019-10-18T10:04:23Z]
Hi ALISTM
It was when making request to the board. The same result occur if I press f5 or if I press the homepage in the html.

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-18T10:13:32Z]
Hi Sprutten,

May I ask for more precision about how you make requests to the board? Thank you for your answer and for your patience. The point is that maybe our development team is missing some detail that prevents them from reproducing the issue.

With regards,

[Sprutten - 2019-10-18T10:42:12Z]
Hi ALISTM
I make request to the board when loading the HTML page in a browser. 
The when the code is uploaded to the board, i open a browser and type the IP address the board has. I tried to make the same HTTP request with a C# script, it worked perfect and I could't reproduce the error. It only occurs when making requests in a browser.

I have discovered that if i make a delay(5ms) or a printf at this function in the hal ethernet driver it works. but thats not feasible since the the system becomes slow.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L951

It also works if i make the same delay at this line.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS/Src/ethernetif.c#L282

I think the error occurs when modifying the transmit descriptor (TDES3 normal descriptor (Read format)). There might be some problem with the cache, but not sure.

Hope it helps.

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-06T09:44:06Z]
Hi Sprutten,

We tried once more to reproduce the issue you have described, with no success. We used Google Chrome Version 76.0.3809.100 (Official Build) (32-bit) as browser.

We sent requests to the MCU by pressing F5, by clicking on the "Reload this page" button of the browser, and by clicking on the "Home page" link on the Web page. We did this each 7 seconds, with no success, however.

A suggestion from our development teams is that you increase the timeout value to fit more your application needs:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS/Src/ethernetif.c#L45

Please let me know whether it solves the problem.

With regards,

[Sprutten - 2019-11-06T10:06:25Z]
Hi ALISTM
Increasing the DMA timeout doesn't fix the issue. My guess to a solution, is to add a synchronization barrier inside the HAL driver after filling in the TxDesc and before restarting the DMA engine from the suspended state. Think there is some sort of sync issues. I have tried to add a DSB in the transmit frame without any luck. https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L951
I think you have to test it on multiple PC's, since the issue is occurring on specific PC's and can't be replicated by PC in which the browser is working.

Please inform me if you need anything from me.

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-06T11:25:27Z]
Hi Sprutten,

Would you mind sending the binary (or hex) file you are using to flash your MCU? Would you also indicate the browser's name and version that yields the failure when used? Does it particularly happen when using "Chrome", or "IE" too? Have you used the "Microsoft Edge"?

Thank you,

[Sprutten - 2019-11-06T11:29:04Z]
Sure, here it is. 

[STM32H743ZI_Nucleo(bin).zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/3814170/STM32H743ZI_Nucleo.bin.zip)

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-06T16:05:33Z]
Hi Sprutten,

We will also need the *.map file corresponding to the binary file you have sent.

Thank you,

[Sprutten - 2019-11-06T17:03:43Z]
Sure, here it is. I also included other debug files.
[Debug.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/3815791/Debug.zip)
With regards

[ALABSTM - 2019-11-08T10:20:46Z]
Hi Sprutten,

Thank you for the artifacts you have sent. Unfortunately, they were of no great help, since you are using a fixed IP address, our development teams say.
We could not perform the test as the network addresses are not the same. It would have been possible if the LWIP_DHCP option were activated.

There is one last thing we could do, however. Would you, please, send us your application code? Just the application code please. We will use it to perform the test again.

With regards,

[Sprutten - 2019-11-08T10:36:58Z]
Ohh, sorry about the DHCP option. But it's literally the stock firmware code example i am using (STM32CubeH7 v1.5.0). The only thing i have changed is the DHCP option.
But here it is.

paragraphs: Issue #5: TCP/IP sample for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo is unstable
Title: TCP/IP sample for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo is unstable

Body:
The TCP/IP sample code for STM32H743ZI_Nucleo doesn't work, as intended: (STM32CubeH7 v. 1.5) 
It's best described here:
https://community.st.com/s/question/0D50X00009ZExEkSAL/stm32h743-nucleo-ethernet-stops-working

Comments:
[ALABSTM - 2019-09-30T14:26:06Z]
Hi Illishar,
The issue you pointed out has been communicated to our development teams. It will be investigated soon. We will be back to you as soon as we get an answer.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2019-10-08T08:29:23Z]
Hi Illishar,
Would you mind providing us with a simple test case other than the one you described on ST Community allowing to reproduce the issue?

[illishar - 2019-10-08T09:20:39Z]
Actually, I have a junior developer working on the issue. So far, he's tracked it down to a missing SYN message in Wireshark, when using multiple TCP connections, to eg. the web-server. It's rather difficult to reproduce. But perhaps we can post the wireshark logs, as a start.

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-08T10:44:02Z]
Hi Illishar,
Could you please confirm you are using the latest firmware version (STM32CubeH7 v1.5.0) and that the issue is systematically reproducible on your side?

Thank you, [Sprutten - 2019-10-09T08:37:19Z]
Hi ALABSTM

I am the junior developer mentioned in this tread. Just to clarify, we are using the STM32H743ZI2_Nucleo boards and not STM32H743ZI_Nucleo. The latest firmware version (STM32CubeH7 v1.5.0) is installed. I am using this example for reference STM32Cube_FW_H7_V1.5.0\Projects\NUCLEO-H743ZI\Applications\LwIP\LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS

What I did to reproduce the issue:
Run the program and make a request to the server, then wait about 5-10 seconds and make a new request. Then the browser is loading forever and can’t get the HTML from the server. The problem is consistent after waiting 5-10 seconds. The example works fine if you make a request every 1-2 seconds.

What I have tested:
•	Use multiple STM32H743ZI2_Nucleo boards 
•	When testing the sample code on different PC setups, the issue is inconsistent. (More than one PC have this issue)
•	Use different browsers. Browsers differs in stability, but Chrome is more unstable.
•	Tried with static and DHCP option, issue occurs on both
•	Use CycloneTCP example. I can’t reproduce the error in this TCP stack. 
•	Use different network adapters and cables (USB, PCI, switch/no switch and built-in)
I have some Wireshark logs to confirm the issue. I have added some comments on the logs from the ST example, see below.

In line 7 we can see the GET / HTTP/1.1 request isn’t answered by the server. I did a new request at line 12 about 5 seconds after the first request and that got answered. Then in line 69 I did a new request after about 10 seconds, to make sure it was reproduceable and that didn’t get answered by the server.

I have attached the Wireshark logs from the ST example and CycloneTCP example. (CycloneTCP is included for reference)
[Wireshark logs.zip](https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/files/3706304/Wireshark.logs.zip)

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-10T08:50:26Z]
Hi Sprutten,

Thank you for all these details. We will communicate them to our development team in charge of investigating this issue. We may eventually ask you for other details if needed. Thank you in advance for your patience and help.

With regards, [ALABSTM - 2019-10-18T09:53:28Z]
Hi Sprutten,
We tried to reproduce the issue you described using the same example and the same board as you. Unfortunately we could not. Investigation is ongoing.
Just to be sure we understood the sequence you described, by "doing a new request", do you mean reloading the Web page on the browser by pressing F5?
Thank you for your answer and in case something new has been spotted from your side, that could shed more light about this behavior, do not hesitate to inform us. Thank you once more.

[Sprutten - 2019-10-18T10:04:23Z]
Hi ALISTM
It was when making request to the board. The same result occur if I press f5 or if I press the homepage in the html.

[ALABSTM - 2019-10-18T10:13:32Z]
Hi Sprutten,

May I ask for more precision about how you make requests to the board? Thank you for your answer and for your patience. The point is that maybe our development team is missing some detail that prevents them from reproducing the issue.

[Sprutten - 2019-10-18T10:42:12Z]
Hi ALISTM
I make request to the board when loading the HTML page in a browser. 
The when the code is uploaded to the board, i open a browser and type the IP address the board has. I tried to make the same HTTP request with a C# script, it worked perfect and I could't reproduce the error. It only occurs when making requests in a browser.

I have discovered that if i make a delay(5ms) or a printf at this function in the hal ethernet driver it works. but thats not feasible since the the system becomes slow.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L951

It also works if i make the same delay at this line.
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS/Src/ethernetif.c#L282
I think the error occurs when modifying the transmit descriptor (TDES3 normal descriptor (Read format)). There might be some problem with the cache, but not sure.

Hope it helps. [ALABSTM - 2019-11-06T09:44:06Z]
Hi Sprutten,
We tried once more to reproduce the issue you have described, with no success. We used Google Chrome Version 76.0.3809.100 (Official Build) (32-bit) as browser.

We sent requests to the MCU by pressing F5, by clicking on the "Reload this page" button of the browser, and by clicking on the "Home page" link on the Web page. We did this each 7 seconds, with no success, however.

A suggestion from our development teams is that you increase the timeout value to fit more your application needs:
https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Projects/NUCLEO-H743ZI/Applications/LwIP/LwIP_HTTP_Server_Netconn_RTOS/Src/ethernetif.c#L45
Please let me know whether it solves the problem. With regards,

[Sprutten - 2019-11-06T10:06:25Z]
Hi ALISTM
Increasing the DMA timeout doesn't fix the issue. My guess to a solution, is to add a synchronization barrier inside the HAL driver after filling in the TxDesc and before restarting the DMA engine from the suspended state. Think there is some sort of sync issues. I have tried to add a DSB in the transmit frame without any luck. https://github.com/STMicroelectronics/STM32CubeH7/blob/e261d820b398846a94f22f4aeb32d86c29546efb/Drivers/STM32H7xx_HAL_Driver/Src/stm32h7xx_hal_eth.c#L951
I think you have to test it on multiple PC's, since the issue is occurring on specific PC's and can't be replicated by PC in which the browser is working.
Please inform me if you need anything from me.

Would you mind sending the binary (or hex) file you are using to flash your MCU? Would you also indicate the browser's name and version that yields the failure when used? Does it particularly happen when using "Chrome", or "IE" too? Have you used the "Microsoft Edge"?

title: [H7] #3: SDMMC_BASE is undefined
content:
Issue #3: SDMMC_BASE is undefined

Title: SDMMC_BASE is undefined

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * arm-none-eabi-gcc
 * #include <stm32h7xx.h>

**Describe the bug**

This line is wrong, since there is no SDMMC peripheral on the device.
c
#define SDMMC               ((SDMMC_TypeDef *) SDMMC_BASE)


The correct peripherals are called SDMMC1 and SDMMC2 and they are correctly defined.

**How To Reproduce**

Try to call SDMMC in your code, which will fail since SDMMC_BASE is undefined.

**Additional context**

diff
-#define SDMMC               ((SDMMC_TypeDef *) SDMMC_BASE)

[Full patch](https://github.com/modm-io/cmsis-header-stm32/blob/master/patches/h7.patch)

Also note that there is a use of a *signed* 32-bit integer in the ETH_TypeDef struct (around line 762):
diff
   uint32_t      RESERVED15[14];
   __IO uint32_t MMCTSCGPR;
   __IO uint32_t MMCTMCGPR;
-  int32_t       RESERVED16[5];
+  uint32_t      RESERVED16[5];
   __IO uint32_t MMCTPCGR;
   uint32_t      RESERVED17[10];
   __IO uint32_t MMCRCRCEPR;

Comments:
[CCASTM - 2019-06-05T09:26:27Z]
Hello Niklas,
Thank you for your finding. This has been communicated to the development team and we will come back to you to confirm or explain this point.
Regards
Christophe

[ASELSTM - 2019-06-18T15:39:59Z]
ST Internal Reference: 68309

[ASELSTM - 2019-06-18T16:26:49Z]
Hi Niklas,

Thank you once again for your contribution. The problem was identified and confirmed, the correction will be available in our next release.

With regards

[CCASTM - 2019-06-18T17:07:22Z]
To be more accurate, the following line will be removed (as not relevant)
#define SDMMC               ((SDMMC_TypeDef *) SDMMC_BASE)

But there is a second question in this Issue regarding ETH_TypeDef.
**Niklas**, can you create a new Issue, as it is more convenient to separate the corrections (better to track the commits)

Thank you
Christophe

[salkinium - 2019-06-18T17:14:38Z]
You can also create issues 😛  \*delegates back\*

title: [H7] #2: HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef is undefined
content:
Issue #2: HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef is undefined

Title: HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef is undefined

Body:
**Describe the set-up**
 * arm-none-eabi-gcc
 * #include <stm32h7xx.h>

**Describe the bug**

HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef is undefined. After cross referencing this with the RM you probably meant to use HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef.

**How To Reproduce**

Trying to access HRTIM1_TIMA in any code will fail, since HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef is undefined:

c
#include <stm32h7xx.h>
HRTIM1_TIMA->TIMxCR = 0;


**Additional context**

diff
-#define HRTIM1_TIMA         ((HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMA_BASE)
-#define HRTIM1_TIMB         ((HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMB_BASE)
-#define HRTIM1_TIMC         ((HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMC_BASE)
-#define HRTIM1_TIMD         ((HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMD_BASE)
-#define HRTIM1_TIME         ((HRTIM_TIM_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIME_BASE)
+#define HRTIM1_TIMA         ((HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMA_BASE)
+#define HRTIM1_TIMB         ((HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMB_BASE)
+#define HRTIM1_TIMC         ((HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMC_BASE)
+#define HRTIM1_TIMD         ((HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIMD_BASE)
+#define HRTIM1_TIME         ((HRTIM_Timerx_TypeDef *) HRTIM1_TIME_BASE)


[Complete patch](https://github.com/modm-io/cmsis-header-stm32/blob/master/patches/h7.patch)

Comments:
[CCASTM - 2019-06-05T09:13:55Z]
Hello Niklas,
Thank you for your finding. This has been communicated to the development team and we will come back to you (a very first analysis confirms the problem)
Regards
Christophe

[ALABSTM - 2019-06-19T09:12:24Z]
Hi Niklas,

The issue you pointed out has been confirmed. A fix will be available in a future release. This closes the issue.

With regards,

[ALABSTM - 2019-06-19T09:12:33Z]
ST Internal Reference: 67769
